vocabulary.definition: v. 并排停放（汽车）( double-park的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: Double-parked, killed the engine, toggled the emergency blinkers, rolled the windows up until there was only a sliver of space for air to slip through.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 并排停车，熄火，打开紧急信号灯，摇起车窗，直到只剩下一小片空间让空气通过

vocabulary.definition: n. 护目镜, 马眼罩
vocabulary.context: Started the engine, cut the emergency blinkers, then honked the horn twice to say goodbye, before pulling off.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 启动发动机，关闭紧急信号灯，然后按两声喇叭表示再见，然后停车

vocabulary.definition: adj. 引起幻觉的, 荧光的；n. 迷幻剂
vocabulary.context: Ugly prewar bathroom tiles, like standing on a psychedelic chessboard.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 丑陋的战前浴室瓷砖，就像站在迷幻的棋盘上

vocabulary.definition: adj. 家庭似的, 舒适的, 自在的
vocabulary.context: "And I said, 'No doubt, homie.'"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我说，'毫无疑问，伙计。'"

vocabulary.definition: n. 胸骨, 胸片, 胸板；[医] 胸骨; 腹板(昆虫)
vocabulary.context: But he loves me." Shay pushed her finger into her own sternum, like pushing a button to turn her heart on.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但他爱我。"谢伊将手指插入自己的胸骨，就像按下按钮来打开她的心脏一样

vocabulary.definition: n. <俚>愚蠢的错误，轻伤( boo-boo的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: The girl who healed your boo-boos when we were kids."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我们还是孩子的时候，那个女孩治愈了你的烦恼。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 大错, 疏忽
vocabulary.context: Then Dante would slowly peel it off because Shay always needed to see it, white where brown used to be, a blood-speckled boo-boo waiting to be blown.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后但丁会慢慢地把它剥下来，因为谢伊总是需要看到它，白色的地方曾经是棕色的，一个沾满血迹的嘘声等待着被吹走

vocabulary.definition: n. 绳针, 套索钉；v.t. 拴牢；[计] 切换
vocabulary.context: Double-parked, killed the engine, toggled the emergency blinkers, rolled the windows up until there was only a sliver of space for air to slip through.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 并排停车，熄火，打开紧急信号灯，摇起车窗，直到只剩下一小片空间让空气通过

vocabulary.definition: n. 无电梯的大楼；adj. 无电梯大楼的
vocabulary.context: This small, two-bedroom, third-floor walk-up with good sunlight and hardwood floors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这间三楼的小型两卧室无电梯公寓拥有良好的阳光和硬木地板

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 将饵食轻放水面上钓鱼, 轻点水面；n. 弹跳, 掠跳；[计] 数据存取协议, 数据分析程序
vocabulary.context: "You dapped me, and we both said 'I love you,' like usual, except you wouldn't let go.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "你拍拍我，我们都像往常一样说'我爱你'，只是你不肯放手

vocabulary.definition: n. 联合；结合；工会化
vocabulary.context: While Gatsby and Kodak certainly reflected the lavish and extreme versions of summer, the rise of unionization combined with overcrowded cities led urban planners to begin developing leisure activities for the working class.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然盖茨比和柯达确实反映了夏季的奢华和极端版本，但工会的兴起加上城市的过度拥挤导致城市规划者开始为工人阶级开发休闲活动

vocabulary.definition: adj. 工资低的; 低收入的
vocabulary.context: Although the middle classes of the 20th century enjoyed the previously aristocratic privilege of escaping to a refreshing summer haven, their vacations created work, often low-paid and seasonal, for others.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管20世纪的中产阶级享有以前贵族般的特权，可以逃到一个清爽的避暑天堂，但他们的假期却为其他人创造了工作，通常是低薪和季节性的工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 引力, 重力, 沉下；[化] 引力
vocabulary.context: In August of 1891, social scientist Edward Hungerford investigated the cultural gravitation toward recreation and leisure, seeking answers as to why Americans felt a need to take time off work for summer rest: "The magnitude of such a movement as this justifies the assumption that the social influence exerted by it are worthy of serious consideration."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1891年8月，社会科学家爱德华·亨格福德（Edward Hungerford）调查了人们对娱乐和休闲的文化吸引力，寻找美国人为何需要在夏季休假的原因："这种运动的规模如此之大，证明了这样的假设：它所产生的社会影响值得认真考虑。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 战前的, 南北战争前的
vocabulary.context: The first vestiges of summer began in the decades before the American Civil War when only the wealthiest class of antebellum elites vacationed in resort towns like Newport, Rhode Island and Saratoga, New York.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 夏季的最初痕迹始于美国内战前的几十年，当时只有最富有的战前精英阶层才会在罗德岛州纽波特和纽约州萨拉托加等度假小镇度假

vocabulary.definition: n. 民主化；[法] 民主化
vocabulary.context: The summer we know and love - the one that features vacations to the beach, 4th of July weekends that turn into 4th of July weeks, and the all but recent invention of casual Fridays or even summer Fridays - is a democratization of leisure that grew out of capitalist forces in the 20th century.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们所熟悉和喜爱的夏天--以海滩度假、7 月 4 日的周末变成 7 月 4 日的一周，以及几乎最近发明的休闲星期五甚至夏季星期五--是 20 世纪资本主义力量中产生的休闲民主化

vocabulary.definition: n. 松弛, 放松, 减轻, 缓和, 休息, 休养；[化] 松弛; 驰豫
vocabulary.context: Summer was no longer just a hot time of year for Americans to endure, but instead an opportunity for rest, relaxation, and recreation that they could enjoy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 夏季不再只是美国人忍受的炎热季节，而是他们可以享受休息、放松和娱乐的机会

vocabulary.definition: n. 期刊；adj. 定期出版的, (有关)期刊的, 间歇(性的), 周期的, 定期的
vocabulary.context: ...culture in conjunction with a rising tourist industry prompted many to perceive summer as "a season of rest - rest from all care." Periodical articles and tourist guides promoted an enticing escape to summer resorts such as Martha's Vineyard, "a maritime Eden where one may shut out the whole world and be content."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...文化与蓬勃发展的旅游业相结合，促使许多人将夏季视为"休息的季节 - 摆脱一切忧虑的休息"。期刊文章和旅游指南宣传玛莎葡萄园岛等避暑胜地是一种诱人的度假方式，"一个海上伊甸园，人们可以在这里隔绝整个世界并感到满足。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 颓废
vocabulary.context: Scott Fitzgerald as the perfect setting to embellish the mysterious decadence of the parties in The Great Gatsby .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯科特·菲茨杰拉德（Scott Fitzgerald）作为点缀《了不起的盖茨比》中各方神秘颓废的完美背景

vocabulary.definition: n. 连接词, 联合, 结合；[计] 合取
vocabulary.context: ...a breathless hurry...If any folk in the wide world have need of a summer refreshment, it is we." Nineteenth century print culture in conjunction with a rising tourist industry prompted many to perceive summer as "a season of rest - rest from all care." Periodical articles and tourist guides promoted an enticing escape to summer resorts such as...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...气喘吁吁的匆忙...如果世界上有任何一个人需要夏季茶点，那就是我们。"十九世纪的印刷文化与新兴的旅游业相结合，促使许多人将夏天视为"休息的季节 - 摆脱所有忧虑的休息。"期刊文章和旅游指南提倡前往避暑胜地，例如......

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不愉快地, 不合口味地
vocabulary.context: What you want?" he demanded disagreeably when I had pinched and slapped him awake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你想要什么？"当我掐醒他并把他打醒时，他不高兴地问道

vocabulary.definition: adj. 象要倒的, 摇晃的, 放荡的
vocabulary.context: Miss Lottie's house was the most ramshackle of all our ramshackle homes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 洛蒂小姐的房子是我们所有摇摇欲坠的房子中最摇摇欲坠的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 悲喜剧的, 又悲又喜的
vocabulary.context: I think now that we must have made a tragicomic spectacle, five or six kids of different ages, each of us clad in only one garment - the girls in faded dresses that were too long or too short, the boys in patchy pants, their sweaty brown chests gleaming in the hot sun.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我想现在我们一定制造了一场悲喜剧的奇观，五六个不同年龄的孩子，我们每个人只穿一件衣服--女孩穿着太长或太短的褪色衣服，男孩穿着补丁裤，汗湿的棕色胸膛在烈日下闪闪发光

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未受指导的, 无姓名和地址的
vocabulary.context: Poverty was the cage in which we all were trapped, and our hatred of it was still the vague, undirected restlessness of the zoo-bred flamingo who knows that nature created him to fly free.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 贫穷是我们所有人都被困在其中的牢笼，而我们对它的仇恨仍然是动物园饲养的火烈鸟那种模糊的、无方向的不安，它知道大自然创造了他自由飞翔

vocabulary.definition: n. 棚户区
vocabulary.context: And so, when I think of that time and that place, I remember only the dry September of the dirt roads and grassless yards of the shantytown where I lived.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以，当我想起那个时间那个地点时，我只记得我居住的棚户区的土路和光秃秃的院子里干燥的九月

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无草的
vocabulary.context: And so, when I think of that time and that place, I remember only the dry September of the dirt roads and grassless yards of the shantytown where I lived.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以，当我想起那个时间那个地点时，我只记得我居住的棚户区的土路和光秃秃的院子里干燥的九月

vocabulary.definition: n. 逼近, 危急, 迫近的危险, 急迫；[医] 迫切, 危迫
vocabulary.context: As I think of those days I feel most poignantly the tag end of summer, the bright, dry times when we began to have a sense of shortening days and the imminence of the cold.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我回想起那些日子时，我最心酸的是夏天即将结束，在明亮、干燥的时期，我们开始感到白昼缩短，寒冷即将来临

vocabulary.definition: n. 辛酸, 强烈, 深刻, 辛辣
vocabulary.context: Yet, there are times when the image of those passionate yellow mounds returns with a painful poignancy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，有时，那些热情的黄色土丘的形象会带着一种痛苦的辛酸回来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 摇摇欲坠的
vocabulary.context: After our few chores around the tumbledown shanty, Joey and I were free to run wild in the sun with other children similarly situated.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我们在破旧的棚屋里做完一些家务后，乔伊和我可以和其他处境相似的孩子一起在阳光下尽情奔跑

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不清楚的, 欠明了的
vocabulary.context: For the most part, those days are ill-defined in my memory, running together and combining like a fresh watercolor painting left out in the rain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在大多数情况下，那些日子在我的记忆中都是模糊的，它们一起奔跑、组合在一起，就像一幅留在雨中的新鲜水彩画

vocabulary.definition: n. 裁缝业, 做衣服, 成衣, 剪裁, 制作
vocabulary.context: That tailoring is coupled with a sense that real people on the app are synthesizing and delivering information, rather than faceless websites.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种定制与应用程序上的真人正在综合和传递信息的感觉相结合，而不是不露面的网站

vocabulary.definition: adv. 奇怪地, 神秘地, 危险地
vocabulary.context: More and more young people are using TikTok's powerful algorithm - which personalizes the videos shown to them based on their interactions with content - to find information uncannily catered to their tastes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 越来越多的年轻人正在使用 TikTok 强大的算法（该算法根据他们与内容的互动对向他们展示的视频进行个性化）来寻找非常适合他们口味的信息

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无面的, 无个性的, 匿名的, 无名的
vocabulary.context: That tailoring is coupled with a sense that real people on the app are synthesizing and delivering information, rather than faceless websites.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种定制与应用程序上的真人正在综合和传递信息的感觉相结合，而不是不露面的网站

vocabulary.definition: n. 周围环境, 气氛
vocabulary.context: A long-winded written review of a restaurant can't capture its ambience, food and drinks like a bite-size clip can, she said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说，一篇冗长的餐厅书面评论无法像小夹子那样捕捉餐厅的氛围、食物和饮料

vocabulary.definition: n. 关键字；[计] 关键字
vocabulary.context: The app is testing a feature that identifies keywords in comments and links to search results for them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该应用程序正在测试一项功能，该功能可以识别评论中的关键字以及它们的搜索结果链接

vocabulary.definition: adj. 单独一处就可以提供各种服务的
vocabulary.context: TikTok "is becoming a one-stop shop for content in a way that it wasn't in its earlier days," said Lee Rainie, who directs internet and technology research at the Pew Research Center.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 皮尤研究中心互联网和技术研究主管李·雷尼 (Lee Rainie) 表示，TikTok"正在以前所未有的方式成为一站式内容商店"

vocabulary.definition: n. 猛击, 苦干, 跋涉；v. 猛击, 苦干
vocabulary.context: Instead of just slogging through walls of text, Gen Z-ers crowdsource recommendations from TikTok videos to pinpoint what they are looking for, watching video after video to cull the content.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Z 世代不再只是费力地翻阅文字墙，而是从 TikTok 视频中众包推荐，以查明他们正在寻找的内容，观看一个又一个视频来筛选内容

vocabulary.definition: adj. 气息长的, 冗长的, 罗唆的
vocabulary.context: A long-winded written review of a restaurant can't capture its ambience, food and drinks like a bite-size clip can, she said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说，一篇冗长的餐厅书面评论无法像小夹子那样捕捉餐厅的氛围、食物和饮料

vocabulary.definition: n. 拖延, 因循, 耽搁；[法] 拖延, 耽搁, 因循
vocabulary.context: Video games or social media may be avoidable, but most students need to use computers for school assignments, build tech skills for the workplace, and learn to combat distraction and procrastination as part of growing up.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 电子游戏或社交媒体可能是可以避免的，但作为成长的一部分，大多数学生需要使用计算机完成学校作业，培养工作场所的技术技能，并学会对抗分心和拖延

vocabulary.definition: adj. 反生产的；使达不到预期目标的
vocabulary.context: Based on her experience with drug and alcohol addiction, she thinks grouping kids together who have problems with screens can be counterproductive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据她吸毒和酗酒的经验，她认为将有屏幕问题的孩子聚集在一起可能会适得其反

vocabulary.definition: n. 反对吸烟；禁止吸烟；adj. 反对吸烟的；v. 反对吸烟（antismoke的现在分词）
vocabulary.context: Along with Common Sense Media, a nonprofit that offers parents research and resources on kids' media use, they are currently launching a "Truth About Tech" campaign that Harris compares to anti-smoking campaigns exposing the workings of Big Tobacco.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们与 Common Sense Media（一家为家长提供儿童媒体使用方面的研究和资源的非营利组织）合作，目前正在发起一项"科技真相"活动，哈里斯将其与揭露大烟草公司运作方式的反吸烟活动进行了比较

vocabulary.definition: n. 交通信号灯
vocabulary.context: Even at a stoplight.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 即使在红绿灯处

vocabulary.definition: adj. 学龄的
vocabulary.context: In the study, researchers asked parents of school-age children to complete a questionnaire based on the criteria for "Internet Gaming Disorder."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这项研究中，研究人员要求学龄儿童的家长根据"网络游戏障碍"的标准填写一份调查问卷

vocabulary.definition: n. 保险公司；[法] 保险人, 承保人, 保险公司
vocabulary.context: This omission is important not only because it shapes therapists' and doctors' understanding of their patients but because without an official DSM code, it is harder to bill insurers for treatment of a specific issue.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一遗漏很重要，不仅因为它影响了治疗师和医生对患者的理解，而且因为如果没有官方的 DSM 代码，就很难向保险公司收取针对特定问题的治疗费用

vocabulary.definition: adj. 包括一切的
vocabulary.context: "We have, as a society, gone all-in on tech," he says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "作为一个社会，我们已经全力发展科技，"他说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可避免的；[法] 可作为无效的, 可回避的, 可避免的
vocabulary.context: Video games or social media may be avoidable, but most students need to use computers for school assignments, build tech skills for the workplace, and learn to combat distraction and procrastination as part of growing up.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 电子游戏或社交媒体可能是可以避免的，但作为成长的一部分，大多数学生需要使用计算机完成学校作业，培养工作场所的技术技能，并学会对抗分心和拖延

vocabulary.definition: n. 色情描写, 黄色书刊, 色情画, 色情照片, 色情文学；[法] 色情画, 色情文学, 黄色读物
vocabulary.context: This, he says, results in "a ton of compulsive behavior" - around everything from pornography to World of Warcraft to Facebook.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他说，这会导致"大量强迫行为"--从色情内容到魔兽世界再到 Facebook

vocabulary.definition: n. 声明, 公告, 宣言, 宣告, 发表, 意见, 决定；[法] 宣判, 宣布, 宣告
vocabulary.context: The word "addiction" may currently be attracting controversy, but you don't need a doctor's official pronouncement to work on putting the devices down more often - or to encourage your kids to do so as well.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "成瘾"这个词目前可能引起争议，但你不需要医生的官方声明来努力减少使用电子设备的次数，或者鼓励你的孩子也这样做

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 扩展...以上, 过度伸张, 走过头；v.i. 伸得过远, 过分, 过火
vocabulary.context: Some critics of social media bans claim that they are an example of governmental overreach.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些社交媒体禁令的批评者声称这是政府越权的一个例子

vocabulary.definition: n. 证实, 查证, 证明属实；[计] 验证
vocabulary.context: For example, the 2023 Protecting Kids on Social Media Act would require platforms to verify users' ages, restrict accounts for children under 13, require parental consent for teens, and prevent them from using or keeping age verification information for other purposes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，2023 年《社交媒体保护儿童法案》将要求平台验证用户年龄、限制 13 岁以下儿童的账户、要求青少年获得家长同意，并阻止他们出于其他目的使用或保留年龄验证信息

vocabulary.definition: n. 下锚, 锚地, 停泊税；[化] 固着; 固定; 锚定
vocabulary.context: Representative Andrew Gray, a state legislator representing East Anchorage in the Alaska State House, was alarmed enough to take action.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿拉斯加州议会代表东安克雷奇的州议员安德鲁·格雷（Andrew Gray）感到震惊，并采取了行动

vocabulary.definition: abbr. semicustom chip row organized layout 半定制芯片行编排电路设计, 半定制芯片行结构版图
vocabulary.context: Several states have proposed laws that limit the way social media companies can collect data and use algorithms to keep users scrolling indefinitely.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些州已提出法律，限制社交媒体公司收集数据和使用算法让用户无限期滚动的方式

vocabulary.definition: n. 制裁, 镇压, 痛击
vocabulary.context: A growing social media crackdown?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 社交媒体打击力度不断加大？

vocabulary.definition: n. 不满, 不平
vocabulary.context: Citing research, Gray said, "social media can lead to body dissatisfaction, eating disorders, low self esteem...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 格雷援引研究称，"社交媒体会导致身体不满意、饮食失调、自卑......

vocabulary.definition: n. 算法；[计] 算法
vocabulary.context: Companies also can't use personal data in recommendation systems - or algorithms that suggest content, such as TikTok's "For You" page - for anyone under 18.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 公司也不能在推荐系统或建议内容的算法中使用针对 18 岁以下任何人的个人数据，例如 TikTok 的"For You"页面

vocabulary.context: For example, the 2023 Protecting Kids on Social Media Act would require platforms to verify users' ages, restrict accounts for children under 13, require parental consent for teens, and prevent them from using or keeping age verification information for other purposes.

vocabulary.definition: n. 虚弱, 病身, 疾病；[医] 衰弱

vocabulary.contextTranslation: "医生们一致要求我完全休息，不要精神兴奋，避免任何剧烈的体育锻炼，"弗拉姆顿宣布道，他一直在一种相当普遍的错觉中工作，即陌生人和偶然认识的人都渴望了解一个人的疾病和虚弱、其原因和治疗的最细微的细节

vocabulary.definition: adv. 口吃地, 支吾地

The case went all the way to the Supreme Court, which ruled that the...

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 帕特森的研究项目本身就如滚雪球般不断发展，这得益于她对科科的热爱以及媒体日益增加的关注

vocabulary.definition: n. 筹款, 募款；adj. 筹款的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有提及大猩猩基金会筹集 250 万美元的筹款计划，将 Koko 迁移到毛伊岛的保护区；钱已经花掉了，但搬迁却从未发生

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 在...周围画线, 限制
vocabulary.context: She lives in a small circumscribed world of dialogues and games with caregivers, play with dolls, decorated cakes on her birthday - and her own credit card (no explanation is offered in the film for how Koko uses a credit card).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她生活在一个与护理人员对话和游戏的小世界里，玩洋娃娃，在生日时装饰蛋糕--还有她自己的信用卡（影片中没有解释科科如何使用信用卡）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非人类的
vocabulary.context: Here is nonhuman primate grief mediated through language: In historical footage in the film, Patterson is seen asking Koko, "What happened to Ball?" In reply, Koko utters these signs in sequence: cat, cry, have-sorry, Koko-love .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是通过语言介导的非人类灵长类动物的悲伤：在电影的历史镜头中，可以看到帕特森问科科，"鲍尔出了什么事？"作为回应，科科依次说出了这些手势：猫、哭、对不起、科科爱

vocabulary.definition: adj. 灵魂的, 精神的, 心灵的；n. 巫师, 灵物, 心灵现象

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 也许最令人担忧的是由不止一个人提出的指控，即一名通灵者负责科科的护理，包括每天给科科服用大量药物

vocabulary.definition: n. 总理, 首相；adj. 首位的, 最初的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 周三晚，BBC纪录片《科科：会说话的大猩猩》在美国首映

vocabulary.definition: n. 对某种文化的适应

He suggests that narrative intelligence may be a crucial step in machine "enculturation," allowing artificial intelligence systems to acquire human social norms, customs, values and etiquette - aspects of commonsense understanding that are notoriously difficult for computers to learn.

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有常识的, 一望而知的, 明明白白的
vocabulary.context: ..."enculturation," allowing artificial intelligence systems to acquire human social norms, customs, values and etiquette - aspects of commonsense understanding that are notoriously difficult for computers to learn.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ..."文化适应"，允许人工智能系统获得人类社会规范、习俗、价值观和礼仪--众所周知，计算机很难学习常识性理解的各个方面

vocabulary.definition: v. 使适应某种文化
vocabulary.context: And just as stories might provide a valuable way to "enculturate" machines, they surely provide a crucial mechanism for the social transmission of information in humans.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如故事可能提供一种"适应"机器的有价值的方式一样，它们也肯定为人类信息的社会传播提供了一种至关重要的机制

vocabulary.definition: n. 一般化, 普遍化, 概括；[化] 推广; 普适化
vocabulary.context: In a book published in 1990, for example, computer and cognitive scientist Roger Schank argued for a crucial link between narrative and intelligence, with narratives guiding learning, structuring memory and supporting generalization.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，在 1990 年出版的一本书中，计算机和认知科学家罗杰·尚克 (Roger Schank) 主张叙事与智力之间存在重要联系，叙事可以指导学习、构建记忆并支持泛化

vocabulary.definition: n. 礼仪, 礼节, 成规；[法] 规则, 成规, 礼节
vocabulary.context: ...crucial step in machine "enculturation," allowing artificial intelligence systems to acquire human social norms, customs, values and etiquette - aspects of commonsense understanding that are notoriously difficult for computers to learn.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...机器"文化适应"的关键一步，使人工智能系统能够获取人类社会规范、习俗、价值观和礼仪--众所周知，计算机很难学习常识性理解的各个方面

vocabulary.definition: adv. 臭名昭著的, 声名狼藉的
vocabulary.context: ...intelligence systems to acquire human social norms, customs, values and etiquette - aspects of commonsense understanding that are notoriously difficult for computers to learn.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...获取人类社会规范、习俗、价值观和礼仪的智能系统--众所周知，计算机很难学习这些常识性理解的方面

vocabulary.definition: adv. 依其申述
vocabulary.context: Gould allegedly characterized humans as "the primates who tell stories." Psychologist Robyn Dawes went much further, suggesting humans are "the primates whose cognitive capacity shuts down in the absence of a story."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据称古尔德将人类描述为"会讲故事的灵长类动物"。心理学家罗宾·道斯（Robyn Dawes）走得更远，认为人类是"在没有故事的情况下认知能力就会关闭的灵长类动物"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 文学的, 文艺的, 精通文学的；[法] 文学的, 从事文学的, 从事写作的
vocabulary.context: Stories have been studied in various forms across a range of disciplines, from literary and media studies to psychology and linguistics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从文学和媒体研究到心理学和语言学，故事已经以各种形式被跨学科研究

vocabulary.definition: n. 无力, 无能为力
vocabulary.context: powerlessness in this poem's mouth, a blend
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这首诗口中的无力感，融为一体

vocabulary.definition: n. 袖口, 一巴掌；v.t. 用巴掌打；v.i. 殴斗
vocabulary.context: numb, already losing feeling in its cuffed phrasing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 麻木，已经在措辞中失去了感觉

vocabulary.definition: n. 唾液, 唾吐, 小雨, 炙叉, 一铲的深度；v.t. 唾吐, 吐出, 降小雨, 用炙叉穿过；v.i. 吐唾沫, 吐痰, 唾弃, 飘霏霏细雨
vocabulary.context: It spits
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 吐口水

vocabulary.definition: n. 摘录；v. 引用, 摘录
vocabulary.context: decide what to excerpt.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 决定摘录哪些内容

vocabulary.definition: n. 隐喻
vocabulary.context: it won't find the right metaphor for the pain
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它找不到正确的痛苦比喻

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 混合；n. 混合
vocabulary.context: powerlessness in this poem's mouth, a blend
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这首诗口中的无力感，融为一体

vocabulary.definition: n. 白昼, 日光；v.t. 使沐浴于日光；v.i. 享有日光
vocabulary.context: Where is the daylight (this poem asks and is
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 日光在哪里（这首诗问并且是

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 保持, 保有, 留住, 记得, 付定金聘请；[经] 保留, 留存
vocabulary.context: It assumed it retained
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 假设它保留

vocabulary.definition: n. 抵抗力, 反抗, 耐力, 阻力, 电阻；[化] 抵抗; 抗性; 阻力; 抗药性; 电阻
vocabulary.context: thus crushed) between existence and resistance,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此被压垮）在存在与抵抗之间，

vocabulary.definition: n. 微生物, 微小动植物；[化] 微生物
vocabulary.context: Fortunately, for almost any kind of bacteria or other microorganisms from Earth, it would be very difficult if not impossible to survive on the Martian surface, never mind flourish.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 幸运的是，对于来自地球的几乎任何种类的细菌或其他微生物来说，在火星表面生存即使不是不可能，也是非常困难的，更不用说繁衍生息了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自立的, 自持的
vocabulary.context: On the Moon for example, a colony must be self-sustaining and protect its inhabitants from the airless, harsh environment outside.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，在月球上，殖民地必须能够自我维持，并保护其居民免受外界空气不流通、恶劣环境的影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 出铁槽；着陆器；把钩工人
vocabulary.context: If bacteria from Earth made it to the Martian surface and survived, it would complicate the search for life there; if a lander or rover was to later identify living organisms in the soil, it might be difficult to determine whether they were just contamination or true native life forms.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果来自地球的细菌到达火星表面并存活下来，那么在那里寻找生命就会变得复杂；如果着陆器或漫游车稍后要识别土壤中的生物体，可能很难确定它们是否只是污染或真正的本土生命形式

vocabulary.definition: n. 生物圈；[医] 生物圈, 生物层
vocabulary.context: Colonization(noun)the process of taking control over a place If Mars does have any kind of biosphere, it should be preserved as much as possible.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 殖民（名词）控制一个地方的过程如果火星确实有任何类型的生物圈，那么它应该尽可能地保留下来

vocabulary.definition: adv. 技术上地；科技地
vocabulary.context: Although still a long ways off technologically, terraforming the Red Planet is seen as a future possibility.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管在技术上还有很长的路要走，但对红色星球进行地球化改造被视为未来的可能性

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可以忽略的, 不足取的, 无用的；[医] 可不计的, 可忽视的
vocabulary.context: The risk of planet-wide contamination is very negligible, but it is still better to take strict preventive measures than to play with chance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 全球范围内污染的风险非常微不足道，但采取严格的预防措施仍然比碰运气要好

vocabulary.definition: n. 污染, 污物；[化] 污染; 沾污
vocabulary.context: There is also the more current but related problem of contamination.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有一个更当前但相关的污染问题

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 弄复杂, 使错综, 使恶化；v.i. 变复杂
vocabulary.context: If bacteria from Earth made it to the Martian surface and survived, it would complicate the search for life there; if a lander or rover was to later identify living organisms in the soil, it might be difficult to determine whether they were just contamination or true native life forms.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果来自地球的细菌到达火星表面并存活下来，那么在那里寻找生命就会变得复杂；如果着陆器或漫游车稍后要识别土壤中的生物体，可能很难确定它们是否只是污染或真正的本土生命形式

vocabulary.definition: n. 重视家庭生活的人, 喜欢在家消遣的男人
vocabulary.context: homebody, body in bed watching TV.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 宅男，躺在床上看电视

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无交通往来的, 无通信的
vocabulary.context: To be a country of trafficless roads
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 成为无交通道路的国家

vocabulary.definition: n. 共同工作的人, 同事, 合作者
vocabulary.context: their new coworker is like that , they can tell
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的新同事就是这样，他们看得出来

vocabulary.definition: n. 小山顶
vocabulary.context: & be a painting, an amateur's hilltop & stars,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是一幅画，一个业余爱好者的山顶和星星，

vocabulary.definition: n. 龙卷风, 暴风；[化] 旋流(分离,除尘)器
vocabulary.context: To be a cyclone
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 成为旋风

vocabulary.definition: n. 画, 绘画, 油漆；[化] 涂漆
vocabulary.context: & be a painting, an amateur's hilltop & stars,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是一幅画，一个业余爱好者的山顶和星星，

vocabulary.definition: n. 剩余, 备用品, 备件, 备用零件, 备用轮胎；adj. 多余的, 备用的, 空闲的, 节约的, 瘦的；v.i. 节约, 省掉, 宽恕；v.t. 节约, 省用, 剩下, 饶恕, 赦免
vocabulary.context: To be, in my spare time,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在空闲时间，

vocabulary.definition: n. 叔父, 伯父, 姨丈
vocabulary.context: America for my uncle, who wants to be China
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我叔叔的美国，他想成为中国

vocabulary.definition: n. 行星, 命运星辰, 杰出的人, 重大影响的事
vocabulary.context: a season from the planet
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 来自星球的季节

vocabulary.definition: n. 电子邮件( e-mail的名词复数 )；v. 电子邮件( e-mail的第三人称单数 )
vocabulary.context: Before you start firing off all those e-mails about money, love, health and so on, give these folks a chance to explain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在你开始发送所有有关金钱、爱情、健康等的电子邮件之前，给这些人一个解释的机会

vocabulary.definition: n. 胜任, 职称, 能力；[医] 能力, 活性
vocabulary.context: In a series of studies, the couple demonstrated that an office window that overlooks a natural scene helps people relax, thus fighting off one of the primary threats to competence, mental fatigue.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一系列研究中，这对夫妇证明，俯瞰自然景观的办公室窗户有助于人们放松，从而抵御能力的主要威胁之一--精神疲劳。 【02】再过一会儿，他半年饭的日子就到了，然后会发生什么呢？

vocabulary.definition: n. 沉淀物, 凝结物, 结果；v.t. 猛抛, 使陷入, 促成, 使沉淀；v.i. 猛地落下；adj. 突然的, 猛地落下的, 轻率的
vocabulary.context: What could have precipitated such an unreasonable act of violence?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是什么导致了如此无理的暴力行为？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 更可取的, 更好的, 更合意的
vocabulary.context: A picture with a little mystery is preferable because "people want to explore, and they want to find things out," he says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 带有一点神秘色彩的图片更好，因为"人们想要探索，他们想要找出答案，"他说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不好不坏的, 通常, 平凡的
vocabulary.context: Strangely enough, the urban scenes were shot by an expert photographer and were much better in quality than the scenes of nature, which were shot by a mediocre photographer after a dry, dull summer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 奇怪的是，城市场景是由专业摄影师拍摄的，质量比由平庸摄影师在干燥、沉闷的夏季后拍摄的自然场景要好得多

vocabulary.definition: n. 无可奈何状态, 无能为力; 无助的状态
vocabulary.context: Nothing is more irritating, or frustrating, than the feeling of helplessness, so if you want to make a difference you've got to take control, right?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有什么比无助的感觉更让人恼火或更沮丧的了，所以如果你想有所作为，你就必须掌控一切，对吧？

vocabulary.definition: n. 诊断；[计] 诊断
vocabulary.context: "The first thing they wanted to do when they got their diagnosis was take a ride in the country," Kaplan says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "当他们得到诊断后，他们想做的第一件事就是去乡下兜风，"卡普兰说

vocabulary.definition: n. 实现, 领悟, 实得；[医] 现实化, 实现
vocabulary.context: "So gradually we came to the realization that what people want to do is participate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "所以我们逐渐意识到人们想做的就是参与

vocabulary.definition: n. 斩首, 撤职, 解雇；[医] 断头术
vocabulary.context: The Aztec civilization that flourished in Mesoamerica between 1345 and 1521 CE has gained an infamous reputation for bloodthirsty human sacrifice with striking tales of beating hearts being ripped from still-conscious victims, decapitation, skinning, and dismemberment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 公元 1345 年至 1521 年间在中美洲蓬勃发展的阿兹特克文明因嗜血的活人祭祀而臭名昭著，其惊人的故事包括从仍有意识的受害者身上扯下跳动的心脏、斩首、剥皮和肢解

vocabulary.definition: n. 不凋花, 苋属植物, 紫红色；[化] 苋菜红
vocabulary.context: These were made from ground amaranth mixed with human blood and honey, with the effigy being burnt or eaten after the ritual.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些雕像是用磨碎的苋菜与人血和蜂蜜混合制成的，仪式后将雕像焚烧或食用

vocabulary.definition: adv. 按照仪式地
vocabulary.context: Priests might sacrifice foodstuffs and objects of precious metals, jade, and shells, which might be ritually buried.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 祭司可能会牺牲食物以及贵金属、玉石和贝壳等物品，这些物品可能会在仪式上被埋葬

vocabulary.definition: v. 当家庭教师( tutor的过去式和过去分词 ); 任大学导师; 任课
vocabulary.context: The victim was the god's manifestation on earth until that final brutal moment of the Tóxcatl ritual when he met his maker, and until then was tutored by priests, given a female entourage, and honored with dances and flowers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 受害者是神的在地球上的显现，直到托克斯卡特仪式的最后残酷时刻，当他遇到他的创造者时，直到那时他都受到牧师的教导，被给予女性随从，并以舞蹈和鲜花表示敬意

vocabulary.definition: n. 演员；模拟艺人
vocabulary.context: The Tezcatlipoca impersonator was treated like royalty for one year prior to the sacrificial ceremony.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特斯卡特利波卡模仿者在祭祀仪式前一年受到皇室般的待遇

vocabulary.definition: n. 食品, 粮食
vocabulary.context: Priests might sacrifice foodstuffs and objects of precious metals, jade, and shells, which might be ritually buried.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 祭司可能会牺牲食物以及贵金属、玉石和贝壳等物品，这些物品可能会在仪式上被埋葬

vocabulary.definition: v. 使仪式化, 奉行仪式主义( ritualize的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: All of these things did happen but it is important to remember that for the Aztecs the act of sacrifice - of which human sacrifice was only a part - was a strictly ritualized process that gave the highest possible honor to the gods and was regarded as a necessity to ensure mankind's continued prosperity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所有这些事情确实发生了，但重要的是要记住，对于阿兹特克人来说，祭祀行为（活人祭祀只是其中的一部分）是一个严格的仪式化过程，给予诸神最高的荣誉，并被认为是确保人类持续繁荣的必要条件

vocabulary.definition: adj. 嗜杀的, 残忍的
vocabulary.context: The Aztec civilization that flourished in Mesoamerica between 1345 and 1521 CE has gained an infamous reputation for bloodthirsty human sacrifice with striking tales of beating hearts being ripped from still-conscious victims, decapitation, skinning, and dismemberment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 公元 1345 年至 1521 年间在中美洲蓬勃发展的阿兹特克文明因嗜血的活人祭祀而臭名昭著，其惊人的故事包括从仍有意识的受害者身上扯下跳动的心脏、斩首、剥皮和肢解

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 供给, 提供, 装设；[化] 供应; 供给; 配料
vocabulary.context: Indeed, warfare was often conducted for the sole purpose of furnishing candidates for sacrifice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，战争的唯一目的往往是提供牺牲品

vocabulary.definition: adv. 可交换地, 可交替地, 可互换地
vocabulary.context: Even in psychological literature you will frequently find the concepts used interchangeably.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 即使在心理学文献中，您也会经常发现这些概念可以互换使用

vocabulary.definition: n. 精神病理学；[医] 精神病理学, 病理心理学
vocabulary.context: Differences between trait fear and trait anxiety: Implications for psychopathology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特质恐惧和特质焦虑之间的差异：对精神病理学的影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 心理生理学, 精神生理学；[医] 精神生理学, 心理生理学
vocabulary.context: Fear and anxiety: animal models and human cognitive psychophysiology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 恐惧和焦虑：动物模型和人类认知心理生理学

vocabulary.definition: adv. 警觉地, 警惕地
vocabulary.context: In a simple example, people who have been rear-ended in a motor vehicle accident frequently describe that, for many weeks or months, they fear being rear-ended again and, as a result, find themselves vigilantly peering into their rear view mirror in anticipation of an impact occurring.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 举一个简单的例子，在机动车事故中被追尾的人经常描述说，在数周或数月的时间里，他们担心再次被追尾，因此，他们发现自己警惕地盯着后视镜，预计会发生碰撞

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再体验；v.i. 复活；再生
vocabulary.context: Such is the case of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), where the consequence of a prior situation where you actually were in danger is re-lived in the present when those emotional memories are triggered.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这就是创伤后应激障碍（PTSD）的情况，当这些情绪记忆被触发时，您之前确实处于危险之中的情况的后果会在当下重新出现

vocabulary.definition: n. 奋发, 激励
vocabulary.context: In contrast to fear, anxiety is a general state of distress that is longer lasting than fear and usually is triggered by something that is not specific, even though it produces physiological arousal, such as nervousness and apprehension (Lang et al., 2000).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与恐惧相反，焦虑是一种普遍的痛苦状态，比恐惧持续时间更长，通常由不特定的事物触发，尽管它会产生生理唤醒，例如紧张和忧虑（Lang et al., 2000）

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 重新浮现
vocabulary.context: There are times when a past fear might re-emerge, even though the present situation does not truly warrant the need to be afraid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有时，过去的恐惧可能会重新出现，尽管目前的情况并不真正值得恐惧

vocabulary.definition: adj. 善良的, 良性的；[医] 良性的
vocabulary.context: Similarly, individuals who are trait fearful-those who tend to have personality characteristics that are dominated by the emotion of fear-will avoid taking risks that are generally perceived by others as relatively benign (Sylvers, et al., 2011).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 同样，具有恐惧特质的人（那些往往具有受恐惧情绪支配的人格特征的人）将避免承担其他人通常认为相对良性的风险（Sylvers 等人，2011）

vocabulary.definition: adv. 即, 就是, 换句话说
vocabulary.context: As a result, some researchers suggest an expanded version of the fight-or-flight response, namely, "freeze, flight, fight, or fright" (Bracha, Ralston, Matsunaga, Williams, & Bracha, 2004).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，一些研究人员提出了战斗或逃跑反应的扩展版本，即"冻结、逃跑、战斗或惊吓"（Bracha、Ralston、Matsunaga、Williams 和 Bracha，2004）

vocabulary.definition: n. 微生物
vocabulary.context: Along with embryos, a spaceship would have to send into space wombs for them to develop in, with the placenta that feeds us, and mother's milk for the newborns, and the many species of micro-organisms that live within our bodies and keep us alive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了胚胎之外，宇宙飞船还必须将它们送入太空子宫，供它们发育，还有喂养我们的胎盘、新生儿的母乳，以及生活在我们体内并维持我们生命的多种微生物

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重新组成, 重新设立；[法] 重新构成, 重新组成, 重新设立
vocabulary.context: An extraterrestrial host in space might reconstitute humanity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 太空中的外星宿主可能会重建人类

vocabulary.definition: adj. 保障生命的, 维持生命的；n. 生命维持治疗（或材料、器材）
vocabulary.context: Instead, we would come to our senses in time to stop killing our life-support system, and not merely out of an instinct for survival.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，我们会及时醒悟，停止破坏我们的生命支持系统，而不仅仅是出于生存的本能

vocabulary.definition: n. 胎盘, 胎座；[化] 胎盘
vocabulary.context: Along with embryos, a spaceship would have to send into space wombs for them to develop in, with the placenta that feeds us, and mother's milk for the newborns, and the many species of micro-organisms that live within our bodies and keep us alive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了胚胎之外，宇宙飞船还必须将它们送入太空子宫，供它们发育，还有喂养我们的胎盘、新生儿的母乳，以及生活在我们体内并维持我们生命的多种微生物

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 除霜；v.i. 解冻
vocabulary.context: Why would they take the chance on defrosting our descendants?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为什么他们要趁机解冻我们的后代？

vocabulary.definition: v. 再播种于
vocabulary.context: A certain kind of optimist imagines that, if we ruin our own Earth, we can reseed our species there.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 某种乐观主义者想象，如果我们毁了自己的地球，我们可以在那里重新播种我们的物种

vocabulary.definition: n. 子宫, 发源地；[医] 子宫
vocabulary.context: Along with embryos, a spaceship would have to send into space wombs for them to develop in, with the placenta that feeds us, and mother's milk for the newborns, and the many species of micro-organisms that live within our bodies and keep us alive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了胚胎之外，宇宙飞船还必须将它们送入太空子宫，供它们发育，还有喂养我们的胎盘、新生儿的母乳，以及生活在我们体内并维持我们生命的多种微生物

vocabulary.definition: n. 唯物主义
vocabulary.context: These communitarian impulses drive us, too, and for most of us they are deeper and more fulfilling than naked materialism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些社群主义冲动也驱动着我们，对于我们大多数人来说，它们比赤裸裸的唯物主义更深刻、更充实

vocabulary.definition: n. 狭小, 小气
vocabulary.context: We deplore the narrowness and intolerance of the Puritans; but they were less narrow and intolerant than the English and most of the Europeans of that day.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们对清教徒的狭隘和不宽容表示遗憾；但他们不像当时的英国人和大多数欧洲人那么狭隘和不宽容

vocabulary.definition: n. 打扰者
vocabulary.context: They banished Roger Williams as a disturber of the peace, not for his opinions; but they bore witness to his spotless character.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们将罗杰·威廉姆斯驱逐为扰乱和平的人，而不是因为他的观点；但他们见证了他一尘不染的品格

vocabulary.definition: n. 文牍主义；[法] 条文主义, 墨守法律条文, 合法主义
vocabulary.context: Puritanism was painfully stern and somber; it was founded on the strictest, unmollified Calvinism; it breathed the air of legalism rather than of free grace, and received its inspiration from the Old Testament rather than the New.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 清教主义是极其严厉和忧郁的。它建立在最严格、最顽固的加尔文主义之上。它呼吸着律法主义的气息，而不是白白的恩典，它的灵感来自旧约而不是新约

vocabulary.definition: n. 家长式作风, 家长式统治；[经] 家长作风
vocabulary.context: Paternalism in government was the rule in the other colonies and in Europe, but nowhere was it carried to such an extreme as in New England.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 政府中的家长作风是其他殖民地和欧洲的普遍现象，但没有哪个地方像新英格兰那样被推向如此极端

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不歪的, 不走歪的, 不编离的, 始终不渝的
vocabulary.context: His unswerving devotion to duty, his unlimited courage based on the fear of God, his love of liberty and hatred of tyranny - these are the qualities that have enthroned him in the memory of the American people.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他对职责的坚定不移的奉献，他基于敬畏上帝的无限勇气，他对自由的热爱和对暴政的憎恨--这些品质使他在美国人民的记忆中登上了宝座

vocabulary.definition: adj. 尊重法律的
vocabulary.context: No further proof of the legalistic tendencies of Puritan worship is needed than a glance at their own laws.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 无需进一步证明清教徒崇拜的律法主义倾向，只要看一眼他们自己的法律即可

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不容忍的, 不容异说的, 偏执的, 偏狭的, 不宽容的, 不耐的, 不耐性的
vocabulary.context: We deplore the narrowness and intolerance of the Puritans; but they were less narrow and intolerant than the English and most of the Europeans of that day.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们对清教徒的狭隘和不宽容表示遗憾；但他们不像当时的英国人和大多数欧洲人那么狭隘和不宽容

vocabulary.definition: adj. 登上王位的；v. 使（国王、女王或主教）登基, 即位( enthrone的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: His unswerving devotion to duty, his unlimited courage based on the fear of God, his love of liberty and hatred of tyranny - these are the qualities that have enthroned him in the memory of the American people.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他对职责的坚定不移的奉献，他基于敬畏上帝的无限勇气，他对自由的热爱和对暴政的憎恨--这些品质使他在美国人民的记忆中登上了宝座

vocabulary.definition: n. 茂盛, 茂盛的生长物, 丰富
vocabulary.context: Laws were made that tended to force the conscience, to curb the freedom of the will, and to suppress the natural exuberance of youth - laws that could not have been enacted and enforced by a people who comprehended the full meaning of Gospel liberty, or had caught that keynote of religious freedom sounded by the ancient prophet and resounded by St.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 制定的法律往往会强迫良心，限制意志自由，并压制年轻人的自然活力 - 这些法律不可能由一个理解福音自由的全部含义的人制定和执行，或者捕捉到古代先知所发出的宗教自由基调并由圣约翰所响亮的人所制定和执行的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 昏暗的, 阴天的, 严峻的, 忧郁的
vocabulary.context: Puritanism was painfully stern and somber; it was founded on the strictest, unmollified Calvinism; it breathed the air of legalism rather than of free grace, and received its inspiration from the Old Testament rather than the New.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 清教主义是极其严厉和忧郁的。它建立在最严格、最顽固的加尔文主义之上。它呼吸着律法主义的气息，而不是白白的恩典，它的灵感来自旧约而不是新约

vocabulary.definition: n. 举行赛会的场所, 露天市场
vocabulary.context: For example, persons of Japanese ancestry in western Washington State were removed to the assembly center at the Puyallup Fairgrounds near Tacoma.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，华盛顿州西部的日裔人士被转移到塔科马附近普亚勒普集市的集会中心

vocabulary.definition: n. 反对法西斯主义者；adj. 反对法西斯主义的
vocabulary.context: Prior to Pearl Harbor, the United States had been involved in the European war only by supplying England and other antifascist countries of Europe with necessary war materials.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 珍珠港事件之前，美国仅通过向英国和欧洲其他反法西斯国家提供必要的战争物资来介入欧洲战争

vocabulary.definition: adj. 土著的, 本地生的, 土生土长的
vocabulary.context: Roosevelt's order affected 117,000 people of Japanese descent, two-thirds of whom were native-born citizens of the United States.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 罗斯福的命令影响了117,000名日裔，其中三分之二是美国本土出生的公民

vocabulary.definition: n. 被拘留者；[法] 拘留犯, 被拘留者
vocabulary.context: One of the most stunning ironies in this episode of American civil liberties was articulated by an internee who, when told that the Japanese were put in those camps for their own protection, countered, "If we were put there for our protection, why were the guns at the guard towers pointed inward, instead of outward?"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这一美国公民自由事件中，最令人震惊的讽刺之一是由一名被拘留者提出的，当他被告知日本人被关进这些集中营是为了保护自己时，他反驳道："如果我们被关在那里是为了保护自己，为什么岗哨塔上的枪口是向内而不是向外？"

vocabulary.definition: n. 拘留；[法] 拘留, 收容, 禁闭
vocabulary.context: The Issei were the first generation of Japanese in this country; the Nisei were the second generation, numbering 70,000 American citizens at the time of internment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一诚家族是这个国家的第一代日本人；尼塞伊家族是第二代，在被拘留时有 70,000 名美国公民

vocabulary.definition: n. 跑道
vocabulary.context: From Puyallup to Pomona, internees found that a cowshed at a fairgrounds or a horse stall at a racetrack was home for several months before they were transported to a permanent location.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从皮阿拉普到波莫纳，被拘留者发现游乐场的牛棚或赛马场的马厩在他们被运送到永久地点之前已经在家待了几个月

vocabulary.definition: n. 间谍活动；[法] 间谍活动, 刺探, 间谍
vocabulary.context: The objectives of the order were to prevent espionage and to protect persons of Japanese descent from harm at the hands of Americans who had strong anti-Japanese attitudes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该命令的目的是防止间谍活动并保护日本血统的人免受具有强烈反日态度的美国人的伤害

vocabulary.definition: n. 现场, 场所；[计] 场所
vocabulary.context: Relocation centers were situated many miles inland, often in remote and desolate locales.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安置中心位于内陆数英里处，通常位于偏远荒凉的地区

vocabulary.definition: adj. 稀疏的, 稀稀落落的, 稀薄的
vocabulary.context: As four or five families with their sparse collections of clothing and possessions squeezed into shared barracks made of tar paper, life took on some familiar routines of socializing and school.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当四五个家庭带着稀少的衣物和财产挤进用焦油纸搭建的共用营房时，生活就开始了一些熟悉的社交和上学生活

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使位于, 使处于
vocabulary.context: Relocation centers were situated many miles inland, often in remote and desolate locales.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安置中心位于内陆数英里处，通常位于偏远荒凉的地区

vocabulary.definition: n. 阻挠议事的活动, 掠夺兵, 暴兵, 海盗；v.i. 掠夺；v.t. (以冗长的演说)阻碍议案通过
vocabulary.context: Opponents launched the longest filibuster in American history, which lasted 57 days and brought the Senate to a virtual standstill.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 反对者发起了美国历史上最长的阻挠议事活动，持续了57天，导致参议院几乎陷入瘫痪

vocabulary.definition: v. (使)偏振, (使)极化, (使)两极分化
vocabulary.context: This decision polarized Americans, fostered debate, and served as a catalyst to encourage federal action to protect civil rights.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一决定使美国人两极分化，引发了辩论，并成为鼓励联邦采取行动保护公民权利的催化剂

vocabulary.definition: n. 同国人；adj. 同国的, 同胞的
vocabulary.context: The Negro and his white compatriots for self-respect and human dignity will not be denied."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黑人和他的白人同胞的自尊和人类尊严不会被否认。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 废止种族隔离
vocabulary.context: The Act outlawed discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin, required equal access to public places and employment, and enforced desegregation of schools and the right to vote.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该法案禁止基于种族、肤色、宗教、性别或国籍的歧视，要求平等进入公共场所和就业，并强制取消学校种族隔离和投票权

vocabulary.definition: n. 停止, 停顿；[医] 停顿
vocabulary.context: Opponents launched the longest filibuster in American history, which lasted 57 days and brought the Senate to a virtual standstill.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 反对者发起了美国历史上最长的阻挠议事活动，持续了57天，导致参议院几乎陷入瘫痪

vocabulary.definition: n. 触媒, 接触剂, 催化剂, 刺激因素, 促进因素；[计] 催化剂
vocabulary.context: This decision polarized Americans, fostered debate, and served as a catalyst to encourage federal action to protect civil rights.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一决定使美国人两极分化，引发了辩论，并成为鼓励联邦采取行动保护公民权利的催化剂

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 报复, 回敬；v.i. 报复, 以牙还牙
vocabulary.context: The real battle was waiting in the Senate, however, where concerns focused on the bill's expansion of federal powers and its potential to anger constituents who might retaliate in the voting booth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，真正的战斗正在参议院等待，参议院的担忧集中在该法案扩大联邦权力及其可能激怒可能在投票站进行报复的选民

vocabulary.definition: n. 听, 听觉, 听讯；[医] 听, 听觉
vocabulary.context: It passed the House on February 10, 1964 after 70 days of public hearings, appearances by 275 witnesses, and 5,792 pages of published testimony.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 经过 70 天的公开听证会、275 名证人出庭以及 5,792 页公开证词后，该法案于 1964 年 2 月 10 日在众议院获得通过

vocabulary.definition: n. 生物技术
vocabulary.context: in chemical engineering and biotech.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 化学工程和生物技术

vocabulary.definition: n. 重罪犯, 恶棍, 瘭疽；[医] 瘭疽, 指头脓炎
vocabulary.context: And for that I am a felon for life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为此我是终身重罪犯

vocabulary.definition: n. 修复（等于rehabilitation）
vocabulary.context: Prison is no rehab.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 监狱不是戒毒所

vocabulary.definition: adj. 上诉的, 有权受理上诉的；[法] 受理上诉的, 上诉的, 控诉的
vocabulary.context: The appellate court ruled that the sanctions charges could not stand and they were overturned because the jury did not get the family money exception to the law.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 上诉法院裁定制裁指控不成立，并被推翻，因为陪审团没有获得家庭资金的法律例外

vocabulary.definition: n. 小型货车
vocabulary.context: They bring me a pair of pants and rush me off into a minivan.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们给我拿了一条裤子，然后把我赶进一辆小型货车

vocabulary.definition: n. 分类帐, 分户总帐, 盖墓石板, 横木；[经] 分户帐, 分类帐
vocabulary.context: By the time we got to trial, we got the discovery, all that evidence: the ledgers, list of customers, an underground banking operation, an international money broker network - it didn't exist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我们进入审判阶段时，我们得到了发现，所有证据：分类账、客户名单、地下银行业务、国际货币经纪网络 - 它并不存在

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 不如实地叙述(或说明), 不称职地代表
vocabulary.context: They claimed in these new charges that I misrepresented where Mom's money came from.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们在这些新指控中声称我歪曲了妈妈的钱的来源

vocabulary.definition: n. 掮客, 经纪人；[经] 经纪人, 掮客
vocabulary.context: By the time we got to trial, we got the discovery, all that evidence: the ledgers, list of customers, an underground banking operation, an international money broker network - it didn't exist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我们进入审判阶段时，我们得到了发现，所有证据：分类账、客户名单、地下银行业务、国际货币经纪网络 - 它并不存在

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使(一地区)人口过剩,使人口过密
vocabulary.context: 13 was a huge, overpopulated place and it took me days and many discreet questions to discover that Eugene was in honors classes for all his subjects; classes that were not open to me because English was not my first language, though I was a straight A student.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 13是一个巨大的、人口过多的地方，我花了几天的时间和许多谨慎的问题才发现尤金正在参加他所有科目的荣誉课程；尽管我是全优学生，但由于英语不是我的母语，这些课程不对我开放

vocabulary.definition: adj. 悬疑的, 令人紧张的
vocabulary.context: A few suspenseful moments later the door opened just a crack.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 过了一会儿，门只开了一条缝

vocabulary.definition: adj. 悦耳的
vocabulary.context: "What do you want?" Her voice was tiny and sweet-sounding, like a little girl's, but her tone was not friendly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "你想要什么？"她的声音很小，听起来很甜，就像一个小女孩的声音，但语气并不友善

vocabulary.definition: adj. <贬>（妇女）乳房小的, 平胸的
vocabulary.context: I hated my skinny flat-chested body, and I envied the black girls who could jump rope so fast that their legs became a blur.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我讨厌自己骨瘦如柴的平胸身材，羡慕那些跳绳快得腿都模糊的黑人女孩

vocabulary.definition: n. 谄上欺下, 势利
vocabulary.context: I was ready for rejection, snobbery, the worst.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我已经做好了被拒绝、势利、最糟糕的准备

vocabulary.definition: n. 招魂术者,关亡人,巫师
vocabulary.context: In fact, soon his photograph would be hung alongside the Sacred Heart and over the spiritist altars that many women kept in their apartments.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，很快他的照片就会挂在圣心旁边，以及许多妇女在她们公寓里供奉的招魂术祭坛上

vocabulary.definition: n. 自动唱机
vocabulary.context: At almost any hour of the day, El Building was like a monstrous jukebox, blasting out salsas from open windows as the residents, mostly new immigrants just up from the island, tried to drown out whatever they were currently enduring with loud music.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 几乎一天中的任何时候，埃尔大厦就像一个巨大的自动点唱机，从开着的窗户里喷出莎莎舞曲，而居民（大多是刚从岛上来的新移民）试图用吵闹的音乐来淹没他们目前正在忍受的一切

vocabulary.definition: adj. 月亮似的, 恍惚的, 发呆的
vocabulary.context: In her abrupt way she said: "Elena, you are acting 'moony.'" Enamorada was what she really said, that is - like a girl stupidly infatuated.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她以她唐突的方式说道："埃琳娜，你表现得很'月亮'。"她真正说的是"Enamorada"，那就是--就像一个愚蠢迷恋的女孩

vocabulary.definition: n. 泼妇( virago的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: But the day President Kennedy was shot there was a profound silence in El Building; even the abusive tongues of viragoes, the cursing of the unemployed, and the screeching of small children had been somehow muted.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但肯尼迪总统被枪杀的那天，埃尔大厦陷入了深深的沉默；就连泼妇们的谩骂声、失业者的咒骂声、小孩子的尖叫声，也不知何故都被静音了

vocabulary.definition: n. <美>萨尔萨舞曲； （辣）沙司，（辣）调味汁( salsa的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: At almost any hour of the day, El Building was like a monstrous jukebox, blasting out salsas from open windows as the residents, mostly new immigrants just up from the island, tried to drown out whatever they were currently enduring with loud music.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 几乎一天中的任何时候，埃尔大厦就像一个巨大的自动点唱机，从开着的窗户里喷出莎莎舞曲，而居民（大多是刚从岛上来的新移民）试图用吵闹的音乐来淹没他们目前正在忍受的一切

vocabulary.definition: n. 踏车, 令人厌倦的工作；[法] 踏车
vocabulary.context: Their effort is an attempt to deal with an idea that has been kicking around for four decades, called "Hedonic Adaptation," or the "Hedonic Treadmill." That theory suggests that good things may move us up on the happiness scale, but in time the glow dims and we return to a point established chiefly by genetics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的努力是试图解决一个已经存在了四十年的想法，称为"享乐适应"或"享乐跑步机"。该理论表明，美好的事物可能会让我们的幸福指数上升，但随着时间的推移，这种光芒会变暗，我们会回到主要由遗传学确定的点

vocabulary.definition: n. 悲观, 悲观情绪, 悲观主义；[医] 悲观主义
vocabulary.context: Sheldon and Lyubomirsky argue that simple lifestyle changes can help keep us a bit happier, "despite pessimism from the current literature that the pursuit of happiness may be largely futile," as Lyubomirsky puts it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 谢尔顿和柳博米尔斯基认为，简单的生活方式改变可以帮助我们保持更快乐，"尽管当前的文献持悲观态度，认为追求幸福可能在很大程度上是徒劳的"，正如柳博米尔斯基所说

vocabulary.definition: n. 气压计；[化] 气压计
vocabulary.context: It seems we are never satisfied, and that brings the happiness barometer down.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们似乎永远不会满足，这导致幸福指数下降

vocabulary.definition: v. 意味, 带有…的性质( savor的现在分词 ); 给…加调味品; 使有风味; 品尝
vocabulary.context: The researchers tested 481 students over two semesters to measure their level of happiness and determine if savoring a good thing could last even a few weeks.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究人员在两个学期内对 481 名学生进行了测试，以衡量他们的幸福程度，并确定品味美好事物是否可以持续几周

vocabulary.definition: n. 遗传学；[化] 遗传学
vocabulary.context: Their effort is an attempt to deal with an idea that has been kicking around for four decades, called "Hedonic Adaptation," or the "Hedonic Treadmill." That theory suggests that good things may move us up on the happiness scale, but in time the glow dims and we return to a point established chiefly by genetics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的努力是试图解决一个已经存在了四十年的想法，称为"享乐适应"或"享乐跑步机"。该理论表明，美好的事物可能会让我们的幸福指数上升，但随着时间的推移，这种光芒会变暗，我们会回到主要由遗传学确定的点

vocabulary.definition: n. 表面, 外表；adj. 表面的, 肤浅的, 面积的
vocabulary.context: "And if you look at studies of various superficial circumstances, like income, where you live, how many cars you have, those are pretty small," Sheldon said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果你看一下对各种表面情况的研究，比如收入、你住在哪里、你有多少辆汽车，这些都是相当小的，"谢尔顿说

vocabulary.context: In his view, he wrote, "the wondrous demonstrations for democracy and economic change...ending in the butchery on Beijing's streets on June 4, were but the most visible and tragic manifestations of China's intellectual, social and political turmoil."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他写道，在他看来，"民主和经济变革的精彩示威......最终以 6 月 4 日北京街头的屠杀告终，不过是中国思想、社会和政治动荡的最明显、最悲惨的表现。"

vocabulary.context: In the weeks that followed, Deng delivered a speech accusing the students of trying to "establish a totally Western-dependent bourgeois republic"; ordered widespread arrests of protesters and their supporters; expelled foreign journalists, and controlled the domestic coverage of the events so strictly that even now it's not known how...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在接下来的几周里，邓小平发表讲话，指责学生试图"建立一个完全依赖西方的资产阶级共和国"；下令大规模逮捕抗议者及其支持者；驱逐外国记者，对国内事件的报道控制得如此严格，以至于现在还不知道如何......

vocabulary.context: It found that this layer was thicker in children who got a lot of cognitive stimulation and had nurturing parents, says Martha Farrah of the University of Pennsylvania.

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 伦敦认知神经科学研究所的凯瑟琳·米尔斯 (Kathryn Mills) 表示，扫描显示青春期期间四个区域发生了巨大的结构变化，这有助于我们理解他人的意图、信仰和愿望

vocabulary.context: "The teen brain isn't broken," says Jay Giedd, a child psychiatry researcher at the National Institute of Mental Health.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "青少年的大脑并没有坏掉，"美国国家心理健康研究所的儿童精神病学研究员杰伊·吉德说

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家在新奥尔良神经科学学会会议上报告说，青少年的大脑确实容易冲动

vocabulary.definition: n. 敏感, 灵敏度；[计] 灵敏度

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说这表明青少年对奖励的敏感性有时可以带来更好的决定

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 威尔康奈尔医学院的 BJ Casey 表示，部分不良评价来自于研究表明，青少年的大脑"天生"会从事危险行为，例如吸毒或不安全的性行为

vocabulary.context: As a result, he proposes, sad volunteers largely rejected the stereotype of philosophers as tweedy, professorial men, helping to minimize the halo effect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，他提出，悲伤的志愿者在很大程度上拒绝了哲学家作为粗花呢的教授的刻板印象，这有助于最大限度地减少光环效应

vocabulary.definition: adv. 口才好地；令人信服地
vocabulary.context: People in sad moods also show a greater willingness to work on demanding tasks, communicate more persuasively and are more concerned with being fair to others than are peers in neutral or happy moods.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与情绪中立或快乐的同龄人相比，情绪悲伤的人也更愿意完成艰巨的任务，更有说服力地沟通，并且更关心公平对待他人

vocabulary.definition: adv. 可扩张地, 辽阔地
vocabulary.context: Among neutral-mood volunteers focusing broadly on an experimental task, those induced to be happy thought even more expansively about the task, whereas those prodded into sadness switched to concentrating on details.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在广泛关注实验任务的中性情绪志愿者中，那些被诱导感到快乐的志愿者对这项任务的思考更加广泛，而那些被刺激到悲伤的志愿者则转而专注于细节

vocabulary.definition: n. 活泼快乐, 戏谑; 童心
vocabulary.context: Sad moods fostered attention to details, they discovered, whereas happy moods promoted playfulness and creativity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们发现，悲伤的情绪会促进对细节的关注，而快乐的情绪会促进乐趣和创造力

vocabulary.definition: n. 黑暗, 沮丧
vocabulary.context: And a little gloominess could help job applicants; lousy moods cut down on the tendency to stereotype others, thus boosting the accuracy of first impressions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一点忧郁可以帮助求职者；糟糕的情绪会减少对他人的刻板印象，从而提高第一印象的准确性

vocabulary.definition: adv. 目光短浅地
vocabulary.context: Mild sadness tends to make people more impatient and thus more apt to focus myopically on taking money now rather than waiting for a bigger financial payoff in the not-too-distant future, Lerner and her colleagues reported in the January Psychological Science .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 勒纳和她的同事在一月份的《心理科学》杂志上报告说，轻微的悲伤往往会让人们更加不耐烦，因此更容易短视地关注现在的金钱，而不是等待在不久的将来获得更大的经济回报

vocabulary.definition: adj. 消息不灵通的, 所知不多的
vocabulary.context: If that's the case, then people may make particularly rash and ill-informed financial decisions after job losses, loved ones' deaths and other distressing events.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果是这样的话，那么人们在失业、亲人去世和其他令人痛苦的事件后可能会做出特别草率和不明智的财务决定

vocabulary.definition: adj. 斗鸡眼的, 歪的, 荒唐的
vocabulary.context: Negative moods topple the halo effect off its cockeyed perch, Forgas reported in the December 2011 European Journal of Social Psychology .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 福加斯在 2011 年 12 月的《欧洲社会心理学杂志》上报道，消极情绪会推翻光环效应

vocabulary.definition: n. 镇定剂, 令人沮丧的人
vocabulary.context: Problems occur when sad moods become so frequent that they blend into an extended downer, she holds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她认为，当悲伤情绪变得如此频繁以至于融入长期的沮丧情绪时，就会出现问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 穿花呢服的, 穿着漂亮的, 松散悠闲的, 男子气概的
vocabulary.context: As a result, he proposes, sad volunteers largely rejected the stereotype of philosophers as tweedy, professorial men, helping to minimize the halo effect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，他提出，悲伤的志愿者在很大程度上拒绝了哲学家作为粗花呢的教授的刻板印象，这有助于最大限度地减少光环效应

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可继承的, 有继承权的, 可遗传的；[法] 可继承的, 有继承权的, 可遗传的
vocabulary.context: And that bright line says: 'We're not going to make genetic changes that are inheritable - that are passed down from one generation to the next.' "
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那条亮线说："我们不会做出可遗传的基因改变--从一代传到下一代。" "

vocabulary.definition: n. 染色体；[化] 染色体
vocabulary.context: Unlike the DNA that most people are familiar with - the 23 pairs of human chromosomes that program most of our body processes - mtDNA is the bit of genetic material inside mitochondria, living structures inside a cell that provide its energy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与大多数人熟悉的 DNA（23 对人类染色体，负责编程我们身体的大部分过程）不同，mtDNA 是线粒体内的一小部分遗传物质，线粒体是细胞内提供能量的生命结构

vocabulary.definition: n. 小过失, 差错( glitch的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: Between 1 in 2,000 and 1 in 4,000 babies may be born each year with syndromes caused by these genetic glitches; the syndromes range from mild to severe.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每年，每 2,000 名到 4,000 名婴儿中就有 1 名可能患有由这些遗传故障引起的综合症；症状从轻微到严重不等

vocabulary.definition: n. 病, 疾病, 弊病；[医] 病
vocabulary.context: Lori Martin, 33, of Houston, found out her son, Will, was born with a genetic malady known as Leigh's syndrome, when he was about 2 years old.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 33 岁的休斯顿洛里·马丁 (Lori Martin) 发现她的儿子威尔 (Will) 出生时患有一种名为利氏综合症 (Leigh's Syndrome) 的遗传性疾病，当时他大约 2 岁

vocabulary.definition: adv. 漫不经心地, 疏忽地; 非故意地
vocabulary.context: There are even bigger concerns, which start with whether the technique is safe for the resulting infant, and whether by trying to fix one problem, scientists may inadvertently introduce mistakes into the human genetic code.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有更大的担忧，首先是该技术对由此产生的婴儿是否安全，以及科学家在试图解决一个问题时是否可能无意中将错误引入人类遗传密码

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使移居, 移植, 使迁移；v.i. 移居, 耐移植；n. 移居者, 移植
vocabulary.context: "We're ready to move on to the next stage and to transplant the embryos we have created this way into a patient," says Shoukhrat Mitalipov of the Oregon Health & Science University in Portland.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 波特兰俄勒冈健康与科学大学的 Shoukhrat Mitalipov 说："我们已经准备好进入下一阶段，将我们以这种方式创造的胚胎移植到患者体内。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 将要成为的, 自称的, 冒充的；[法] 自愿成为的, 愿成而未成的
vocabulary.context: The new egg could then be fertilized in the laboratory with the would-be father's sperm.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，新的卵子可以在实验室中与未来父亲的精子受精

vocabulary.definition: n. 改革论者, 进步论者；adj. 前进的, 累进的, 进步的
vocabulary.context: The progressive illness was linked to genetic problems in his mitochondria.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 进行性疾病与线粒体的遗传问题有关

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 以重音念, 强调, 重读
vocabulary.context: Over and over again, I heard that power doesn't change people as much as it accentuates their preexisting traits.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我一遍又一遍地听说，权力并没有改变人们，而是强调了人们先前存在的特征

vocabulary.definition: v. 先前存在
vocabulary.context: Over and over again, I heard that power doesn't change people as much as it accentuates their preexisting traits.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我一遍又一遍地听说，权力并没有改变人们，而是强调了人们先前存在的特征

vocabulary.definition: n. 猪, 贪婪者, 象猪般的人；v. (使)拱起
vocabulary.context: They hogged the quick, exciting work and dumped the long, dull responsibilities on a colleague.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们霸占了快速、令人兴奋的工作，而把漫长、沉闷的责任交给了同事

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 拔开...的塞子, 拔去...的插头, 除掉...的障碍物
vocabulary.context: The participants' odds of moving it away, turning it off, or unplugging it spiked from 42 percent to 69 percent if they had just written about a time when they had power.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果参与者刚刚写下他们有电的时间，那么他们将其移开、关闭或拔掉电源的可能性从 42% 飙升至 69%

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 剥夺律师资格
vocabulary.context: Back in the late 1930s, a man fresh out of law school was trying his first case when the judge threatened to disbar him: "I have serious doubts whether you have the ethical qualifications to practice law," the judge said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 早在 1930 年代末，一名刚从法学院毕业的男子正在审理他的第一起案件，法官威胁要取消他的律师资格："我严重怀疑你是否具备执业律师的道德资格，"法官说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 环境形成的；情形的
vocabulary.context: The "power of a host of situational variables can dominate an individual's will to resist."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "一系列情境变量的力量可以支配个人的抵抗意愿。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 填补的, 多余的, 附加的, 补足的；n. 语助词, 虚词, 感叹词, 诅咒语, 咒骂语, 惊叹语, 填补物, 附加物
vocabulary.context: As Slack founder and CEO Stewart Butterfield quipped, "It doesn't make you an [expletive].
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如 Slack 创始人兼首席执行官斯图尔特·巴特菲尔德 (Stewart Butterfield) 打趣道，"这并不会让你成为[脏话]

vocabulary.definition: adj. 虐待狂的, 残酷成性的
vocabulary.context: "In only a few days, our guards became sadistic," psychologist Philip Zimbardo said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 心理学家菲利普·津巴多（Philip Zimbardo）说："仅仅几天之内，我们的警卫就变得虐待狂了。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使道德败坏, 使堕落, 挫折锐气
vocabulary.context: The Catholic Church has offered demoralizing revelations of how priests have abused their power over nuns and children.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 天主教会揭露了神父如何对修女和儿童滥用权力，这令人士气低落

vocabulary.definition: adv. 恶魔般地；非常恼人地
vocabulary.context: That I could, with the extinction of your miserable existence, restore those victims whom you have so diabolically murdered!"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我可以随着你悲惨的存在的消失，让那些被你如此残忍地谋杀的受害者复活！"

vocabulary.definition: n. 憎恶, 嫌恶, 令人厌恶的人
vocabulary.context: But I scarcely observed this; rage and hatred had at first deprived me of utterance, and I recovered only to overwhelm him with words expressive of furious detestation and contempt.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但我几乎没有注意到这一点；愤怒和仇恨起初让我无法说话，后来我才恢复过来，用表达愤怒和蔑视的言语压倒了他

vocabulary.definition: adv. 疏忽地；粗心大意地
vocabulary.context: You reproach me with your creation; come on, then, that I may extinguish the spark which I so negligently bestowed."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你用你的创造来责备我；那么，来吧，我可以扑灭我如此疏忽所赐予的火花。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 微弱, 衰弱, 模糊
vocabulary.context: I was troubled; a mist came over my eyes, and I felt a faintness seize me; but I was quickly restored by the cold gale of the mountains.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我很烦恼；我的眼睛蒙上了一层雾气，我感到一阵眩晕。但山间的寒风很快让我恢复了过来

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使饱足, 使厌腻, 充分满足；adj. 饱足的, 厌腻的
vocabulary.context: If you will comply with my conditions, I will leave them and you at peace; but if you refuse, I will glut the maw of death, until it be satiated with the blood of your remaining friends."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果你愿意遵守我的条件，我就让他们和你都安宁；但如果你拒绝，我将吞食死亡的胃，直到它被你剩下的朋友的鲜血填满。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可溶解的, 可分解的, 可解除的
vocabulary.context: Yet you, my creator, detest and spurn me, thy creature, to whom thou art bound by ties only dissoluble by the annihilation of one of us.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，你，我的创造者，厌恶并抛弃了我，你的创造物，你与我之间的纽带只有在我们中的一个人被消灭时才能解除

vocabulary.definition: n. 恶意, 歹意, 恶性；[法] 极端憎恨, 极大的恶意, 恶意言行
vocabulary.context: He approached; his countenance bespoke bitter anguish, combined with disdain and malignity, while its unearthly ugliness rendered it almost too horrible for human eyes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他走近了；他的面容显示出极度的痛苦，夹杂着蔑视和恶毒，而其超凡脱俗的丑陋使得它对于人类的眼睛来说几乎太可怕了

vocabulary.definition: n. 罪行, 犯罪；[法] 不端行为, 犯罪, 恶性
vocabulary.context: Remember that I am thy creature; I ought to be thy Adam, but I am rather the fallen angel, whom thou drivest from joy for no misdeed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 记住我是你的创造物；我本应是你的亚当，但我却是堕落的天使，你无缘无故地将他从欢乐中赶走

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非世间的, 神秘的, 怪异的
vocabulary.context: He approached; his countenance bespoke bitter anguish, combined with disdain and malignity, while its unearthly ugliness rendered it almost too horrible for human eyes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他走近了；他的面容显示出极度的痛苦，夹杂着蔑视和恶毒，而其超凡脱俗的丑陋使得它对于人类的眼睛来说几乎太可怕了

vocabulary.definition: n. 恳求, 乞求
vocabulary.context: Will no entreaties cause thee to turn a favourable eye upon thy creature, who implores thy goodness and compassion?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有任何恳求会让你对你的生物投以好感，因为你的生物恳求你的善良和同情心吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 警官；警员
vocabulary.context: Then the official, calling the soldiers, said, "I am the police-officer of this district, and I question you because the merchant with whom you spent last night has been found with his throat cut.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，官员呼叫士兵们说："我是这个地区的警察，我向你们提问是因为昨晚与你们共处的那个商人被发现喉咙被割断了

vocabulary.definition: n. 盘问, 讯问；v.t. 盘问, 审问
vocabulary.context: Aksionov wondered why he was asked all these questions, but he described all that had happened, and then added, "Why do you cross-question me as if I were a thief or a robber?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿克肖诺夫想知道为什么他被问到所有这些问题，但他描述了所发生的一切，然后补充道，"为什么你盘问我，就好像我是小偷或强盗一样？

vocabulary.definition: v. 解开皮带( unstrap的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: The soldiers and the police-officer unstrapped Aksionov's luggage and searched it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 士兵和警察解开阿克肖诺夫的行李并进行搜查

vocabulary.definition: n. 卢布(俄国货币)
vocabulary.context: Aksionov swore he had not done it; that he had not seen the merchant after they had had tea together; that he had no money except eight thousand rubles of his own, and that the knife was not his.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿克肖诺夫发誓他没有这么做；他们一起喝完茶后，他就没有见到商人了。他除了自己的八千卢布外一无所有，而且那把刀也不是他的

vocabulary.definition: v. 盘问( cross-question的现在分词 ); 反复询问; 审问
vocabulary.context: Aksionov answered him fully, and said, "Won't you have some tea with me?" But the official went on cross-questioning him and asking him.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿克肖诺夫完整地回答了他，并说道："你不和我一起喝杯茶吗？"但该官员继续盘问他并询问他

vocabulary.definition: adj. 金发的
vocabulary.context: Aksionov was a handsome, fair-haired, curly-headed fellow, full of fun, and very fond of singing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿克肖诺夫是一位英俊的、金发卷发的小伙子，充满乐趣，非常喜欢唱歌

vocabulary.definition: n. 温顺, 柔和
vocabulary.context: The prison authorities liked Aksionov for his meekness, and his fellow-prisoners respected him: they called him "Grandfather," and "The Saint." When they wanted to petition the prison authorities about anything, they always made Aksionov their spokesman, and...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 监狱当局喜欢阿克肖诺夫的温顺，他的狱友也尊敬他：他们称他为"祖父"和"圣人"。当他们想向监狱当局请愿任何事情时，他们总是让阿克肖诺夫成为他们的发言人，而且......

vocabulary.definition: n. 三头马车
vocabulary.context: Suddenly a troika drove up with tinkling bells and an official alighted, followed by two soldiers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 突然，一辆三辆马车叮当作响地驶来，一名官员下了车，后面跟着两名士兵

vocabulary.definition: adj. 引起骚扰的, 暴乱的, 放荡的
vocabulary.context: When quite a young man he had been given to drink, and was riotous when he had had too much; but after he married he gave up drinking, except now and then.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当他还是个年轻人的时候，他就被人酗酒，喝多了就变得暴躁。但结婚后他就戒酒了，除了偶尔

vocabulary.definition: n. 询问；[经] 询价, 询盘
vocabulary.context: Enquiries as to his character were made in Vladimir.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在弗拉基米尔对他的性格进行了询问

vocabulary.definition: n. disrespectable的变形
vocabulary.context: "For a long time, comics had this air of disrespectability," Higgins said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "很长一段时间以来，漫画都带有一种不尊重的气氛，"希金斯说

vocabulary.definition: n. 再度出现
vocabulary.context: Captain America, a Marvel Comics character that originally debuted in the 1940s shortly after Superman, made a reemergence in the 2000s and the 2011 film, Captain America: The First Avenger , took on the 9/11 attacks.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国队长是漫威漫画中的角色，最初在超人之后不久的 2000 年代首次亮相，并在 2000 年代重新出现，2011 年的电影《美国队长：复仇者先锋》承担了 9/11 袭击事件

vocabulary.definition: v.t. (根据演员体形与性格)分配角色, 浇铸(铅字)
vocabulary.context: "Because I was typecast extraordinarily," West said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "因为我的风格非常独特，"韦斯特说

vocabulary.definition: n. 忘恩者；adj. 不知恩的, 忘恩的
vocabulary.context: Why be an ingrate?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为什么要忘恩负义？

vocabulary.definition: n. 写博客的人；博客使用者
vocabulary.context: "Clark Kent is actually a blogger at this moment in time," DC's DiDio said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "克拉克·肯特此时此刻实际上是一位博主，"DC 的迪迪奥说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 顽固的, 死硬的
vocabulary.context: For die-hard fans in those years, admitting you like men in tights became about as cool as attending a Star Trek convention.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于那些年的铁杆粉丝来说，承认自己像穿紧身衣的男人就像参加《星际迷航》大会一样酷

vocabulary.definition: adj. 隐喻性的, 比喻性的
vocabulary.context: The superheroes themselves have changed their metaphorical tunes ever so slightly over the years in response to the challenges we need them to tackle throughout history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 多年来，超级英雄本身已经稍微改变了他们的隐喻基调，以应对我们需要他们在整个历史中应对的挑战

vocabulary.definition: adj. 超人的, 人类能力所不能及的
vocabulary.context: Part of their appeal is that we can relate to them, despite their being superhuman.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的部分吸引力在于我们可以与他们产生共鸣，尽管他们是超人

vocabulary.definition: n. 公民权, 特权, 特许经营权, 免赔额；v.t. 给以特权, 给以...公民权
vocabulary.context: "We look at Batman as being the ultimate policeman and Superman as the ultimate fireman," said Dan DiDio, the co-publisher for DC Entertainment, the comic book company that owns both franchises.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们认为蝙蝠侠是终极警察，超人是终极消防员，"拥有这两个系列的漫画公司 DC Entertainment 的联合出版商丹·迪迪奥 (Dan DiDio) 说道

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自由放任的；n. 放任政策
vocabulary.context: The other hidden cause of decay was the do-nothing, almost laissez-faire attitude of the federal government.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 腐败的另一个隐藏原因是联邦政府无所作为、近乎放任的态度

vocabulary.definition: n. 笨人, 傻子
vocabulary.context: Mencken, a celebrated (and scathing) columnist during the "Roaring Twenties" wrote that: "No other such a complete and dreadful nitwit [as Harding] is to be found in the pages of American history." Harding proposed reducing the national debt, protecting farm interests, and cutting back on immigration.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 门肯是"咆哮的二十年代"期间著名的（而且尖刻的）专栏作家，他写道："在美国历史上，找不到任何一个像哈丁那样彻底而可怕的白痴了。"哈丁提议减少国债、保护农业利益并减少移民

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 闪身, 闪开, 闪避
vocabulary.context: It was a period when they reveled in the high jinks of what the period's historian Nathan Miller called an "era of wonderful nonsense," but when they also suffered the crushing consequences of what economists called "unfettered capitalism."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这段时期，他们陶醉于历史学家内森·米勒所说的"美妙胡言乱语的时代"，但同时他们也遭受了经济学家所说的"不受约束的资本主义"的毁灭性后果

vocabulary.definition: n. (喻)困难的境地；v.t. 使(球)处于困难的位置, 使为难, 使处困境, 妨碍, 阻挠, 阻碍
vocabulary.context: Hoover tried to stymie the fall by increasing public works spending to $434 million - an enormous sum for the period - but it was far from enough to turn the economy around.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 胡佛试图通过将公共工程支出增加到 4.34 亿美元来阻止经济下滑--这对当时来说是一笔巨款--但这远远不足以扭转经济

vocabulary.definition: n. 狐步舞；v.i. 跳狐步舞
vocabulary.context: Above-ground, jazz was the king and American foxtrot, Viennese waltz, and Argentine tango dominated the dance floors; Greta Garbo, Clark Gable and other Hollywood celebrities drew millions in the new movie houses that showed "talkies" (movies with sound);...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在地面上，爵士乐为王，美国狐步舞、维也纳华尔兹和阿根廷探戈主导了舞池；葛丽泰·嘉宝、克拉克·盖博和其他好莱坞名人在放映"有声电影"（有声电影）的新电影院中吸引了数百万人；...

vocabulary.definition: adj. 对话过多的
vocabulary.context: ...the dance floors; Greta Garbo, Clark Gable and other Hollywood celebrities drew millions in the new movie houses that showed "talkies" (movies with sound); sports stars' pictures filled the newspapers; and fans packed the huge baseball stadiums sprouting on the outskirts of big cities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...舞池；葛丽泰·嘉宝、克拉克·盖博和其他好莱坞名人在放映"有声电影"（有声电影）的新电影院中吸引了数百万人；报纸上充斥着体育明星的照片；大城市郊区的大型棒球场里挤满了球迷

vocabulary.definition: adv. 有效地；显著地
vocabulary.context: A former vice president and senator, he was elected in 1921 in a landslide - on a program tellingly titled "return to normalcy" - after a campaign during which he sat on the porch of his home in Marion, Ohio, and received delegations of voters.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为前副总统和参议员，他于 1921 年以压倒性优势当选--在一项名为"恢复正常"的计划中--在竞选期间，他坐在俄亥俄州马里恩家的门廊上，接待了选民代表团

vocabulary.definition: n. 不胜任, 不够格, 不合格, 不适合, 无能力, 不熟练, 无资格；[医] 机能不全, 闭锁不全, 关闭不全
vocabulary.context: Harding was judged by one of his biographers as: "A prime example of incompetence, sloth, and feeble good nature in the White House." H.L.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 哈丁的一位传记作者评价他为"白宫无能、懒惰和软弱善良的典型例子"。 H.L.

vocabulary.definition: n. 名字, 绰号
vocabulary.context: The decade's popular moniker was, in many ways, appropriate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个十年的流行绰号在很多方面都是恰当的

vocabulary.definition: n. 马提尼（一种鸡尾酒, 等于Martini）
vocabulary.context: One of these lawful drinkers was Secretary of Commerce Herbert Hoover, who had his daily two martinis after work in the Belgian embassy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 商务部长赫伯特·胡佛（Herbert Hoover）就是这些合法饮酒者之一，他每天在比利时大使馆下班后喝两杯马提尼酒

vocabulary.definition: n. 党派性, 党派活动, 派系分裂倾向
vocabulary.context: Traditionally, historians have argued that the witchcraft crisis resulted from factionalism in Salem Village, deliberate faking, or possibly the ingestion of hallucinogens by the afflicted.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 传统上，历史学家认为巫术危机是由塞勒姆村的派系斗争、蓄意造假或可能是受害人摄入致幻剂造成的

vocabulary.definition: adv. 偶发地；零星地
vocabulary.context: The war with the Indians continued for six more years, though sporadically.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与印第安人的战争又持续了六年，尽管是零星的

vocabulary.definition: adv. 很少发生, 不常发生, 罕见, 稀罕, 偶有, 不寻常
vocabulary.context: While witchcraft was often feared, it was punished only infrequently.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然巫术常常令人恐惧，但它很少受到惩罚

vocabulary.definition: n. 摄取；[医] 食入, 摄食
vocabulary.context: Traditionally, historians have argued that the witchcraft crisis resulted from factionalism in Salem Village, deliberate faking, or possibly the ingestion of hallucinogens by the afflicted.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 传统上，历史学家认为巫术危机是由塞勒姆村的派系斗争、蓄意造假或可能是受害人摄入致幻剂造成的

vocabulary.definition: n. (白人和黑人的)混血儿；adj. (黑白)混血儿的, 淡褐色的
vocabulary.context: It is worth noting that while Tituba, one of the first people accused of witchcraft, has traditionally been portrayed as a black or mulatto woman from Barbados, that was not the case.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 值得注意的是，蒂图巴是第一批被指控施行巫术的人之一，传统上被描绘成来自巴巴多斯的黑人或混血妇女，但事实并非如此

vocabulary.definition: adv. 一样, 一致, 相同, 一直不变, 一贯, 始终如一, 均质, 一律, 均匀；[计] 均匀地

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的同时代人一致称她为印度人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 恶魔的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 善意的, 慈善的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国最早的定居者在广阔且有时无情的土地上挣扎求存，他们知道自己被一个神秘的宇宙所包围，宇宙中充满了无形的灵魂--既有仁慈的，也有邪恶的--影响着他们的生活

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我一直希望我的父母能像我同学的父母一样，因为我妈妈是唯一一个穿着萨尔瓦卡米兹（传统孟加拉裤装）出席家长会的人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 声音重浊的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它有两张脸：一侧是一个被砍下的头，是国王或其他值得注意的人的头，另一侧是别的东西，一些能给我们带来安慰的东西：一只鸟，一条鱼，一种毛皮动物

vocabulary.definition: adj. 全知的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们称他们为全知者，我们称他们为光辉者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 值得注目的, 显著的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它有两张脸：一侧是一个被砍下的头，是国王或其他值得注意的人的头，另一侧是别的东西，一些能给我们带来安慰的东西：一只鸟，一条鱼，一种毛皮动物

vocabulary.definition: n. 石堆纪念碑, 石冢, 堆石界标

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你们从某个遥远的世界来到这里，来到这个干涸的湖岸和这个石冢，来到这个黄铜圆柱体，在我们所有记录的日子的最后一天，我在其中写下我们的最后一句话：

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有用的, 良好的, 赞成的, 顺利的；[医] 良好的, 顺利的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有时他们很残酷，喝了我们的血，但他们也给了我们雨水和阳光、顺风、丰收、肥沃的动物和许多孩子

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最后，所有的水井都被污染了，所有的河流都充满了污物，所有的海洋都死了；没有土地可以种植粮食了

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当然，部落主义是有等级之分的

vocabulary.definition: n. detribalize的变形
vocabulary.context: In his recent book Tribe , Sebastian Junger argues for the importance of such socially-bonding values, citing the detribalization of modern life as a cause for our current social and political misdirection.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 塞巴斯蒂安·容格在他最近的著作《部落》中论证了这种社会纽带价值观的重要性，并指出现代生活的非部落化是我们当前社会和政治误导的一个原因

vocabulary.definition: adj. 只关注自身的；封闭的
vocabulary.context: Extreme behavior comes from a radicalized sense of belonging, a desperate attachment to a single-minded goal that causes a short-sightedness of the "other." Extreme behavior is inward-looking, intolerant, unwilling to grow, to look out.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 极端行为来自于一种激进的归属感，一种对单一目标的绝望依恋，导致"他人"的短视。极端行为是内向的、不宽容的、不愿成长、不愿向外看的

vocabulary.definition: n. 无政府主义；[经] 无政府主义
vocabulary.context: "Sacred" here doesn't necessarily imply religious values: "Secularized sacred," as Atran refers to it, can play a key role as well, as in political notions of "human rights" or in ideologies that attempt to save "humanity" through political revolution (socialism, anarchism, communism, fascism, etc.)
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这里的"神圣"并不一定意味着宗教价值观：正如阿特兰所说，"世俗化的神圣"也可以发挥关键作用，就像在"人权"的政治概念或试图通过政治革命（社会主义、无政府主义、共产主义、法西斯主义等）来拯救"人类"的意识形态中一样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 被期待已久的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 天堂之门的成员相信，彗星尾随的不明光点是一个期待已久的救赎不明飞行物，它运送外星人，将他们转移到纯粹的精神存在平面

vocabulary.definition: adj. 天启的, <<启示录>>的
vocabulary.context: History is filled with similar examples of apocalyptic sects whose members chose death over life, some peaceful and others extremely aggressive to anyone who doesn't "belong."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 历史上充满了类似的世界末日教派的例子，这些教派的成员选择了死亡而不是生命，一些教派是和平的，另一些教派则对任何不"属于"的人极具侵略性

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 极端行为来自于一种激进的归属感，一种对单一目标的绝望依恋，导致"他人"的短视。极端行为是内向的、不宽容的、不愿成长、不愿向外看的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 心胸狭窄的, 气量小的, 固执己见的
vocabulary.context: However, taken to its extreme, tribalism is divisive, prejudiced, small-minded, and, as we see every day in the news, deadly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，极端地讲，部落主义是分裂的、偏见的、心胸狭隘的，而且正如我们每天在新闻中看到的那样，它是致命的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 想到这里，他反复喊道："你不知道我是谁吗？"并鼓起勇气拒绝现场清醒测试

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当温特沃斯沿着 27 号公路向西行驶时，迷失方向的温特沃斯漂过两条车道，与一辆载有一家四口的小型货车相撞，让这名青少年面对面地接受了可能改变人生的一课

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自我中心的, 自我本位的, 利己主义的
