vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我很感激我终于可以回到我作为一个以自我为中心的混蛋的生活，相信整个世界都围着他转，"温特沃斯补充道

vocabulary.definition: adj. 精心构思的, 有好亲戚的, 出身名门的
vocabulary.context: Wentworth later said the only thing that got him through that dark time was thinking of his rich, well-connected loved ones.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 温特沃斯后来说，唯一让他度过那段黑暗时光的就是想着他富有、人脉广泛的亲人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 下层社会的；下等阶层的; 下等的, 劣等的, 低级的; 低年级的
vocabulary.context: Peter's University Hospital, suffering from conditions ranging from poor to lower-class.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 圣彼得大学医院的条件从贫困到下层不等

vocabulary.definition: adj. 没有受伤的, 未受损伤的
vocabulary.context: Wentworth escaped unscathed and unpunished, however, when his airbags deployed and a team of high-powered attorneys rushed to the scene and rescued him from the brink of personal responsibility.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，当温特沃斯的安全气囊展开时，一队强大的律师赶到现场，将他从个人责任的边缘解救出来，温特沃斯毫发无伤，也没有受到任何惩罚

vocabulary.definition: adv. 在上；在前
vocabulary.context: Wentworth, reportedly ignoring the protests of his classmates, got behind the wheel of his turbocharged Supra 2000GT after consuming half the contents of a bottle of Goldschläger at a friend's party.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据报道，Wentworth 在朋友的聚会上喝掉了一半的 Goldschläger 酒后，无视同学的抗议，驾驶了他的涡轮增压 Supra 2000GT

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 转开, 避免, 防止
vocabulary.context: Tragedy was averted, however, when his mother paid a consultant to testify before the judge that Wentworth had suffered emotional trauma.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，当他的母亲聘请顾问在法官面前作证说温特沃斯遭受了情感创伤时，悲剧得以避免

vocabulary.definition: v.t. vi. 职业化, 专业化
vocabulary.context: But the other thing was it resourced us training police departments across the state on how to think about potential racial bias and ways to further professionalize what they were doing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但另一件事是，它为我们培训全州警察部门如何考虑潜在的种族偏见以及如何进一步专业化他们正在做的事情提供了资源

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 搞政治；v.t. 使具有政治性
vocabulary.context: They end up being stilted and politicized, and folks are locked into the positions they already have.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们最终变得生硬和政治化，人们被锁定在他们已经拥有的立场上

vocabulary.definition: v. 召开( convene的现在分词 ); 召集; （为正式会议而）聚集; 集合
vocabulary.context: But I do recognize that as President, I've got some convening power, and there are a lot of good programs that are being done across the country on this front.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但我确实认识到，作为总统，我拥有一些召集权，而且全国各地在这方面正在实施许多好的计划

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我反省, 深思
vocabulary.context: And then, finally, I think it's going to be important for all of us to do some soul-searching.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最后，我认为对我们所有人来说进行一些自我反省是很重要的

vocabulary.definition: n. 机能不良, 机能障碍；[医] 机能障碍, 机能不良
vocabulary.context: They understand that some of the violence that takes place in poor black neighborhoods around the country is born out of a very violent past in this country, and that the poverty and dysfunction that we see in those communities can be traced to a very difficult history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们明白，全国各地贫困黑人社区发生的一些暴力事件源于这个国家非常暴力的过去，我们在这些社区看到的贫困和功能失调可以追溯到一段非常艰难的历史

vocabulary.definition: n. 争论, 争吵
vocabulary.context: Along the same lines, I think it would be useful for us to examine some state and local laws to see if it - if they are designed in such a way that they may encourage the kinds of altercations and confrontations and tragedies that we saw in the Florida case, rather than diffuse potential altercations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 同样，我认为对我们来说，检查一些州和地方法律是有用的，看看它们的设计方式是否会鼓励我们在佛罗里达州案件中看到的那种争吵、对抗和悲剧，而不是分散潜在的争吵

vocabulary.definition: n. 注释, 评论, 说明；[法] 注释, 评注, 评论
vocabulary.context: I know that there's been commentary about the fact that the "stand your ground" laws in Florida were not used as a defense in the case.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我知道有人评论说佛罗里达州的"坚守立场"法律没有被用作此案的辩护

vocabulary.definition: adj. 富有的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1913 年 5 月 29 日，巴黎一个温暖的春天夜晚，霍夫曼说，香榭丽舍剧院里富有的人群还没有准备好迎接舞台上锯齿状的节奏、嘎吱作响的不和谐音和舞者奇怪的抽搐

vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这部芭蕾舞剧是由伟大的尼金斯基编排的，"霍夫曼说，"观众中的噪音、打斗和喊叫声非常大，他不得不向舞者大声喊出数字，以便他们知道自己应该做什么。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 慰抚, 安抚, 和解, 谋求...的好感

vocabulary.contextTranslation: "《春之祭》的副标题，"霍夫曼说，"是"异教俄罗斯的图画"，这是对异教仪式的庆祝，最终导致选定的年轻女子献祭以安抚春神

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 英格兰在弗朗西斯·德雷克爵士的领导下，凭借速度更快、机动性更强的舰船，击败了西班牙无敌舰队，标志着全球超级大国的转变

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再磨光, 刷新
vocabulary.context: The colonists got to work rebuilding and refurbishing the fortification and dwellings left by the 1585 expedition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 殖民者开始重建和整修 1585 年远征留下的防御工事和住宅

vocabulary.definition: adj. 稀薄的, 微细的, 纤细的, 脆弱的；[医] 细薄的, 纤细的
vocabulary.context: Once again, the tenuous relationship between the English and the Algonquian broke down.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 英国人和阿尔冈昆人之间的脆弱关系再次破裂

vocabulary.definition: n. 土木工事, 土方工程
vocabulary.context: In addition to the homes constructed along the perimeter of the earthwork, the metal-working shop of Thomas Hariot and Joachim Gans was located nearby.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了沿着土方工程周边建造的房屋外，托马斯·哈里奥特 (Thomas Harriot) 和约阿希姆·甘斯 (Joachim Gans) 的金属加工车间也位于附近

vocabulary.definition: n. 海岸, 沿海地带；adj. 海边的
vocabulary.context: The goal of England's 1584 expedition was not to establish a colony but to scout potential locations along the eastern seaboard for future settlement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1584年英国远征的目标不是建立殖民地，而是沿着东海岸寻找未来定居的潜在地点

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 绕行, 陷害, 包围, 智取
vocabulary.context: Protestant England, under the rule of Elizabeth I, sought to circumvent Spanish dominance in the region by establishing colonies in the New World.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 新教英格兰在伊丽莎白一世的统治下，试图通过在新大陆建立殖民地来规避西班牙在该地区的统治地位

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 证明, 表明, 作证；v.t. 为...作证
vocabulary.context: Barlowe, in his account of the voyage, attested that "the soil is the most plentiful, sweet, fruitful, and wholesome of all the world."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 巴洛在他对这次航行的描述中证明，"这里的土壤是世界上最肥沃、最甘甜、最富饶、最有益健康的。"

vocabulary.definition: n. loveless（无爱情的, 没有爱的）的变形

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 沙漠一直延伸到过去、历史、西方世界，以及干旱和饥荒的根源--无爱的大山

vocabulary.definition: n. 平稳, 平静, 温和
vocabulary.context: Instead they see us staring at them, without begging, and with a bulging placidity in our eyes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，他们看到我们盯着他们，没有乞求，眼里充满了平静

vocabulary.definition: n. 陌生

vocabulary.definition: n. 空运, 空中补给线, 空运物资；v.t. 空运

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们袭击了空运和救援物资，并将大部分食物分发给他们自己和部落成员

vocabulary.definition: n. 否定, 反面, 不存在；[医] 否定, 否认
vocabulary.context: From its peaks, at night, the grim spirits of negation chant their awesome soul-shrinking songs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 夜晚，阴森的否定之灵从它的山峰上吟唱着令人敬畏的灵魂收缩之歌

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 空气中弥漫着腐臭黄油、有毒热气和恶臭污水的味道

vocabulary.definition: n. 教父；v.t. 当教父

vocabulary.definition: n. 无限大, 无限；[计] 无穷

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 似乎花了无限长的时间才到达教学楼

vocabulary.definition: n. 荒芜, 荒废, 荒凉
vocabulary.context: As the cow wandered about in the apparent desolation of the room, it must have seemed odd to the people recording it all that I should have made myself so comfortable among the dead.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当那头牛在房间里明显荒凉的地方徘徊时，记录这一切的人们一定觉得很奇怪，我竟然让自己在死人中间如此舒服

vocabulary.definition: n. 对位法, 旋律配合, 复调

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 世界响起了丰富的对位法、混合的旋律、香味、质感：厨房里压碎的薄荷和大蒜，咖啡中冲泡的小豆蔻，沙发上堆满了精美刺绣的巴勒斯坦枕头

vocabulary.definition: adv. 非常(富有的/伟大的)
vocabulary.context: The world rang rich counterpoint, mixed melodies, fragrances, textures: crushed mint and garlic in the kitchen, cardamom brewing in coffee, fabulously embroidered Palestinian pillows plumped on the couch.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 世界响起了丰富的对位法、混合的旋律、香味、质感：厨房里压碎的薄荷和大蒜，咖啡中冲泡的小豆蔻，沙发上堆满了精美刺绣的巴勒斯坦枕头

vocabulary.definition: n. 毛茛属植物
vocabulary.context: I try to think of supportive women in my community whom I could surprise - friends who might be able to use a bunch of red ranunculuses, a plate of hot gingerbread when it is not even their birthday.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我试着想想我的社区中那些我可以给我带来惊喜的支持女性--那些朋友们，她们可能会在生日之前使用一束红色毛茛属植物，一盘热姜饼

vocabulary.definition: n. 悲伤；懊悔；伤心事；v.i. 懊悔；遗憾；感到悲伤；v.t. 为…悲痛
vocabulary.context: I hold in my heart so many sorrowing individuals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我心里有那么多悲伤的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 侄孙女,甥孙女
vocabulary.context: During a local poetry workshop with fourth-graders, a girl hands me a folded note: "Poetry is eating all my problems." My great-niece stomps her foot.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在当地与四年级学生举行的诗歌研讨会上，一个女孩递给我一张折叠的纸条："诗歌正在解决我所有的问题。"我的曾侄女跺着脚

vocabulary.definition: adj. 结舌的, 说不出话的, 缄默的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们该怎么办？我一时语塞

vocabulary.definition: n. 到麦加朝圣过的伊斯兰教徒

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她为被称为朝圣而感到自豪，事后穿着多层白色衣服

vocabulary.definition: adv. 伤心地, 悲伤地
vocabulary.context: But after September 11, she started poking herself into my dreams again, kindly, sorrowfully: "Say this is not who we are."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但9月11日之后，她又开始把自己戳进我的梦里，友善而悲伤地说道："说这不是我们。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 艺术品鉴赏家

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我立刻就知道这是西蒙·沙欣，一位阿拉伯裔美国小提琴演奏家，一位优雅的男人，穿着笔挺的白衬衫和黑色西装，演奏得像天使一样

vocabulary.definition: n. 命令
vocabulary.context: But unlike some of the nearby walls of graffiti, this tag was done at the behest of the building owner.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但与附近的一些涂鸦墙不同的是，这个标签是根据建筑物业主的要求完成的

vocabulary.context: But at 70 feet tall, the mural is impossible to miss, a testament to changing perceptions of art, vandalism and the neighborhood - and it's only partially obscured by nearby green construction barriers that have been hastily tagged with white spray paint.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但这幅 70 英尺高的壁画不容错过，它证明了人们对艺术、破坏行为和社区观念的改变，而且它只是被附近匆忙用白色喷漆标记的绿色建筑屏障部分遮挡

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在他们不再受到嘲笑了；没有祈祷，也没有钟声；

vocabulary.context: Their flowers the tenderness of patient minds,

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了唱诗班之外，也没有任何哀悼的声音，--

vocabulary.context: Our ponies were taken away from us under the promise that they would be replaced by oxen and large horses; it was long before we saw any, and then we got very few.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们的小马被从我们身边带走，并承诺它们将被牛和大马取代；很久之后我们才看到任何东西，然后我们得到的就很少了

vocabulary.definition: n. 煽动者；[化] 搅拌器; 搅拌机; 搅抖装置
vocabulary.context: Those old men really wished their people to improve, but little men, so-called chiefs were made to act as disturbers and agitators.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那些老人确实希望他们的人民能够进步，但是小人物，所谓的酋长却被迫充当扰乱者和煽动者

vocabulary.definition: n. 借口, 托词；v. 以...为借口
vocabulary.context: We tried with the men we had, but on one pretext or another, we were shifted from one place to another, or were told that such a transfer was coming.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们尝试用我们现有的人员，但由于这样或那样的借口，我们被从一个地方转移到另一个地方，或者被告知这样的转移即将到来

vocabulary.definition: n. 中伤, 诽谤；v.t. 中伤, 诽谤
vocabulary.context: I was abused and slandered, to weaken my influence for good.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我受到谩骂和诽谤，永久削弱了我的影响力

vocabulary.context: We were faint with hunger and maddened by despair.

vocabulary.definition: n. 困境, 窘境；v.t. 宣誓, 保证
vocabulary.context: Red Cloud delivers the following speech after the Wounded Knee Massacre in order to shed light on the plight of the Native American peoples living on reservations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 红云在伤膝大屠杀后发表以下演讲，以阐明生活在保留地的美洲原住民的困境

vocabulary.definition: n. 定额, 定量, 配给；v.t. 配给, 定量供应
vocabulary.context: Our rations began to be reduced; they said we were lazy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们的口粮开始减少；他们说我们很懒

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 引诱, 招致, 归纳出, 感应；[医] 诱导, 感应
vocabulary.context: But the hunger-crazed who had taken fright at the soldiers' coming and went to the Badlands could not be induced to return to the horrors of reservation life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，那些因为士兵的到来而感到恐惧而前往荒地的饥饿狂们却无法被诱导回到保留地生活的恐怖之中

vocabulary.definition: n. 感应, 感觉, 感官, 意识, 观念, 情理, 知觉, 理智；v.t. 感觉, 觉察, 检测；[计] 阅读; 检测
vocabulary.context: How does any man of sense suppose that so great a number of people could get work at once unless they were once supplied with the means to work and instructors enough to teach them?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 任何有理智的人怎么会认为如此多的人可以立即找到工作，除非他们曾经获得过工作手段和足够的指导员来教导他们？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 禁欲主义的, 不以苦乐为意的, 坚忍的, 淡泊的
vocabulary.context: You must either languish in bitterness, or learn to compose your feelings, by stoical indifference.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你必须要么在痛苦中煎熬，要么学会用坚忍的冷漠来平复你的感情

vocabulary.definition: adj. 引起爱情的, 令人钟爱的, 可爱的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 和他在一起，作为你的保护者和知心朋友，你可以向自己保证许多可爱的快乐

vocabulary.definition: n. 依附, 固执, 坚持；[化] 粘合
vocabulary.context: Strict adherence to the conduct recommended in the former chapters, is highly important.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 严格遵守前几章中建议的行为非常重要

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无法形容的, 不可言喻的, 避讳的
vocabulary.context: You perceive that "Innocence, candor, sincerity, heroism and piety, express themselves with grace ineffable in every attitude, in every feature of the man you love." You are therefore highly concerned how you may secure an equal share, and a permanent continuance of his affection and esteem.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你察觉到"纯真、坦率、真诚、英雄主义和虔诚，在你所爱的人的每一种态度、每一个特征中都以难以言喻的优雅表达出来。"因此，你非常关心如何才能获得平等的份额，并永久延续他的感情和尊重

vocabulary.definition: n. 坦白, 直率

You are therefore highly concerned how you may secure an equal share, and a permanent continuance of his affection and esteem.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你察觉到"纯真、坦率、真诚、英雄主义和虔诚，在你所爱的人的每一种态度、每一个特征中都以难以言喻的优雅表达出来。"因此，你非常关心如何才能获得平等的份额，并永久延续他的感情和尊重

The colonists won, and the nation which was established as a result of their victory has held unfailingly that these two fundamental principles of democratic government are not only the spiritual source of our national existence but have been our chief historic pride and at all times the sheet anchor of our...

vocabulary.context: Every delay, every trick, every political dishonesty from now on will antagonize the women of the land more and more, and when the party or parties which have so delayed woman suffrage finally let it come, their sincerity will be doubted and their appeal to the new voters will be met with suspicion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从现在起，每一次拖延、每一次诡计、每一次政治不诚实都会越来越激怒这片土地上的妇女，而当那些如此拖延妇女选举权的政党最终放任不管时，他们的诚意将受到怀疑，他们对新选民的呼吁也将受到怀疑

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，我不相信我们有意愿、决心、持久力来完成这项工作，而不需要更多的东西--这就是我们道德想象力的不断扩展；坚持认为我们都有一些不可简化的东西

vocabulary.definition: adv. 狂热地, 盲信地
vocabulary.context: "Mamma, Mamma, Mamma!" And the sister, sinking down on the floor, striking the wood with her forehead fanatically, twisting herself about and quivering like a person in an epileptic fit, groaned: "Jesus, Jesus - Mamma - Jesus!"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "妈妈，妈妈，妈妈！"妹妹倒在地板上，用额头疯狂地敲打木头，像癫痫发作的人一样扭动着身体，颤抖着，呻吟着："耶稣，耶稣--妈妈--耶稣！"

vocabulary.definition: n. 相面术, 面相, 外貌, 地貌；[医] 面容诊断, 面容, 面相
vocabulary.context: Her pale physiognomy was so composed now that she had passed away, so resigned, that one felt sure a sweet soul had dwelt in that body, that this serene grandmother had spent an untroubled existence, that this virtuous woman had ended her life without any shock, without any remorse.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她苍白的面容如此平静，现在她已经去世了，如此顺从，人们确信她的身体里住着一个甜蜜的灵魂，这位宁静的祖母度过了一个无忧无虑的生活，这位贤惠的女人没有任何震惊，没有任何悔恨地结束了自己的生命

vocabulary.definition: adj. 受风吹雨打的
vocabulary.context: Then the fit gradually subsided, and they now wept in a less violent fashion, like the rainy calm that follows a squall on a storm-beaten sea.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，痉挛逐渐平息，他们的哭泣不再那么剧烈，就像暴风雨过后的平静的大海

vocabulary.definition: n. 主桅支索, 支柱, 中流砥柱
vocabulary.context: She had been their mainstay, their guide, the best part of their youth, of that happy portion of their lives which had vanished; she had been the bond that united them to existence, the mother, the mamma, the creative flesh, the tie that bound them to their ancestors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她曾是他们的中流砥柱，他们的向导，是他们青春中最美好的一部分，是他们生命中已经消失的幸福时光。她是将他们与存在联系在一起的纽带，母亲，母亲，富有创造力的肉体，是将他们与祖先联系在一起的纽带

vocabulary.definition: adj. 做错事的；[法] 做错事的, 有罪过的, 走入歧途的
vocabulary.context: He, the son, had become a magistrate and, wielding the weapon of the law, struck down without pity the feeble and the erring.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的儿子成为了一名地方法官，挥舞着法律的武器，毫不怜悯地击杀了软弱和犯错的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 朗诵, 语调, 唱；[化] 声调
vocabulary.context: They recalled circumstances, words, smiles, certain intonations of voice which belonged to one whom they should never hear speaking to them again.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们回忆起当时的情景、言语、微笑和某些语调，而这些都是他们再也不会听到的人的说话声

vocabulary.definition: n. 纪念物, 令人回忆的东西
vocabulary.context: At the opposite side of the prostrate body the other hand seemed still to grasp the rumpled sheet with that wondering movement which is called the fold of the dying, and the lines had retained little creases as a memento of those last motions which precede the eternal motionlessness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在倒下的身体的另一侧，另一只手似乎仍然用一种令人惊奇的动作抓住了皱巴巴的床单，这种动作被称为垂死的折叠，并且线条保留了小折痕，作为永恒静止之前的最后动作的纪念品

vocabulary.definition: adv. 心烦意乱地
vocabulary.context: On his knees beside the bed, her son, a magistrate of inflexible principles, and her daughter Marguerite - in religion, Sister Eulalie - were weeping distractedly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的儿子，一位原则顽固的法官，和她的女儿玛格丽特--宗教上的尤拉莉修女--跪在床边，心不在焉地哭泣

vocabulary.definition: n. motionless（不动的, 静止的）的变形
vocabulary.context: At the opposite side of the prostrate body the other hand seemed still to grasp the rumpled sheet with that wondering movement which is called the fold of the dying, and the lines had retained little creases as a memento of those last motions which precede the eternal motionlessness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在倒下的身体的另一侧，另一只手似乎仍然用一种令人惊奇的动作抓住了皱巴巴的床单，这种动作被称为垂死的折叠，并且线条保留了小折痕，作为永恒静止之前的最后动作的纪念品

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无痛苦地, 不费力地
vocabulary.context: She had died painlessly, tranquilly, like a woman whose life was irreproachable, and she now lay on her back in bed, with closed eyes, calm features, her long white hair carefully arranged, as if she had again made her toilet ten minutes before her death.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她死时无痛、平静，就像一个生命无可挑剔的女人，现在她仰面躺在床上，闭着眼睛，面容平静，长长的白发精心梳理，仿佛死前十分钟又上过一次厕所

vocabulary.definition: adj. 厌恶人类的, 不愿与人来往的
vocabulary.context: The water-plug being left in solitude, its overflowings sullenly congealed, and turned to misanthropic ice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 水塞被遗弃，溢出的水阴沉地凝结，变成了厌世的冰

vocabulary.definition: adv. 恳求地, 哀求地
vocabulary.context: "Jacob," he said, imploringly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "雅各布，"他恳求地说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 沉默寡言的, 自足的, 完备的；[化] 自备的
vocabulary.context: Hard and sharp as flint, from which no steel had ever struck out generous fire; secret, and self-contained, and solitary as an oyster.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 像燧石一样坚硬而锋利，没有任何钢可以从其中打出猛烈的火焰；秘密，独立，像牡蛎一样孤独

vocabulary.definition: n. 磨石, 砂轮, 石磨；[机] 磨石
vocabulary.context: But he was a tight-fisted hand at the grindstone, Scrooge!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但他是一个铁石心肠的人，斯克鲁奇！

vocabulary.definition: n. 暗示, 提示, 通知；[法] 告知, 通知, 暗示
vocabulary.context: It was the voice of Scrooge's nephew, who came upon him so quickly that this was the first intimation he had of his approach.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是斯克鲁奇侄子的声音，他很快就来到了他身边，这是他第一次感觉到他的到来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 东的, 向东的, 从东的；adv. 向东
vocabulary.context: ...that Hamlet's Father died before the play began, there would be nothing more remarkable in his taking a stroll at night, in an easterly wind, upon his own ramparts, than there would be in any other middle-aged gentleman rashly turning out after dark in a breezy spot - say Saint Paul's Churchyard for instance - literally to astonish his...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...哈姆雷特的父亲在戏剧开始前就去世了，他在夜里乘着东风在自己的城墙上散步，没有什么比任何其他中年绅士在天黑后鲁莽地出现在一个有微风的地方--例如圣保罗教堂墓地--真正让他的惊讶更引人注目的了......

vocabulary.definition: n. 五金器件
vocabulary.context: I might have been inclined, myself, to regard a coffin-nail as the deadest piece of ironmongery in the trade.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我自己可能倾向于将棺材钉视为行业中最致命的五金制品

vocabulary.definition: n. 遗产受赠人；[经] 遗产承受人, 受遗赠者
vocabulary.context: Scrooge was his sole executor, his sole administrator, his sole assign, his sole residuary legatee, his sole friend, and sole mourner.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯克鲁奇是他唯一的遗嘱执行人、他唯一的管理人、他唯一的受让人、他唯一的剩余受遗赠人、他唯一的朋友和唯一的哀悼者

vocabulary.definition: adv. 肃静地, 沉默地
vocabulary.context: With an ill-will Scrooge dismounted from his stool, and tacitly admitted the fact to the expectant clerk in the Tank, who instantly snuffed his candle out, and put on his hat.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯克罗吉怀着恶意从凳子上下来，心照不宣地向坦克里等候的职员承认了这一事实，后者立即熄灭了蜡烛，戴上了帽子

vocabulary.definition: n. 宽面；adv. 横着
vocabulary.context: You may talk vaguely about driving a coach-and-six up a good old flight of stairs, or through a bad young Act of Parliament; but I mean to say you might have got a hearse up that staircase, and taken it broadwise, with the splinter-bar towards the wall and the door towards the balustrades: and done it easy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你可能会含糊地谈论驾驶一辆六人座客车爬上一段古老的楼梯，或者通过一项糟糕的新议会法案；但我的意思是说，你可能已经有一辆灵车上楼梯，并宽阔地行驶，碎片杆朝向墙壁，门朝向栏杆：而且很容易做到

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自我控制的
vocabulary.context: "They get more self-controlled."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "他们变得更加自控。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 表演不充分, 扣着大牌不出而出小牌, 对...轻描淡写；v.i. 表演角色不充分；n. 秘密行动, 不充分的表演
vocabulary.context: Prone to focus on and overestimate short-term payoffs and underplay longer-term consequences of what they do.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 倾向于关注并高估短期回报，而低估其行为的长期后果

vocabulary.definition: n. 信任, 祭器台, 供桌, 凭证；[法] 设计人, 创立人, 创设人
vocabulary.context: As for peer pressure, the new brain research "gives credence to the idea that this isn't a choice that kids are making to give in to their friends, that biologically, they're more vulnerable to that," he said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 至于同伴压力，新的大脑研究"证实了这样一种观点，即这不是孩子们向朋友屈服的选择，从生物学角度来说，他们更容易受到这种影响，"他说

vocabulary.definition: n. 加速剂, 加速器, 油门；[化] 加速器; 早强剂; 促进剂; 硫化促进剂
vocabulary.context: The teenage brain, Laurence Steinberg says, is like a car with a good accelerator but a weak brake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 劳伦斯·斯坦伯格说，青少年的大脑就像一辆油门良好但刹车较弱的汽车

vocabulary.definition: adj. 考虑不充分的, 欠考虑的, 不妥当的
vocabulary.context: "...These qualities often result in impetuous and ill-considered actions and decisions."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "......这些品质常常导致鲁莽和考虑不周的行动和决定。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 评价过高, 过高估价；n. 估计过高, 评价过高
vocabulary.context: Prone to focus on and overestimate short-term payoffs and underplay longer-term consequences of what they do.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 倾向于关注并高估短期回报，而低估其行为的长期后果

vocabulary.definition: v. (使)发展不完全, (使)显影不足
vocabulary.context: "As any parent knows," wrote Justice Anthony Kennedy for the 5-4 majority, youths are more likely to show "a lack of maturity and an underdeveloped sense of responsibility" than adults.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "正如所有家长都知道的那样，"法官安东尼·肯尼迪以 5 比 4 的多数票写道，青少年比成年人更有可能表现出"缺乏成熟度和不发达的责任感"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 冲动的, 猛烈的, 轻率的, 狂暴的
vocabulary.context: "...These qualities often result in impetuous and ill-considered actions and decisions."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "......这些品质常常导致鲁莽和考虑不周的行动和决定。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 报应, 惩罚, 报酬；[法] 惩罚, 报应, 报答
vocabulary.context: In any case, the decision for each defendant should balance a number of reasons for punishment, like retribution, protecting society, deterring future crime, and rehabilitation, said Ash, who's a member of the American Psychiatric Association's Committee on Judicial Action.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国精神病学协会司法行动委员会成员阿什说，无论如何，对每个被告的决定都应该平衡多种惩罚原因，例如报复、保护社会、阻止未来犯罪和康复

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不能赎回的；[经] 不能偿还的, 不能兑现的
vocabulary.context: For me, these words capture the irredeemable loss that young people must endure and the daunting challenge that they must brave as they depart from childhood and face the great unknowns of growing up.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对我来说，这些话抓住了年轻人必须承受的不可挽回的损失，以及他们在告别童年、面对成长过程中的巨大未知时必须勇敢面对的艰巨挑战

vocabulary.definition: n. 不满, 不平, 背叛；[法] 挑唆背判, 离判, 不满
vocabulary.context: Now they are set adrift in a sea of disaffection.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在他们在不满的海洋中漂流

vocabulary.definition: adj. 敌手的,对手的,对抗(性)的
vocabulary.context: Suddenly (it seems to parents) their little darling has been replaced by a more adversarial adolescent who doesn't act like he or she misses them the way they miss the child.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 突然间（在父母看来）他们的小宝贝被一个更具敌意的青少年所取代，他或她的表现不像他们想念孩子那样想念他们

vocabulary.definition: n. 吊秋千
vocabulary.context: At this transition into adolescence the young person can feel in danger of having nothing stable to hold onto, caught between the trapeze of childhood that has been let go and the trapeze of growing up not yet within his or her grasp.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在进入青春期的这个过渡阶段，年轻人可能会感到没有任何稳定的东西可以抓住的危险，夹在已被放开的童年的空中飞人和尚未掌握的成长的空中飞人之间

vocabulary.definition: n. 磨擦, 磨损, 磨损处；[化] 磨耗; 磨蚀
vocabulary.context: As loss of the old relationship creates more distance and abrasion between them, the break with childhood is made.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着旧关系的丧失，他们之间的距离和摩擦越来越大，与童年的决裂也随之而来

vocabulary.definition: n. 无感情, 无兴趣, 冷漠；[医] 无情[感], 淡漠
vocabulary.context: Now early adolescent apathy causes the separation from childhood to begin as young people start caring less about what used to matter most.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在，青少年早期的冷漠导致年轻人开始不再关心过去最重要的事情，从而开始与童年分离

vocabulary.definition: n. 不赞成
vocabulary.context: Now they have a more aloof early adolescent who is more reluctant to be touched, who would rather spend time with friends, who feels too old to play with parents, who is embarrassed by their public company, who is more private and less forthcoming, and who seems to court their disapproval through deliberate resistance and opposition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在，他们有一个更加冷漠的早期青少年，更不愿意被触摸，宁愿花时间和朋友在一起，觉得自己太老了，不能和父母一起玩，因为上市公司而感到尴尬，更加注重隐私，不那么乐于助人，似乎通过故意抵抗和反对来博得他们的反对

vocabulary.definition: n. 抑制, 克制, 束缚；[医] 约束, 拘束
vocabulary.context: Now the young person anticipates more freedom from parental restraint and more freedom for new experiences.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在，年轻人期望有更多的自由来摆脱父母的束缚，并有更多的自由来体验新的经历

vocabulary.definition: n. 民意调查人, 民意测验经办人
vocabulary.context: That faith is faltering, especially among the poor, says pollster Dimock.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 民意调查专家迪莫克表示，这种信念正在动摇，尤其是在穷人中

vocabulary.definition: n. 喇叭边(或号角)嘹亮吹奏声, 吹牛
vocabulary.context: Composer Aaron Copland, a gay Jewish son of immigrants, captured the expansive optimism of the American Dream in 1942, in his "Fanfare for the Common Man."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作曲家亚伦·科普兰 (Aaron Copland) 是一位犹太移民的同性恋儿子，他在 1942 年的《普通人的号角》中捕捉到了美国梦的广阔乐观主义

vocabulary.definition: n. 退休人员, 退休者；[法] 退休者, 退职者
vocabulary.context: "I think the American Dream for the average man doesn't exist any more," retiree Linden Strandberg says on a recent visit to the Smithsonian American History museum in Washington, D.C.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我认为普通人的美国梦已不复存在，"退休人员林登·斯特兰德伯格最近参观华盛顿特区的史密森尼美国历史博物馆时说道

vocabulary.definition: n. 组成者, 构成者, 筹划者；[电] 调帧器
vocabulary.context: "And the framers answered that question by saying it's a rising sun."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "制宪者回答了这个问题，说这是旭日东升。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 主题, 基调, 动机, 基本图案；[法] 主旨, 主题, 动机
vocabulary.context: The American motifs of growth and optimism even stretch back as far as the Constitutional Convention.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国的增长和乐观主义主题甚至可以追溯到制宪会议

vocabulary.definition: adj. 扩张性的, 使扩大的, 广阔的；[化] 膨胀的; 可以膨胀的
vocabulary.context: Composer Aaron Copland, a gay Jewish son of immigrants, captured the expansive optimism of the American Dream in 1942, in his "Fanfare for the Common Man."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作曲家亚伦·科普兰 (Aaron Copland) 是一位犹太移民的同性恋儿子，他在 1942 年的《普通人的号角》中捕捉到了美国梦的广阔乐观主义

vocabulary.definition: adv. 显著地, 惊人地, 引人注目地
vocabulary.context: "If you go back to the Great Depression where the American Dream originated as a concept, strikingly enough, there was still hope and optimism about the future," White says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果你回到美国梦作为一个概念起源的大萧条时期，令人惊讶的是，人们对未来仍然充满希望和乐观，"怀特说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 乐观的, 乐观主义的, 乐天的
vocabulary.context: Higher income blacks are pretty optimistic about the American Dream, as are higher income whites."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 高收入黑人对美国梦非常乐观，高收入白人也是如此。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 教父或教母
vocabulary.context: The celebration typically begins with a mass, attended by family and godparents, followed by a party with extended family and friends.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 庆祝活动通常以由家人和教父母参加的弥撒开始，然后是由家人和朋友参加的聚会

vocabulary.definition: n. 脚后跟, 踵, 后部, 倾侧；v.t. 尾随, 装以鞋跟, 倾侧, 追赶；v.i. 紧随, 用脚后跟传球
vocabulary.context: Other features include the celebrant giving away a childhood doll to a younger sister, or the placement of heeled shoes on her feet.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他特色包括主持者将童年玩偶赠送给妹妹，或将高跟鞋放在她的脚上

vocabulary.definition: n. 女子成年身分, 女子成年期, 女子成年资格, 女子气质, 女子特性
vocabulary.context: It translates from Spanish as "fifteen years" and is seen as an important celebration marking a girl's passage to womanhood.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它在西班牙语中翻译为"十五年"，被视为标志着女孩成年的重要庆祝活动

vocabulary.definition: n. 冕状头饰
vocabulary.context: Many celebrations, mostly for young girls celebrating their sweet sixteen, include rituals such as the placement of a tiara on the birthday girl's head, a candle lighting ceremony, replacing the girl's slippers with heeled shoes, which is reminiscent of the Quinceañera, and a father-daughter dance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多庆祝活动，主要是为年轻女孩庆祝她们甜蜜的十六岁，包括一些仪式，如在生日女孩头上戴上皇冠、点燃蜡烛仪式、用高跟鞋代替女孩的拖鞋（这让人想起成人礼）以及父女舞蹈

vocabulary.definition: adj. 回忆的, 怀旧的；n. 往事叙述者, 回忆录作者
vocabulary.context: Many celebrations, mostly for young girls celebrating their sweet sixteen, include rituals such as the placement of a tiara on the birthday girl's head, a candle lighting ceremony, replacing the girl's slippers with heeled shoes, which is reminiscent of the Quinceañera, and a father-daughter dance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多庆祝活动，主要是为年轻女孩庆祝她们甜蜜的十六岁，包括一些仪式，如在生日女孩头上戴上皇冠、点燃蜡烛仪式、用高跟鞋代替女孩的拖鞋（这让人想起成人礼）以及父女舞蹈

vocabulary.definition: n. 宗派, 教派
vocabulary.context: While the Jewish faith has many different sects, boys usually become a bar mitzvah at the age of 13, and girls typically become a bat mitzvah at the age of 12 or 13, depending on the sect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然犹太信仰有许多不同的教派，但男孩通常在 13 岁时接受成人礼，女孩通常在 12 或 13 岁时接受成人礼，具体取决于教派

vocabulary.definition: adj. 正统的, 传统的, 惯常的
vocabulary.context: It is an important ceremony marking the confirmed's commitment to their faith in the Catholic, Eastern Orthodox, Episcopal and Anglican churches, as well as in several Protestant denominations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是一个重要的仪式，标志着确认者对天主教、东正教、圣公会和英国圣公会以及几个新教教派的信仰的承诺

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 举起, 提拔, 鼓舞
vocabulary.context: Some prefer to have a simple birthday party of elevated importance with friends and family instead.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有些人更喜欢与朋友和家人举办一个简单的、重要的生日聚会

vocabulary.definition: n. 民族同化论
vocabulary.context: In A Raisin In The Sun for the first time on the stage: an African student, Asagai, articulates his vision of the coming liberation of Africa and the revolutionary future; the natural hair style and African dress appear in a ghetto setting; the young daughter, Beneatha, attacks the concept of "assimilationism...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在《阳光下的葡萄干》中，非洲学生浅井首次登上舞台，阐述了他对即将到来的非洲解放和革命未来的愿景；自然的发型和非洲服饰出现在贫民窟的环境中；年幼的女儿贝内莎 (Beneatha) 攻击"同化主义"的概念......

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 剥夺...的继承权；[法] 剥夺继承权
vocabulary.context: Lorraine Hansberry, participating both as a dramatist and as a leader in the historic liberation efforts of our time, left a legacy of commitment to the struggles of the disinherited and oppressed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 洛林·汉斯伯里作为剧作家和领导者参与了我们这个时代的历史性解放努力，留下了致力于被剥夺继承权和被压迫者斗争的遗产

vocabulary.definition: n. 利用；[医] 利用
vocabulary.context: These were the themes of Black identity, Black power, and liberation - from the mass symbolic utilization of the "Black is Beautiful" slogan with its emphasis on images of Blackness reflecting unmistakable African ancestry, to the assertion of growing links with Black Africa - the passionate belief in which...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些是黑人身份、黑人权力和解放的主题--从"黑人是美丽的"口号的大规模象征性运用，强调反映明确无误的非洲血统的黑人形象，到与黑人非洲日益增长的联系的断言--热情的信念......

vocabulary.definition: adv. 显而易见地; 不会弄错地; 不会误解地; 明白地
vocabulary.context: Nor did it solve the problems of a theatre racist in vital respects - but the mere fact a cast could be assembled in one play proclaimed unmistakably the depths and of additional Black talent waiting forth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它也没有在重要方面解决戏剧种族主义的问题--但单是演员阵容可以聚集在一部戏剧中这一事实就明确地宣告了等待的黑人人才的深度和更多

vocabulary.definition: n. 全体, 合唱曲, (女人的)全套服装；[化] 总体; 整体; 系综
vocabulary.context: ...Glynn Turman - and, he even in the smallest roles, Douglas Turner Ward, distinguished founder and Artistic Director of the Negro Ensemble Company, and Lonne Elder III, author of Ceremonies In Dark Old Men .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...Glynn Turman - 而且，他甚至扮演最小的角色，Douglas Turner Ward，黑人合奏团的杰出创始人和艺术总监，以及Lonne Elder III，《黑暗老人的仪式》的作者

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 十字架上钉死
vocabulary.context: Sacred heart of the crucified Jesus, take away our hearts o' stone and give us hearts of flesh!"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 被钉在十字架上的耶稣的圣心，拿走我们的石心，给我们肉心吧！"

vocabulary.definition: adv. 完全, 分明地, 赤裸裸地
vocabulary.context: These words, spoken by Lorraine Hansberry before a conference of young Black writers at the very beginning of her career starkly illustrate the circumstances of the playwright's childhood in the 1930s.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些话是洛林·汉斯伯里 (Lorraine Hansberry) 在其职业生涯初期在一次年轻黑人作家会议上说的，赤裸裸地说明了这位剧作家 20 世纪 30 年代的童年环境

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 注入, 使充满, 泡制, 鼓舞；v.i. 泡
vocabulary.context: ...reflecting unmistakable African ancestry, to the assertion of growing links with Black Africa - the passionate belief in which infused all the writings of Lorraine Hansberry, from her first play, set in Chicago, to her last, set in Africa.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...反映了明显的非洲血统，以及与黑非洲日益增长的联系的断言--洛林·汉斯伯里的所有作品都充满了这种热情的信念，从她以芝加哥为背景的第一部戏剧，到以非洲为背景的最后一部戏剧

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 回响, 反响, 被反射；v.t. 使反响, 使回响, 反射；adj. 回响的, 反射的
vocabulary.context: And all the while the confluence of the Black experience and that Irish wail she had heard "in a different key" continued to reverberate within.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黑人的经历和她"以不同的调"听到的爱尔兰哀号的融合一直在内心持续回响

vocabulary.definition: n. 迅速, 快
vocabulary.context: Etched within its seemingly simple tale of a Black family's refusal to sacrifice human dignity to the demands of a racist society, were multiple themes and levels of the Black revolution in consciousness that was to erupt with stunning swiftness in the 1960s, although most American theater-goers were unprepared to recognize this at the time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个黑人家庭拒绝为了满足种族主义社会的要求而牺牲人类尊严的看似简单的故事中，铭刻着黑人意识革命的多个主题和层面，这场革命在 20 世纪 60 年代以惊人的速度爆发，尽管当时大多数美国戏剧观众还没有准备好认识到这一点

vocabulary.definition: adj. 光电的, 灯光摄影装置的；[电] 光电的
vocabulary.context: For example: the photoelectric effect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如：光电效应

vocabulary.definition: adv. 怪异地
vocabulary.context: Einstein was clearly intelligent, but not outlandishly more so than his peers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 爱因斯坦显然很聪明，但并不比他的同辈聪明得多

vocabulary.definition: n. 智能, 智囊
vocabulary.context: Einstein's brainpower was not limitless.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 爱因斯坦的脑力并不是无限的

vocabulary.definition: n. [物]黑体(能全部吸收辐射能的物体)
vocabulary.context: Other physicists like Max Planck (working on a related problem: blackbody radiation), more senior and experienced than Einstein, were closing in on the answer, but Einstein got there first.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他物理学家，如马克斯·普朗克（研究相关问题：黑体辐射），比爱因斯坦更资深、更有经验，正在接近答案，但爱因斯坦首先找到了答案

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不敬的, 不逊的, 无礼的
vocabulary.context: What's remarkable about 1905 is that a single person authored all five papers, plus the original, irreverent way Einstein came to his conclusions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1905 年的非凡之处在于，所有五篇论文都是由一个人撰写的，而且爱因斯坦得出结论的方式也是原创的、不敬的

vocabulary.definition: n. 靠背
vocabulary.context: Einstein's sister, Maja, recalled "even when there was a lot of noise, he could lie down on the sofa, pick up a pen and paper, precariously balance an inkwell on the backrest and engross himself in a problem so much that the background noise stimulated rather than disturbed him."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 爱因斯坦的妹妹玛雅回忆道，"即使有很大的噪音，他也可以躺在沙发上，拿起笔和纸，摇摇晃晃地平衡靠背上的墨水瓶，全神贯注于一个问题，以至于背景噪音刺激而不是打扰他。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 周壁的, 体壁的, 顶骨的, 侧膜的；n. 顶骨
vocabulary.context: In 1999, neuroscientist Sandra Witelson reported that Einstein's inferior parietal lobe, and area related to mathematical reasoning, was 15% wider than normal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1999年，神经科学家桑德拉·维特尔森报告说，爱因斯坦的顶下叶以及与数学推理相关的区域比正常人宽15%

vocabulary.definition: adj. 负有义务的, 受惠的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他并不受制于论文导师或任何其他权威人物。"他的思想可以自由地漫游

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 爱因斯坦的妹妹玛雅回忆道，"即使有很大的噪音，他也可以躺在沙发上，拿起笔和纸，摇摇晃晃地平衡靠背上的墨水瓶，全神贯注于一个问题，以至于背景噪音刺激而不是打扰他。"

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 雪还是像以前一样厚，当然我们看不到前面十五步；但在我们周围，雪床的白色光芒使我们能够辨认出由覆盖的鼠尾草灌木丛形成的光滑的糖面包丘，就在我们面前，有两条微弱的凹槽，我们知道它们是逐渐填充和慢慢消失的轮迹

vocabulary.context: While we were considering what to do, the young Swede landed from the canoe and took his pedestrian way Carson-wards, singing his same tiresome song about his "sister and his brother" and "the child in the grave with its mother," and in a short minute faded and disappeared in the white oblivion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我们考虑要做什么时，年轻的瑞典人从独木舟上岸，步行前往卡森沃德，唱着同样令人厌烦的歌曲，内容是他的"妹妹和他的兄弟"以及"坟墓里的孩子和他的母亲"，然后在短短的一分钟内消失在白色的遗忘中

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中，英国利物浦大学的地质年代学家 Courtney Sprain 和同事三次前往印度西高止山脉，这里是德干地盾最厚的熔岩沉积物的所在地

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 普林斯顿大学的格尔塔·凯勒（Gerta Keller）等科学家就他们在消灭地球上 60% 的动植物物种（包括大多数恐龙）方面发挥了多大作用展开了激烈的争论

vocabulary.context: This phenomenon is often referred to as "fake news." The placement of "fake news" on the internet can be haphazard or unplanned, while propaganda is typically part of a larger strategy by its creator.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种现象通常被称为"假新闻"。在互联网上发布"假新闻"可能是随意或无计划的，而宣传通常是其创建者更大战略的一部分

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 过去，世界许多不同地区的政府常常在战争时期利用从报纸文章到电影预告等各种手段来进行宣传

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 散播, 传播, 宣传；v.i. 广为传播
vocabulary.context: Anyone who intentionally spreads misinformation for political purposes is disseminating propaganda.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 任何出于政治目的故意传播错误信息的人都是在传播宣传

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 两国都试图让本国公民相信对方的邪恶和虚伪

vocabulary.definition: num. 第二十, 二十分之一

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管宣传在历史上一直是一个中性术语，但在二十世纪它变得与操纵更加相关

vocabulary.definition: n. 独立
vocabulary.context: There isn't a clear-cut division between a complex plot and a simple one, but in general, simple plots exist in shows where every episode begins as if the one before it hasn't happened (known as stand-alone episodes).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 复杂情节和简单情节之间没有明确的划分，但一般来说，简单情节存在于节目中，每一集开始时就好像前一集没有发生一样（称为独立剧集）

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 翻新胎面；n. 翻新的轮胎

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 俱乐部每天回顾电视节目，Reddit 和 Tumblr 等热门网站上充斥着有关当前电视节目的对话

vocabulary.definition: adv. 典型地, 标准地
vocabulary.context: Unless students begin to study television in school, some younger Americans may never see the TV shows that other cultures find quintessentially American.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除非学生开始在学校学习电视，否则一些年轻的美国人可能永远看不到其他文化认为典型的美国电视节目

vocabulary.definition: adj. 轮廓清晰的, 明确的, 清楚的；[法] 清晰的, 明确的, 轮廓清楚的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 复杂情节和简单情节之间没有明确的划分，但一般来说，简单情节存在于节目中，每一集开始时就好像前一集没有发生一样（称为独立剧集）

vocabulary.definition: abbr. 情景喜剧（situation comedy）
vocabulary.context: Both sitcoms have been dubbed and translated around the world, making the characters and their lives synonymous with the American experience to people everywhere.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这两部情景喜剧都在世界各地进行了配音和翻译，使角色及其生活成为世界各地人们美国经历的代名词

vocabulary.definition: n. 电影院, 电影
vocabulary.context: But whereas plays and cinema are visual, they do not tend to be as graphic as golden era TV shows.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，虽然戏剧和电影是视觉的，但它们往往不像黄金时代的电视节目那样生动

vocabulary.definition: adv. 明显地；公开地, 公然地
vocabulary.context: Propriety and censorship laws prevented Shakespeare from showing overtly visual elements related to his mature themes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 版权和审查法阻止莎士比亚展示与其成熟主题相关的公开视觉元素

vocabulary.definition: n. 礼貌, 规矩, 适当；[计] 专有权
vocabulary.context: Propriety and censorship laws prevented Shakespeare from showing overtly visual elements related to his mature themes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 版权和审查法阻止莎士比亚展示与其成熟主题相关的公开视觉元素

vocabulary.definition: n. 违反者, 犯规者, 罪人；[法] 违反者, 违法者, 罪人
vocabulary.context: Although that result is not particularly surprising (who doesn't like money?), participants also felt that this result was enough to right the wrong: they generally did not decide to also punish the transgressor - even when it was easy to do.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然这个结果并不特别令人惊讶（谁不喜欢钱？），但参与者也认为这个结果足以纠正错误：他们通常不会决定也惩罚违规者 - 即使这很容易做到

vocabulary.definition: n. 公平, 无私, 不偏；[法] 公正无私, 公平, 公正
vocabulary.context: This finding calls into question our reliance on the punitive impartiality of juries and judges.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一发现使我们对陪审团和法官的惩罚性公正性的依赖产生了质疑

vocabulary.definition: adj. 和解的, 调停的, 调和的
vocabulary.context: Though less often employed, reconciliatory nonpunitive approaches toward restoring justice have proved successful in the real world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然不太常用，但事实证明，恢复正义的和解非惩罚性方法在现实世界中是成功的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 报应的
vocabulary.context: More often than not, the third parties chose the most retributive option, where the victim was monetarily compensated and the transgressor was punished by having his or her payout decreased.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第三方往往会选择最具报复性的选择，即对受害者进行金钱补偿，而违法者则受到减少其赔偿的惩罚

vocabulary.definition: n. 做坏事的人
vocabulary.context: In another series of games, we asked participants to act like juries - neutral third parties doling out punishment to the wrongdoer and compensation to the afflicted.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在另一系列游戏中，我们要求参与者像陪审团一样行事--中立的第三方对违法者进行惩罚，并对受影响的人进行赔偿

vocabulary.definition: adv. 友善地
vocabulary.context: Recent research from our laboratory suggests that tactics such as compensating the victim have significant advantages in their ability to amicably reconcile differences.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们实验室的最新研究表明，补偿受害者等策略在友好调和分歧的能力方面具有显着优势

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重新分配
vocabulary.context: After observing this unfair treatment, participants had to decide how to reapportion the money between the individual who had divided the money and the person who received an unjust portion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在观察到这种不公平待遇后，参与者必须决定如何在分配金钱的人和收到不公正份额的人之间重新分配金钱

vocabulary.definition: n. 支出, 付出款项；[经] 付出款项, 花费, 支出
vocabulary.context: One option for restoring justice was to punish the culprit for unfair treatment by reducing that individual's monetary payout - a response commonly observed both in the lab and in the real world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 恢复正义的一个选择是通过减少个人的金钱支出来惩罚不公平待遇的罪魁祸首--这种反应在实验室和现实世界中都很常见

vocabulary.definition: n. 概要, 缩影, 象征
vocabulary.context: Such a cycle would be the epitome of the ancient "eye for an eye" notion of justice, in which punishment is commensurate with the crime, an approach that still underlies most modern legal systems, including that of the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这样的循环将是古代"以眼还眼"正义观念的缩影，即惩罚与犯罪相称，这种方法仍然是大多数现代法律体系（包括美国的法律体系）的基础

vocabulary.definition: adj. 连续四年的, 每四年一次的；n. 第四周年
vocabulary.context: What do these two failures say about our quadrennial discontent?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这两次失败说明了我们四年一度的不满情绪是什么？

vocabulary.definition: n. （政党决定政策或推举竞选人的）核心成员( caucus的名词复数 ); 决策干部; 决策委员会; 秘密会议
vocabulary.context: Democrats, in addition to primaries, also have caucuses, which are conducted by the party and (like the primaries) can be open, closed, semi-open, or semi-closed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 民主党除了初选外，还有党团会议，由党内组织，（与初选一样）可以是开放式、封闭式、半开放式或半封闭式

vocabulary.definition: n. 生存能力, 发育能力；[医] [生]活[能]力, 生机
vocabulary.context: In 1965, Congress passed the historic Voting Rights Act, which protected the viability of the 15th Amendment by guaranteeing that the federal government would intervene if any state would attempt to deny a citizen his or her voting rights on the basis of race.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1965年，国会通过了历史性的投票权法案，该法案保证如果任何州试图基于种族剥夺公民的投票权，联邦政府将进行干预，从而保护了第十五修正案的可行性

vocabulary.definition: n. 领导人会议, 核心会议；v.i. 开核心会议
vocabulary.context: The caucus rules trump the entire system.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 核心小组的规则高于整个体系

vocabulary.definition: n. 前总统

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在福布斯的一篇文章中，包容性创意共享平台 Lips 的创始人 Annie Brown 解释了针对某些群体的算法偏见如何导致一种被称为"影子禁令"的现象

vocabulary.definition: n. 乐观者, 乐观主义者

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 托马斯·查莫罗-普雷穆齐克 (Tomas Chamorro-Premuzic) 是一位乐观主义者，他是一位心理学教授，也是《我，人类：人工智能、自动化和重新夺回使我们独特的东西》一书的作者

vocabulary.context: Why more teens are calling it quits
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为什么越来越多的青少年选择戒烟

vocabulary.contextTranslation: Facebook、Twitter、Instagram、TikTok 和 Snapchat 等平台使我们能够即时与比以往更多的人建立联系

vocabulary.definition: adj. 开发的, 剥削的, (糟蹋地)利用的
vocabulary.context: Not only is social media linked with negative effects on mental and physical health, according to Hoplamazian, some young people connect social media to feeling "disenfranchised with the world in general." These teens see social media companies as exploitative and dishonest.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 霍普拉马齐安认为，社交媒体不仅与身心健康产生负面影响有关，一些年轻人还将社交媒体与"被剥夺了与整个世界的权利"联系在一起。这些青少年认为社交媒体公司具有剥削性且不诚实

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不脸红的, 不害羞的, 脸皮厚的
vocabulary.context: "It's more that instead of this unabashed trend upward, [social media use has] flattened for teens or [is] going down slightly." Data from Pew supports this, showing that Facebook use by teens ages 13-17 dropped from 71% in 2014-2015 to just 31% in 2022.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "更重要的是，青少年的[社交媒体使用]并没有出现这种毫不掩饰的上升趋势，而是趋于平缓或略有下降。"皮尤研究中心的数据支持了这一点，显示 13-17 岁青少年的 Facebook 使用率从 2014-2015 年的 71% 下降到 2022 年的 31%

vocabulary.definition: adj. 聪明的, 有知识的
vocabulary.context: Paraphrasing what he has heard, Hoplamazian says some teens feel like in the eyes of social media companies, "I'm a commodity and they're using my data for free and I just don't want to participate in that." He credits young people as "more knowledgeable and aware of being manipulated and used as data through social media."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Hoplamazian 解释说，他所听到的内容是，一些青少年在社交媒体公司眼中感觉"我是一种商品，他们免费使用我的数据，而我只是不想参与其中。"他认为年轻人"知识更丰富，更意识到自己被社交媒体操纵和用作数据"

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个伪装成 13 岁乌克兰男孩的计算机程序通过了所谓的图灵测试，达到了技术和哲学的门槛：它欺骗了三分之一的人类对话者，让他们相信他们正在与真人而不是机器交谈

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 绝对必要的是，每个学生，无论其学校的组成如何，都可以获得相同数量的资源，并对最适合他们需求的课程和教学法获得相同的关注

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，她在服用锂剂后的很多年里都表现得很好，最后，她被戒掉了锂剂，看看没有它会怎样，她又患上了另一种精神病，然后陷入了我所见过的最严重的抑郁症，她坐在父母的公寓里，或多或少有些紧张，基本上一动不动，日复一日

vocabulary.definition: n. 脆弱, 虚弱；[化] 易碎性
vocabulary.context: We live with an inbuilt fragility, having to do with the dark history of the genocide.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们生活在一种内在的脆弱之中，这与种族灭绝的黑暗历史有关

vocabulary.definition: adj. 内藏的；嵌入的；内置的
vocabulary.context: We live with an inbuilt fragility, having to do with the dark history of the genocide.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们生活在一种内在的脆弱之中，这与种族灭绝的黑暗历史有关

vocabulary.definition: n. 洗脑；v.t. 对人洗脑
vocabulary.context: Because had we gone the classic route of justice, we would have a broken country because of the numbers involved, because of the level of brainwashing and propaganda by the state that committed genocide.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因为如果我们走经典的正义之路，我们的国家将会因为所涉及的人数而破碎，因为实施种族灭绝的国家的洗脑和宣传程度

vocabulary.definition: n. 常态
vocabulary.context: And again, to balance all this, to bring sanity, to bring normalcy, to get our economic development going, to open up to the world, it's not an easy task.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 再说一遍，要平衡这一切，要保持理智，要恢复正常，要让我们的经济发展继续下去，要向世界开放，这不是一件容易的事

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不存在的
vocabulary.context: And I think also perhaps because the recovery of Rwanda and the advancement we've seen in the last 20 years is perhaps unprecedented for a country that was basically nonexistent 20 years ago.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我认为也许还因为卢旺达的复苏和我们在过去 20 年所看到的进步对于一个 20 年前基本上不存在的国家来说可能是前所未有的

vocabulary.definition: n. 神智健全, 头脑清楚, 通情达理；[医] 精神健全
vocabulary.context: And again, to balance all this, to bring sanity, to bring normalcy, to get our economic development going, to open up to the world, it's not an easy task.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 再说一遍，要平衡这一切，要保持理智，要恢复正常，要让我们的经济发展继续下去，要向世界开放，这不是一件容易的事

vocabulary.definition: n. 代表团, 派遣代表团, 代表的地位(或权力)；[经] 派遣, 委任, 委托
vocabulary.context: INSKEEP: You know, I believe you mentioned that just before we began this conversation, you were meeting a delegation from the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因斯基普：你知道，我相信你提到过，就在我们开始这次谈话之前，你正在会见一个来自美国的代表团

vocabulary.definition: adv. 政治上
vocabulary.context: But also that there has not been an opening of political space, that Rwanda is not nearly as politically open as many people might like it to be.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但政治空间尚未开放，卢旺达在政治上并不像许多人希望的那样开放

vocabulary.definition: n. 少数, 未成年, 少数民族；adj. 少数的, 属于少数派的
vocabulary.context: 20 years ago, nearly a million members of the Tutsi minority were slaughtered by extremist members from the Hutu majority.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 20年前，近百万图西族成员遭到胡图族极端分子的屠杀

vocabulary.definition: n. 金属加工术, 金属工
vocabulary.context: Aside from slaves, most Greek poleis would have had a number of free foreigners ( xenoi ) who had chosen to re-locate from other areas of Greece, the Mediterranean, and the Near East, bringing with them skills such as pottery and metalworking.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了奴隶之外，大多数希腊城邦还拥有一些自由外国人（xenoi），他们选择从希腊其他地区、地中海和近东搬迁，带来了陶器和金属加工等技能

vocabulary.definition: adv. 可论证地, 明确地, 凭论证
vocabulary.context: There were even intellectual arguments from philosophers like Aristotle, which propounded the belief that slaves were demonstrably inferior, a product of their environment and inherited characteristics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 亚里士多德等哲学家甚至提出了理智上的论点，认为奴隶显然是低等的，是他们的环境和遗传特征的产物

vocabulary.definition: n. 仲裁者, 调解者, 媒介物, 中间人；adj. 中间的, 媒介的
vocabulary.context: In certain periods such as war, helots were required to serve in the armed forces and, fighting well, they could even earn an escape from their lot and join the intermediary social groups which existed below the level of full-citizen and included such individuals as children with parents of mixed status (e.g.: father-citizen, mother- helot ).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在战争等特定时期，黑劳士被要求参军，如果战斗力出色，他们甚至可以逃脱命运，加入到完全公民水平以下的中间社会群体中，其中包括父母混合身份的儿童（例如：父亲与公民、母亲与黑劳士）

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重新安置；迁移；v.i. 重新安置；迁移新址；n. 浮动
vocabulary.context: Aside from slaves, most Greek poleis would have had a number of free foreigners ( xenoi ) who had chosen to re-locate from other areas of Greece, the Mediterranean, and the Near East, bringing with them skills such as pottery and metalworking.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了奴隶之外，大多数希腊城邦还拥有一些自由外国人（xenoi），他们选择从希腊其他地区、地中海和近东搬迁，带来了陶器和金属加工等技能

vocabulary.definition: n. 古希腊的高级妓女, 名妓,妾,交际花
vocabulary.context: The second, was the higher-class prostitute ( hetaira ).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第二个是高级妓女（hetaira）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 新近到达的,新近产生的
vocabulary.context: Engaged in manufacturing, trade, and commerce, these were the nouveau riche.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从事制造业、贸易和商业的人是暴发户

vocabulary.definition: n. 希洛人(古斯巴达奴隶), 奴隶, 农奴
vocabulary.context: The most famous example is the helot class of Sparta.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最著名的例子是斯巴达的黑劳士阶层

vocabulary.definition: n. 菲利亚；友情；友爱
vocabulary.context: Of course, love may have developed between the couple but the best that might be hoped for was philia - a general friendship/love sentiment; eros , the love of desire, was to be found elsewhere, at least for the male.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当然，夫妻之间可能已经发展出爱情，但最好的希望是 philia--一种普遍的友谊/爱情情感；爱欲，即对欲望的爱，可以在其他地方找到，至少对于男性来说是这样

vocabulary.definition: n. 暴发户
vocabulary.context: Engaged in manufacturing, trade, and commerce, these were the nouveau riche.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从事制造业、贸易和商业的人是暴发户

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不法的, 不正当的, 禁止的；[法] 违法的, 违禁的, 被禁止的
vocabulary.context: Women, children, immigrants (both Greek and foreign), laborers, and slaves all had defined roles, but there was interaction (often illicit) between the classes and there was also some movement between social groups, particularly for second generation offspring and during times of stress such as wars.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 妇女、儿童、移民（希腊和外国）、劳工和奴隶都有明确的角色，但阶级之间存在互动（通常是非法的），社会群体之间也存在一些流动，特别是对于第二代后代和在战争等压力时期

vocabulary.definition: v. 使迷失方向( disorientate的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: Montezuma-fearful, puzzled, dejected, and disorientated by these strange men-submitted.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 蒙特祖玛--对这些奇怪的人屈服了，感到恐惧、困惑、沮丧和迷失方向

vocabulary.definition: n. 占星家
vocabulary.context: Dismayed soothsayers and astrologers maintained they did not see it, and Montezuma cast them into cages where they starved to death.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 沮丧的占卜家和占星家坚称他们没有看到它，蒙特祖玛把他们扔进笼子里，让他们饿死

vocabulary.definition: n. 精明而讲实际的人
vocabulary.context: But Montezuma was mired in magic and superstition while Cortés was a hardheaded realist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但蒙特祖玛深陷魔法和迷信的泥潭，而科尔特斯则是一个顽固的现实主义者

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 订正, 矫正, 改正, 调正, 校正, 精馏, 整流；[化] 精馏
vocabulary.context: He quickly rectified matters.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他很快就纠正了问题

vocabulary.definition: n. 救助者( savior的复数形式 ); 救星; (S-)救世主; 上帝
vocabulary.context: In the Spanish, they saw their saviors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在西班牙语中，他们看到了自己的救世主

vocabulary.definition: n. 大帆船
vocabulary.context: By the time spies brought tales of mountains floating upon the sea (Spanish galleons), and men with "flesh very white...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到那时，间谍们带来了漂浮在海上的山脉（西班牙大帆船）的故事，以及"皮肤非常白......

vocabulary.definition: n. 预兆, 前兆, 征兆；v.t. 预示, 有...的前兆, 显出...的预兆
vocabulary.context: Long before Cortés landed at Vera Cruz on Good Friday, 1519, omens of doom appeared.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 早在 1519 年耶稣受难日科尔特斯登陆维拉克鲁斯之前，厄运的预兆就出现了

vocabulary.definition: n. 现实主义者, 写实主义者, 实在论者
vocabulary.context: But Montezuma was mired in magic and superstition while Cortés was a hardheaded realist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但蒙特祖玛深陷魔法和迷信的泥潭，而科尔特斯则是一个顽固的现实主义者

vocabulary.definition: n. 大错, 大失策；v.i. 失策, 跌跌撞撞地走, 犯错；v.t. 做错
vocabulary.context: Was this a political blunder or a shrewd tactical move?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是一个政治错误还是一个精明的战术举动？

vocabulary.definition: n. 任意, 武断, 专断, 反复无常
vocabulary.context: Even though most of us realize the arbitrariness of age, we still place a great deal of stock in it especially when we reach those infamous decade markers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管我们大多数人都意识到年龄的任意性，但我们仍然对它抱有很大的信心，尤其是当我们到达那些臭名昭著的十年标记时

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 乱穿马路
vocabulary.context: If you'd rather your neighbors didn't know your exact age, you better be sure not to get involved in a jaywalking accident or fender bender.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果您希望邻居不知道您的确切年龄，那么您最好确保不要卷入乱穿马路事故或发生车祸

vocabulary.definition: n. 扭弄物体的人, 六便士银币, 饮酒作乐
vocabulary.context: If you'd rather your neighbors didn't know your exact age, you better be sure not to get involved in a jaywalking accident or fender bender.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果您希望邻居不知道您的确切年龄，那么您最好确保不要卷入乱穿马路事故或发生车祸

vocabulary.definition: n. 尖锐, (疾病的)剧烈, 敏锐；[化] 锐度; 分辨能力
vocabulary.context: It's difficult to put a number on exactly how much you can expect your mental acuity to slow, particularly if you can't put your mind to the test in a computerized lab.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 很难准确地计算出你的思维敏锐度会下降多少，特别是如果你不能在计算机化实验室中测试你的思维的话

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自我实现的, 实现自己抱负的； （预言等）本身自然会实现的
vocabulary.context: We're so hyped by the media to blame minor memory problems on this dreaded illness that, if you're not careful, these occasional lapses become self-fulfilling prophecies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 媒体大肆宣传我们将这种可怕的疾病归咎于轻微的记忆问题，以至于如果你不小心，这些偶尔的失误就会变成自我实现的预言

vocabulary.definition: <废> n.忧郁, 疑病；v. 使忧郁
vocabulary.context: We're so hyped by the media to blame minor memory problems on this dreaded illness that, if you're not careful, these occasional lapses become self-fulfilling prophecies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 媒体大肆宣传我们将这种可怕的疾病归咎于轻微的记忆问题，以至于如果你不小心，这些偶尔的失误就会变成自我实现的预言

vocabulary.definition: adv. 实验式地, 在实验上
vocabulary.context: For one thing, it's not an "independent" variable because it can't be experimentally manipulated.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一方面，它不是一个"独立"变量，因为它无法通过实验操纵

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高龄的, 衰老的, 老年期的；[医] 老年的
vocabulary.context: Conversely, if you become convinced that you're getting "senile" (a terrible word!), you can accelerate your psychological aging clock.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，如果你确信自己正在"衰老"（一个可怕的词！），你的心理衰老时钟就会加速

vocabulary.definition: n. 防卫物, 挡泥板
vocabulary.context: If you'd rather your neighbors didn't know your exact age, you better be sure not to get involved in a jaywalking accident or fender bender.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果您希望邻居不知道您的确切年龄，那么您最好确保不要卷入乱穿马路事故或发生车祸

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 减去, 扣掉, 减少；v.i. 做减法；[计] 减
vocabulary.context: Being able to subtract a few years from this subjective age, or age that you feel, may actually buy you a few more objective years of healthy and productive life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 能够从这个主观年龄或你感觉的年龄中减去几年，实际上可能会给你带来几年更客观的健康和富有成效的生活

vocabulary.definition: adv. 有创造力地；率先的
vocabulary.context: Even though it's their first encounter, the two converse dynamically and inventively - an intense back-and-forth that equates love with religion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管这是他们的第一次见面，两人的对话充满活力且富有创意--激烈的来回对话将爱情与宗教等同起来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 胸襟狭窄的, 不大方的, 吝啬的
vocabulary.context: But they connect in such a powerful way - right away - that it's ungenerous to say that their religion of love is only silly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但他们以如此强大的方式联系在一起--立刻--以至于说他们的爱的宗教只是愚蠢是不厚道的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有礼貌的, 客气的；adv. 有礼貌地, 客气地
vocabulary.context: Which mannerly devotion shows in this;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这表明了何等虔诚的奉献；

vocabulary.definition: n. 催产素；[化] 缩宫素; 催产素
vocabulary.context: Some studies claim that we can clearly distinguish what happens in our brains at the moment of initial attraction - when chemicals related to pleasure, excitement and anxiety predominate - from what happens in true romantic attachment, when attachment hormones like oxytocin take over.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些研究声称，我们可以清楚地区分最初吸引时大脑中发生的情况（此时与快乐、兴奋和焦虑相关的化学物质占主导地位）与真正的浪漫依恋中发生的情况（当催产素等依恋激素接管时）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 低级的, 劣等的, 轻度的；[医] 轻度的(如感染), 低的(如热度)
vocabulary.context: This is our way of believing in low-grade love at first sight, while still scorning its full-blown form.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这就是我们相信低级一见钟情的方式，同时仍然蔑视其成熟的形式

vocabulary.definition: n. 糖衣；v.t. 裹以糖衣, 粉饰
vocabulary.context: To its detractors, love at first sight must be an illusion - the wrong term for what is simply infatuation, or a way to sugarcoat lust.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于它的批评者来说，一见钟情一定是一种幻觉--这个词错误地描述了单纯的迷恋，或者是一种粉饰欲望的方式

vocabulary.definition: n. 安魂曲, 安灵曲, 挽歌, 悲歌
vocabulary.context: Despairing (but still determined), he visits her grave and sings to her a stirring requiem, set to the tune of "American Pie":
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 绝望（但仍然坚定）的他拜访了她的坟墓，并向她唱了一首激动人心的安魂曲，曲调是《美国派》：

vocabulary.definition: n. 摆设酒宴者, 饮酒狂欢者( reveler的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: This is not a man with an office furniture catalog, or two revelers grinding at a club.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这不是一个拿着办公家具目录的人，也不是两个在俱乐部里狂欢的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 迷惑, 迷恋, 醉心
vocabulary.context: To its detractors, love at first sight must be an illusion - the wrong term for what is simply infatuation, or a way to sugarcoat lust.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于它的批评者来说，一见钟情一定是一种幻觉--这个词错误地描述了单纯的迷恋，或者是一种粉饰欲望的方式

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重击, 打, 击败, 毁灭, 侵袭, 打动；v.i. 重击, 打；n. 重击, 打
vocabulary.context: If you find yourself smitten with someone you've only just met, you'll question whether you should give the feeling so much weight - and risk ending up like Michael.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果你发现自己爱上了一个刚刚认识的人，你会质疑是否应该如此重视这种感觉 - 并冒着像迈克尔一样的风险

vocabulary.definition: n. 无处不在, 遍布
vocabulary.context: Considering the sheer pervasiveness of unethical behavior demonstrated by crime statistics, the "cheater's high" that people experience when engaging in forbidden activities may be a more potent motivator than most people realize.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 考虑到犯罪统计数据显示的不道德行为的普遍存在，人们在从事被禁止的活动时所经历的"作弊快感"可能是比大多数人意识到的更强大的动机

vocabulary.definition: adj. 反社会的, 不爱社交的
vocabulary.context: Though we tend to associate things like the "cheater's high" with sociopathic behavior, it is likely more common than we care to believe.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然我们倾向于将"作弊者的兴奋"之类的事情与反社会行为联系起来，但这种情况可能比我们想象的更常见

vocabulary.definition: adj. 人手不足的, 秘密的, 卑劣的
vocabulary.context: Is there an emotional high that people experience when they get away with doing something underhanded?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当人们做了不正当的事情而逃脱惩罚时，他们是否会经历一种情绪高涨？

vocabulary.definition: n. 黑客, 骇客；电脑黑客（hacker的复数）
vocabulary.context: The motivation provided by the "cheater's high" can drive a wide range of different problem behaviors, including the billions of dollars in damage caused by online hackers who bypass security systems largely for the "bragging rights" involved.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "作弊者的兴奋"提供的动机可能会引发各种不同的问题行为，包括在线黑客绕过安全系统造成的数十亿美元的损失，这些黑客主要是为了涉及"吹牛的权利"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 一般化, 概括, 推广；v.i. 形成概念, 笼统讲
vocabulary.context: While the research is based on experiments that may not generalize to the real world, Nicole Ruedy and her fellow researchers suggest that emotions play a stronger role in ethical decision-making than conventional models seem willing to acknowledge.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然这项研究基于可能无法推广到现实世界的实验，但 Nicole Ruedy 和她的研究人员同事认为，情绪在道德决策中发挥的作用比传统模型似乎愿意承认的更重要

vocabulary.definition: adj. 守法的, 安分守纪的；[法] 守法的, 安分守已的, 守秩序的
vocabulary.context: While tempting to dismiss Abagnale and others like him as being substantially different from law-abiding people with a well-developed moral sense, is that necessarily the case?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然人们很容易认为阿巴内尔和其他像他一样的人与具有良好道德感的守法人士有本质上的不同，但情况一定是这样吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 逃避, 藉口；[法] 回避, 规避, 逃避
vocabulary.context: But what about relatively victimless crimes such as computer piracy, tax evasion, insurance fraud, or cheating on tests?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是对于计算机盗版、逃税、保险欺诈或考试作弊等相对无受害者的犯罪呢？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 顽固的, 任性的, 性急的, 猛烈的
vocabulary.context: I was heady with happiness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我欣喜若狂

vocabulary.definition: adj. 边缘的, 最低限度的, 有旁注的；[医] 缘的
vocabulary.context: Though the "high" was often only marginal, the feeling of having "gotten away" with cheating seems to override feelings of guilt or shame at cheating.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然"兴奋"往往只是微不足道的，但"摆脱"作弊的感觉似乎压倒了因作弊而产生的内疚或羞耻感

vocabulary.definition: n. 香槟酒, 香槟酒色；[化] 香槟酒
vocabulary.context: Since I hadn't yet had my first taste of alcohol, I couldn't compare the feeling to a champagne high, but it was the most delightful sensation I'd ever experienced.

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 审讯人员有时会采取极端的、道德上有问题的措施来逼供，包括震耳欲聋的噪音、强烈的情绪操纵以及扣留食物、水和休息

vocabulary.context: "Many of these interrogations involve these extreme techniques," says study coauthor Elizabeth Loftus, a psychology and social behavior professor at the University of California, Irvine.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "许多审讯都涉及这些极端的技术，"研究合著者、加州大学欧文分校心理学和社会行为教授伊丽莎白·洛夫特斯说

vocabulary.definition: n. 分期连载作品, 连载小说；adj. 连续的, 一连串的, 连载的, 分期偿还的；[计] 连续的; 串行的
vocabulary.context: That ambiguity can lead to a shocking number of wrongful convictions, as dissections of high-profile trials in the NPR podcast Serial and the Netflix documentary Making a Murderer reveal.

vocabulary.definition: n. 地球物理学；[化] 地球物理; 地球物理学
vocabulary.context: "There's been controversy for some time as to how to determine the killing rate of elephants," says lead author Thure Cerling, a distinguished professor of geology, geophysics and biology at the University of Utah.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "关于如何确定大象的猎杀率，一段时间以来一直存在争议，"主要作者、犹他大学地质学、地球物理学和生物学杰出教授 Thure Cerling 说

vocabulary.definition: n. 放射性碳, 碳-14；[化] 放射性碳
vocabulary.context: To settle this debate, Cerling and his colleagues used carbon-14 dating, a well-established method that relies on radiocarbon produced by nuclear tests carried out in the 1950s and 1960s.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了解决这场争论，Cerling 和他的同事使用了碳 14 测年法，这是一种成熟的方法，依赖于 20 世纪 50 年代和 1960 年代进行的核试验产生的放射性碳

vocabulary.definition: v. 成木乃伊状( mummify的过去式和过去分词 ); 干瘪; 使干瘪; 使成木乃伊
vocabulary.context: This method has been used in forensics to date mummified corpses found in the desert, for example, or to determine how long it takes for cocaine to travel from forest to urban consumer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种方法已被用于法医学，例如确定沙漠中发现的木乃伊尸体的年代，或者确定可卡因从森林传播到城市消费者手中需要多长时间

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未经试验的, 未经考验的
vocabulary.context: "One can no longer hide and pretend ignorance, in the hope that illegal objects such as ivory will remain untested," he says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "人们不能再隐藏和假装无知，希望象牙等非法物品不会受到检验，"他说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 得到确认的；固定下来的
vocabulary.context: To settle this debate, Cerling and his colleagues used carbon-14 dating, a well-established method that relies on radiocarbon produced by nuclear tests carried out in the 1950s and 1960s.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了解决这场争论，Cerling 和他的同事使用了碳 14 测年法，这是一种成熟的方法，依赖于 20 世纪 50 年代和 1960 年代进行的核试验产生的放射性碳

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 引爆, 使爆炸, 使爆裂；v.i. 爆炸, 发爆炸声
vocabulary.context: and Soviet Union detonated so many bombs that they changed the concentration of carbon-14 in the atmosphere by a factor of two.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 和苏联引爆了如此多的炸弹，导致大气中碳14的浓度改变了两倍

vocabulary.definition: adv. 亲切地, 亲密地, 个人性地
vocabulary.context: This finding suggests that the recent surge in elephant deaths - savannah-dwelling populations have declined by 30 percent in the last seven years, while elephants living in forests dropped 62 percent from 2002 to 2013 - are intimately linked with the illegal global trade in ivory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一发现表明，近期大象死亡人数激增--过去七年中，居住在大草原的大象数量下降了 30%，而 2002 年至 2013 年间，生活在森林中的大象数量下降了 62%--与全球非法象牙贸易密切相关

vocabulary.definition: n. 违禁品, 走私；adj. 禁运的, 非法买卖的
vocabulary.context: Alternatively, it could be that East Africa has more established illegal networks for moving contraband goods, or that savannah elephants are simply easier to find and kill.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 或者，东非可能有更成熟的非法运输违禁品网络，或者草原象更容易被发现和猎杀

vocabulary.definition: n. 腰布

vocabulary.definition: n. 红皮肤人
vocabulary.context: Though many have been retired, including Oklahoma's Little Red in 1972, notable examples - baseball's Cleveland Indians and Atlanta Braves, and football's Washington Redskins - continue, perhaps not as mascots, but in naming conventions and the use of Indian motifs in logos.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管许多球队已经退役，包括 1972 年俄克拉荷马州的小红队，但著名的例子--棒球的克利夫兰印第安人队和亚特兰大勇士队，以及橄榄球队的华盛顿红皮队--仍在继续，也许不是作为吉祥物，而是在命名惯例和徽标中使用印第安图案

vocabulary.definition: n. 研究班, 专题讨论会；[经] 讨论会, 专家讨论会
vocabulary.context: "We need to bring out the history, and that's the point of the seminar, is that it's not a benign sort of undertaking," explains Gover.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们需要揭示历史，这就是研讨会的重点，这不是一项良性的事业，"戈弗解释道

vocabulary.definition: n. 连合活字, 商标, 徽标；[计] 徽标
vocabulary.context: Though many have been retired, including Oklahoma's Little Red in 1972, notable examples - baseball's Cleveland Indians and Atlanta Braves, and football's Washington Redskins - continue, perhaps not as mascots, but in naming conventions and the use of Indian motifs in logos.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管许多球队已经退役，包括 1972 年俄克拉荷马州的小红队，但著名的例子--棒球的克利夫兰印第安人队和亚特兰大勇士队，以及橄榄球队的华盛顿红皮队--仍在继续，也许不是作为吉祥物，而是在命名惯例和徽标中使用印第安图案

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不协调的, 不一致的, 不合适的
vocabulary.context: Gover, who today is the director of the American Indian Museum, says he remembers thinking, "I couldn't quite understand why an Indian would get up and dance when the Sooners scored a touchdown." Of Pawnee heritage, Gover says he understands now that the use of Indian names and imagery for mascots is more than just incongruous.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 戈弗现在是美国印第安人博物馆馆长，他说他记得当时的想法："我不太明白为什么当捷者队触地得分时印第安人会站起来跳舞。"关于波尼族的传统，戈弗表示，他现在明白，使用印度名字和图像作为吉祥物不仅仅是不协调

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 热敷, 煽动
vocabulary.context: And in the days of the revolution, Abigail Adams wrote her husband, John Adams, at the continental Congress, which was framing the laws of the infant nation that, ''if-in the new laws-particular care and attention are not paid to the ladies, we are determined to foment a rebellion and will not hold ourselves bound to obey any laws in which we have no voice."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在革命时期，阿比盖尔·亚当斯在大陆会议上写信给她的丈夫约翰·亚当斯，当时大陆会议正在制定这个新生国家的法律，"如果在新的法律中没有对女士们给予特别的照顾和关注，我们就会决心煽动叛乱，并且不会要求自己遵守任何我们没有发言权的法律。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 阳光突现
vocabulary.context: In Wilmington, Del., in 1869, Memorial Day included a procession of Methodists, Baptists, Unitarians and Catholics; white Grand Army of the Republic posts in parade with a black post; and the "Mount Vernon Cornet Band (colored)" keeping step with the "Irish Nationalists with the harp and the sunburst flag of Erin."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1869年，在特拉华州威尔明顿，阵亡将士纪念日，卫理公会教徒、浸信会教徒、一神论者和天主教徒参加了游行。白色的共和国大军在阅兵式上插着黑色的柱子；和"弗农山短号乐队（彩色）"与"拿着竖琴和艾琳旭日旗的爱尔兰民族主义者"保持同步

vocabulary.definition: adj. 时间翘曲的
vocabulary.context: Blacks had a place in this Confederate narrative, but only as time-warped loyal slaves who were supposed to remain frozen in the past.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黑人在南方邦联的叙事中占有一席之地，但只是作为时间扭曲的忠诚奴隶，他们应该被冻结在过去

vocabulary.definition: n. 棱堡, 阵地工事
vocabulary.context: The symbolic power of this Low Country planter aristocracy's bastion was not lost on the freedpeople, who then, in cooperation with white missionaries and teachers, staged a parade of 10,000 on the track.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自由民并没有忽视这个低地种植园贵族堡垒的象征力量，他们随后与白人传教士和教师合作，在跑道上举行了10,000人的游行

vocabulary.definition: adj. 平等主义的；n. 平等主义
vocabulary.context: Some events even stressed the Union dead as the source of a new egalitarian America, and a civic rather than a racial or ethnic definition of citizenship.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些事件甚至强调联邦死亡是新平等主义美国的源泉，以及公民身份的公民定义，而不是种族或民族定义

vocabulary.definition: adj. 军人的, 像军人的, 适于军人的
vocabulary.context: Memorial Days were initially occasions of sacred bereavement, and from the war's end to the early 20th century they helped forge national reconciliation around soldierly sacrifice, regardless of cause.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阵亡将士纪念日最初是纪念丧亲之痛的神圣日子，从战争结束到20世纪初，阵亡将士纪念日帮助围绕士兵牺牲（无论出于何种原因）达成民族和解

vocabulary.definition: adv. 正直地
vocabulary.context: I love you straightforwardly, without complexities or pride;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我爱你直截了当，没有复杂，没有骄傲；

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 或火射出的康乃馨之箭

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从大地升起，黑暗地生活在我的身体里

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如此接近，你放在我胸口的手就是我的手，

vocabulary.context: I love you straightforwardly, without complexities or pride;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我爱你直截了当，没有复杂，没有骄傲；

vocabulary.definition: n. 敏感性, 感情；[电] 磁化系数
vocabulary.context: As a researcher who studies the search and recommendation systems, I and my colleagues show that this dangerous combination of corporate profit motive and individual susceptibility makes the problem difficult to fix.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为一名研究搜索和推荐系统的研究人员，我和我的同事表明，企业利润动机和个人敏感性的这种危险结合使得问题难以解决

vocabulary.definition: n. 最佳化, 最优化；[计] 优化
vocabulary.context: Search engine optimization techniques use this knowledge to increase the visibility of websites.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 搜索引擎优化技术利用这些知识来提高网站的可见度

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未经核对的, 未经证实的
vocabulary.context: This pattern of thrilling and unverified stories emerging and people clicking on them continues, with people apparently either being unconcerned with the truth or believing that if a trusted service such as Google Search is showing these stories to them then the stories must be true.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种令人兴奋且未经证实的故事不断出现，人们点击它们的模式仍在继续，人们显然要么不关心真相，要么相信，如果谷歌搜索等值得信赖的服务向他们展示这些故事，那么这些故事一定是真实的

vocabulary.definition: n. 导管, 水管, 沟渠；[电] 导管
vocabulary.context: Search engines are one of society's primary gateways to information and people, but they are also conduits for misinformation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 搜索引擎是社会获取信息和人员的主要门户之一，但它们也是错误信息的传播渠道

vocabulary.definition: n. 调音师, 调音的人, 调节器；[电] 调谐器
vocabulary.context: The search service feels validated with positive relevance feedback and learns that it is OK to show a cat playing a piano when people search for piano tuners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 搜索服务感觉得到了积极的相关反馈的验证，并了解到当人们搜索钢琴调音器时，显示一只猫在弹钢琴是可以的

vocabulary.definition: adv. 医学, 医疗, 医术, 内科, 医药
vocabulary.context: One study found that more popular YouTube videos about diabetes are less likely to have medically valid information than less popular videos on the subject, for instance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，一项研究发现，与不太受欢迎的糖尿病相关视频相比，更受欢迎的有关糖尿病的 YouTube 视频不太可能包含医学上有效的信息

vocabulary.definition: n. 曳, 牵引, 牵引力；[医] 牵引
vocabulary.context: More recently, a disproven report claiming China let the coronavirus leak from a lab gained traction on search engines because of this vicious cycle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最近，一份未经证实的报告声称中国让冠状病毒从实验室泄漏，由于这种恶性循环，在搜索引擎上引起了关注

vocabulary.definition: adj. 使人感动的, 非常好的
vocabulary.context: Because people are drawn to the sensational, this dance between algorithms and human nature can foster the spread of misinformation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于人们被耸人听闻的事物所吸引，算法与人性之间的这种舞蹈可能会助长错误信息的传播

vocabulary.definition: adj. 记不起的,不被想起的, 记不住的, 忘却的
vocabulary.context: Therefore it was true that the tribe lived, that it stood for the obedience of all its members, way down into the forgotten past, whose very resting-places were unremembered.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，部落确实存在过，它代表着所有成员的服从，一直到被遗忘的过去，他们的安息之地也无人记得

vocabulary.definition: v. 诅咒, 强烈遣责, 宣布革出教门
vocabulary.context: that was Sit-cum-to-ha, shrilly anathematizing the dogs as she cuffed and beat them into the harnesses.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那是"Sit-cum-to-ha"，一边尖声咒骂那些狗，一边给它们戴上手铐并把它们打进挽具

vocabulary.definition: n. 绿色, 新鲜, 未熟
vocabulary.context: The rise of the sap, the bursting greenness of the willow bud, the fall of the yellow leaf-in this alone was told the whole history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 树液的兴起，柳芽的嫩绿，黄叶的落下--仅此一事就讲述了整个历史

vocabulary.definition: n. 部落男子
vocabulary.context: The chief was his son, stalwart and strong, head man of the tribesmen, and a mighty hunter.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 酋长是他的儿子，身材魁梧、强壮，是部落的首领，也是一位强大的猎人

vocabulary.definition: adv. 发着抖
vocabulary.context: The thought made the old man panicky for the moment, and he stretched forth a palsied hand which wandered tremblingly over the small heap of dry wood beside him.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个念头让老人一时惊慌失措，他伸出一只麻木的手，颤抖着在身旁的一小堆干木头上走来走去

vocabulary.definition: n. [动]狼獾,豹熊,狼獾皮
vocabulary.context: It would die soon, perhaps, and they would burn a hole through the frozen tundra and pile rocks above to keep the wolverines away.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 也许它很快就会死掉，他们会在冰冻的苔原上烧一个洞，并在上面堆起岩石来驱赶狼獾

vocabulary.definition: n. 足迹
vocabulary.context: In the midst were the deep impressions of the splay-hoofed game, and all about, everywhere, were the lighter footmarks of the wolves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中有八蹄动物留下的深刻印象，到处都是狼的浅色足迹

vocabulary.definition: n. 追踪者, 跟踪雷达, 拉纤者；[法] 追踪人, 探索者, 追踪空中移动物体的仪器
vocabulary.context: Eager-footed, they took the trail, and even he, Koskoosh, slow of sight and an unversed tracker, could have followed it blind, it was so wide.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们急切地走上了这条小路，即使是他，科斯科什，视力迟钝，又不懂追踪，也可能盲目地沿着这条路走，因为它太宽了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 疥癣的, 污秽的
vocabulary.context: Reassured that it was indeed there, his hand returned to the shelter of his mangy furs, and he again fell to listening.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 确信它确实在那里，他的手回到了肮脏的皮毛的庇护所，然后他再次开始倾听

vocabulary.definition: n. 特定场合, 当前；adj. 特定场合的
vocabulary.context: Overcome for the nonce by his hereditary fear of man, the brute retreated, raising a prolonged call to his brothers; and greedily they answered, till a ring of crouching, jaw-slobbered gray was stretched round about.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个野蛮人暂时克服了他对人类遗传的恐惧，撤退了，对他的兄弟们发出了长时间的呼唤。他们贪婪地回答着，直到周围拉出了一圈蹲伏着、流着口水的灰色人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不值得考虑的, 被忽视的, 欠考虑的
vocabulary.context: "Henry, the mere unconsidered drippings of your daily income would -"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "亨利，你日常收入中的一点点不经意的损失都会--"

vocabulary.definition: n. 优先, 居先；[计] 先后次序; 优先规定
vocabulary.context: ...and so on, climbing steadily, as my notoriety augmented, until I reached the highest altitude possible, and there I remained, taking precedence of all dukes not royal, and of all ecclesiastics except the primate of all England.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...等等，随着我的名声越来越大，我的名声越来越大，直到我达到了可能的最高高度，我就留在那里，优先于所有非王室的公爵，以及除全英格兰的灵长类动物之外的所有神职人员

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不能决定的, 不能解决的
vocabulary.context: That disposes of the indeterminable quantities; the remainder of the matter is tangible, solid, and may be classed and labeled with certainty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 处理不可确定的数量；物质的其余部分是有形的、坚实的，并且可以确定地进行分类和标记

vocabulary.definition: n. 宴会
vocabulary.context: He received me with the enthusiasm proper in my case, upbraided me for being so tardy in my duty, and said that there was only one way to get his forgiveness, and that was to take the seat at his dinner-party that night made vacant by the illness of one of his guests.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他以与我的情况相称的热情接待了我，责备我在我的职责中如此迟到，并说只有一种方法可以得到他的原谅，那就是那天晚上在他的晚宴上占据因一位客人生病而空出来的座位

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无隔宿之粮的, 仅能糊口的
vocabulary.context: From being a poor, struggling, little hand-to-mouth enterprise, it had become celebrated, and overcrowded with customers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它从一家贫穷、苦苦挣扎、勉强糊口的小企业，变成了声名显赫、顾客盈门的企业

vocabulary.definition: adv. injudicious的变形
vocabulary.context: It couldn't be settled, of course, struggle as we might and did, he finally (and injudiciously) trying to play birth and antiquity, and I "seeing" his Conqueror and "raising" him with Adam, whose direct posterity I was, as shown by my name, while he was of a collateral branch, as shown by his, and...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当然，无论我们如何努力，这件事都无法解决，他最终（而且不明智地）试图扮演出生和古代的角色，而我"看到"了他的征服者，并与亚当一起"抚养"了他，我是亚当的直系后裔，正如我的名字所表明的那样，而他是一个并列分支，正如他的名字所表明的那样，并且......

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未经删节的, 完整的
vocabulary.context: There are not words enough in the unabridged to describe it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 未删节的文字不足以描述它

vocabulary.context: He was worshiping, with all his body and soul, but he looked as if he couldn't stir hand or foot.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他正在全身心地敬拜，但他看起来好像手脚都动不了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无嫌疑的, 不可疑的, 无疑虑的
vocabulary.context: the interest the minister's wife and daughter took in our little affair, and the endless ingenuities they invented to save us from interruption, and to keep the minister in the dark and unsuspicious - well, it was just lovely of them!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 牧师的妻子和女儿对我们的小事很感兴趣，他们发明了无穷无尽的聪明才智来使我们免受干扰，并使牧师不知情且不怀疑--好吧，他们真是太可爱了！

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 弄湿, 使沮丧；v.i. 变湿, 丧气
vocabulary.context: Thus, race has the power to dampen our desire to be merciful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，种族有能力抑制我们仁慈的愿望

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家证实了这一点，表明推理能力、冲动控制和神经发育方面存在显着差异
