vocabulary.context: Scrutinize(verb)to examine something carefully 2.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Scrutinize（动词）仔细检查某事 2.

vocabulary.definition: n. 杀人, 杀人者；[医] 杀人, 杀人者
vocabulary.context: Consider Florida, which is the state that had most often assigned juveniles life without parole sentences in cases other than homicide.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 以佛罗里达州为例，该州最常在除凶杀案以外的案件中判处青少年无假释终身监禁

vocabulary.definition: adj. 刑罚的, 惩罚性的；[法] 刑罚的, 惩罚性的, 结予惩处的
vocabulary.context: Though often overlooked, perhaps race is key to helping us understand people's support for punitive policies more generally.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然种族经常被忽视，但也许种族是帮助我们更广泛地了解人们对惩罚性政策的支持的关键

vocabulary.definition: adj. 仁慈的, 慈悲的
vocabulary.context: Thus, race has the power to dampen our desire to be merciful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，种族有能力抑制我们仁慈的愿望

vocabulary.definition: n. 不一致；[医] 差异, 不等
vocabulary.context: But as our society has scrutinized this line between juvenile and adult, there has been little discussion of how race might influence people's perceptions of juvenile status, despite widespread and substantial racial disparities in juvenile sentencing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，当我们的社会仔细审视青少年和成人之间的界限时，尽管青少年量刑中存在广泛且巨大的种族差异，但很少有人讨论种族如何影响人们对青少年地位的看法

vocabulary.definition: n. 假释, 誓言, 释放宣言；v.t. 宣誓后释放, 使假释出狱
vocabulary.context: For the second time in two years, they are poised to rule on the constitutionality of sentencing juveniles to life in prison with no possibility of parole.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 两年内第二次，他们准备就判处青少年终身监禁且不得假释的可能性是否符合宪法作出裁决

vocabulary.definition: v. 认为, 相信
vocabulary.context: This is why race must be considered in discussions about how we protect juveniles and what punishments are deemed appropriate for them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这就是为什么在讨论我们如何保护青少年以及什么样的惩罚被认为适合他们时必须考虑种族问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 空着的, 没人占的, 未被占领的
vocabulary.context: The man doesn't acknowledge you as you sit down because the man knows more about the unoccupied seat than you do.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当你坐下时，这个人并没有认出你，因为他比你更了解空着的座位

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 放弃, 断念, 抛弃；[法] 遗弃, 抛弃, 摒绝
vocabulary.context: You sit next to the man on the train, bus, in the plane, waiting room, anywhere he could be forsaken.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在火车上、公共汽车上、飞机上、候诊室里，以及任何他可能被遗弃的地方，你都坐在那个人旁边

vocabulary.context: And death shall be no more; Death, thou shalt die.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 死亡将不再存在；死亡，你就该死

vocabulary.definition: adv. 永恒地, 常常
vocabulary.context: One short sleep past, we wake eternally
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 短暂的睡眠过去，我们永远醒来

vocabulary.definition: n. 推翻, 瓦解, 倾覆；v.t. 打倒, 推翻, 倾覆
vocabulary.context: For those whom thou think'st thou dost overthrow
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于那些你认为你已经推翻的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 递送, 交付, 分娩, 交货, 引渡；[化] 交货额
vocabulary.context: Rest of their bones, and soul's delivery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的骨头安息，灵魂得救

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可怕的
vocabulary.context: Mighty and dreadful, for thou art not so;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 强大而可怕，因为你并非如此；

vocabulary.definition: n. 毒药, 毒, 毒物, 有毒害的事物；v.t. 毒害, 毒杀, 使中毒；v.i. 放毒, 下毒
vocabulary.context: And dost with poison, war, and sickness dwell,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 毒药、战争和疾病居住在其中，

vocabulary.definition: n. 笔划, 打, 中风, 抚, 摩, 冲程；v.t. 划尾桨, 抚, 摩, 划去；[计] 笔划
vocabulary.context: And better than thy stroke; why swell'st thou then?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 比你的中风更好；那你为什么膨胀呢？

vocabulary.definition: n. 骨头, 骨, 骨制品；v.t. 剔骨；v.i. 专心致志
vocabulary.context: Rest of their bones, and soul's delivery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的骨头安息，灵魂得救

vocabulary.definition: n. 小薄片, 扁薄的一层, 火星, 晒鱼架子；v.t. 使成薄片；v.i. 剥落
vocabulary.context: Of easy wind and downy flake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 轻松的风和柔软的片状

vocabulary.definition: adj. 绒毛的, 柔和的, 有草丘陵地的
vocabulary.context: Of easy wind and downy flake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 轻松的风和柔软的片状

vocabulary.definition: n. 农舍
vocabulary.context: To stop without a farmhouse near
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 附近没有农舍就停下来

vocabulary.definition: n. 马具, 挽具状带子, 甲胄；v.t. 给...上挽具, 驾驭, 披上甲胄, 利用...以产生动力
vocabulary.context: He gives his harness bells a shake
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他摇了摇他的马具铃

vocabulary.definition: n. 怪人, 同性恋者；adj. 奇怪的, 不舒服的, 可疑的；v.t. 搞糟
vocabulary.context: My little horse must think it queer
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的小马一定觉得很奇怪

vocabulary.definition: n. 木材, 木制品；v.t. 植林于, 给...添加木柴；v.i. 收集木材
vocabulary.context: Whose woods these are I think I know.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我想我知道这些是谁的树林

vocabulary.definition: n. 扫除, 打扫, 肃清, 视野, 范围, 全胜；v.t. 扫除, 掸去, 猛拉, 扫荡, 肃清, 冲走, 刮起, 环视, 掠过, 扫射；v.i. 扫, 打扫, 袭击, 席卷, 扫视, 掠过
vocabulary.context: The only other sound's the sweep
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 唯一的其他声音是扫频声

vocabulary.definition: n. 错误, 误会；v.i. 犯错, 误认；v.t. 误解, 弄错；[计] 错误
vocabulary.context: To ask if there is some mistake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 请问是否有错误

vocabulary.definition: n. 村庄；adj. 乡村的, 村庄的
vocabulary.context: His house is in the village though;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然他的房子在村里；

vocabulary.definition: n. 舰长的职位, 大将的才略, 舰长的衔头
vocabulary.context: He was actually gazetted to a captaincy in a marching regiment!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他实际上被任命为行军团的队长！

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不狡猾的, 诚实的
vocabulary.context: He was evidently good, and sweet, and lovable, and guileless; and so it was exceedingly painful to see him stand there, as serene as a graven image, and deliver himself of answers which were veritably miraculous for stupidity and ignorance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他显然是善良的、可爱的、可爱的、诚实的。因此，看到他站在那里，像一尊雕刻的雕像一样平静，并给出对愚蠢和无知来说确实是奇迹般的答案，是极其痛苦的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不虚装门面的, 不装模作样的, 谦逊的
vocabulary.context: He is just as good and sweet and lovable and unpretending as a man can be, but he doesn't know enough to come in when it rains.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他就像一个男人一样善良、可爱、可爱、朴实无华，但他不知道下雨时要进来

vocabulary.definition: adv. 真实地, 真正地, 真地
vocabulary.context: He was evidently good, and sweet, and lovable, and guileless; and so it was exceedingly painful to see him stand there, as serene as a graven image, and deliver himself of answers which were veritably miraculous for stupidity and ignorance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他显然是善良的、可爱的、可爱的、诚实的。因此，看到他站在那里，像一尊雕刻的雕像一样平静，并给出对愚蠢和无知来说确实是奇迹般的答案，是极其痛苦的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 幸运, 好运
vocabulary.context: He has been pursued, day by day and year by year, by a most phenomenal and astonishing luckiness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他日复一日、年复一年地被一种非凡而惊人的幸运所追赶

vocabulary.definition: n. 古时骑兵掌旗官之官阶[职位]
vocabulary.context: So I took my poor little capital that I had saved up through years of work and grinding economy, and went with a sigh and bought a cornetcy in his regiment, and away we went to the field.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 于是，我带着我通过多年的工作和艰苦的经济积攒下来的可怜的小本钱，叹了口气，在他的团里买了一个角，然后我们就去了田野

vocabulary.definition: n. 准男爵
vocabulary.context: He has been a shining soldier in all our wars for half a generation; he has littered his military life with blunders, and yet has never committed one that didn't make him a knight or a baronet or a lord or something.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 半代人以来，他一直是我们所有战争中的光辉战士；他的军旅生活充满了错误，但从未犯下任何不能让他成为骑士、男爵、领主或其他什么的错误

vocabulary.definition: adv. 显著地, 超群地
vocabulary.context: It was at a banquet in London in honour of one of the two or three conspicuously illustrious English military names of this generation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是在伦敦举行的一场宴会上，纪念这一代两三个杰出的英国军事人物之一

vocabulary.definition: n. 庄严, 崇高, 高尚
vocabulary.context: Better men grow old and gray in the service before they climb to a sublimity like that.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更好的人在达到那样的崇高之前，会在服役中变老、变白

vocabulary.definition: n. 半神半人, 小神, 受崇拜的人
vocabulary.context: It was food and drink to me to look, and look, and look at that demigod; scanning, searching, noting: the quietness, the reserve, the noble gravity of his countenance; the simple honesty that expressed itself all over him; the sweet unconsciousness of his...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 看着、看着、看着那个半神，对我来说就像食物和饮料；扫视、搜寻、注意：他脸上的安静、矜持、高贵的严肃；他浑身上下都流露着简单的诚实；他甜蜜的无意识......

vocabulary.definition: n. 盘问
vocabulary.context: ...punished, and Black protesters could not be singled out for exercising constitutionally protected rights to assemble. Under Motley's cross-examination, W.J. Haley, chief inspector of the Birmingham Police Department, admitted the city had targeted movement leaders and acknowledged the mass arrests were the first time in 20 years the city had enforced...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...受到惩罚，黑人抗议者不能因为行使宪法保护的集会权而被单独挑出来。在莫特利的盘问下，伯明翰警察局首席督察 W.J. Haley 承认该市针对运动领导人，并承认大规模逮捕是该市 20 年来首次实施大规模逮捕

vocabulary.definition: adj. 开路先锋的,开创性的
vocabulary.context: The pathbreaking protégée of Thurgood Marshall, Motley had spent many years as the only woman lawyer at the NAACP Legal Defense and Education Fund (LDF).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莫特利是瑟古德·马歇尔的开创性门生，多年来一直担任全国有色人种协进会法律辩护和教育基金（LDF）的唯一女律师

vocabulary.definition: adj. 盲从的, 固执己见的, 执迷的, 顽固的
vocabulary.context: But he felt no such obligation toward bigoted state and local judges.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但他觉得对偏执的州和地方法官没有这样的义务

vocabulary.definition: adv. 守法地；合法地
vocabulary.context: The children could not lawfully be punished for protesting segregation, the Appeals Court ruled, and ultimately returned to school within 24 hours of the superintendent's retaliatory action.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 上诉法院裁定，孩子们不能因抗议隔离而受到合法惩罚，并最终在学监采取报复行动后 24 小时内返回学校

vocabulary.definition: adj. 顶点的, 高潮的
vocabulary.context: Indeed, the zeal of the protesters in April and May of that year led to a climactic legal battle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，当年四月和五月抗议者的热情导致了一场法律斗争的高潮

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 处刑, 对...处罚(刑), 使不利；[法] 处罚, 对...处以刑事惩罚
vocabulary.context: At this precarious moment, Motley responded by filing a lawsuit in federal court arguing that the school board's action violated the rights of all 1,100 students who had been penalized.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这个岌岌可危的时刻，莫特利向联邦法院提起诉讼作为回应，认为学校董事会的行为侵犯了所有受到处罚的1100名学生的权利

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 转移, 使偏斜；v.i. 偏斜
vocabulary.context: Employing a tactic that Thurgood Marshall had urged fellow Black lawyers to use in the face of bigoted comments, Motley deflected the insult and pushed back with cool professionalism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莫特利采用了瑟古德·马歇尔敦促其他黑人律师在面对偏执评论时使用的策略，转移了侮辱，并以冷静的专业精神进行了反击

vocabulary.definition: n. 被告；[经] 被告方
vocabulary.context: "There is not a shred of evidence," she insisted, "that these defendants engaged in unlawful activity of any kind." Regardless of the court order blocking the demonstration, protesters could not legally be punished, and Black protesters could not be singled out for exercising constitutionally protected rights to assemble.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "没有任何证据，"她坚称，"这些被告从事任何形式的非法活动。"无论法院下令阻止示威活动，抗议者都无法受到法律惩罚，黑人抗议者也不能因行使宪法保护的集会权而被单独挑出来

vocabulary.definition: n. 移居, 轮回；[医] 移行, 移动; 血细胞渗出
vocabulary.context: If there is any truth in the theory of transmigration, this particular mouse must certainly have been in a former state a member of the Alpine Club.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果轮回理论有任何道理，那么这只特定的老鼠肯定曾经是阿尔卑斯俱乐部的成员

vocabulary.definition: adj. 酒神节的, 狂饮作乐的
vocabulary.context: He had been staying at a country vicarage, the inmates of which had been certainly neither brutal nor bacchanalian, but their supervision of the domestic establishment had been of that lax order which invites disaster.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他一直住在一个乡村牧师住宅里，里面的囚犯当然既不野蛮也不疯狂，但他们对国内机构的监督却是松懈的，这会招致灾难

vocabulary.definition: adj. 凌乱的,不整洁的
vocabulary.context: Like a hunted beast breaking cover and dashing madly toward some other haven of momentary safety he threw aside his rug, and struggled frantically into his disheveled garments.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就像一头被猎杀的野兽冲破掩体，疯狂地冲向另一个暂时安全的避难所，他扔开地毯，疯狂地挣扎着穿上他凌乱的衣服

vocabulary.definition: n. 贬抑, 屈辱, 谦卑
vocabulary.context: All the blood in his body seemed to have mobilized in one concentrated blush, and an agony of abasement, worse than a myriad mice, crept up and down over his soul.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他全身的血液似乎都被调动成一团浓浓的红晕，一种比无数老鼠还要强烈的屈辱的痛苦在他的灵魂中爬上爬下

vocabulary.definition: n. 心乱, 不安, 狼狈
vocabulary.context: To a man of his temperament and upbringing even a simple railway journey was crammed with petty annoyances and minor discords, and as he settled himself down in a second-class compartment one September morning he was conscious of ruffled feelings and general mental discomposure.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于一个像他这样的性情和教养的人来说，即使是一次简单的铁路旅行也充满了琐碎的烦恼和轻微的不和谐，当他在九月的一个早晨在二等车厢安顿下来时，他意识到心情烦躁和普遍的精神不安

vocabulary.definition: n. 终点, 界标, 终点站；[法] 界标, 界牌, 界石
vocabulary.context: ...a lady of about the same age as himself, seemed inclined for slumber rather than scrutiny; the train was not due to stop till the terminus was reached, in about an hour's time, and the carriage was of the oId-fashioned sort that held no communication with a corridor, therefore no further traveling companions were likely to intrude on...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...一位与他年龄相仿的女士，似乎更倾向于睡觉而不是仔细观察；火车要到终点站才停下来，大约一个小时后，车厢是老式的，与走廊没有任何联系，因此不可能再有其他旅伴闯入......

vocabulary.definition: n. 甜菜根
vocabulary.context: Crimsoning to the hue of a beetroot and keeping an agonized watch on his slumbering fellow traveler, he swiftly and noiselessly secured the ends of his railway rug to the racks on either side of the carriage, so that a substantial curtain hung athwart the compartment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的脸色涨得像甜菜根一样，痛苦地注视着熟睡的旅伴，他迅速无声地将铁路地毯的两端固定在车厢两侧的架子上，这样车厢里就挂上了厚重的窗帘

vocabulary.definition: n. 透孔织物, 透雕细工
vocabulary.context: He had never been able to bring himself even to the mild exposure of openwork socks in the presence of the fair sex.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他从来都无法让自己在女性面前轻微地暴露镂空袜子

vocabulary.definition: n. 轻率, 轻浮, 不稳定
vocabulary.context: "Surely leaving off one small mouse wouldn't bring on a chill," she exclaimed, with a levity that Theodoric accounted abominable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "当然，留下一只小老鼠不会让人感到寒冷，"她大声说道，语气轻浮，狄奥多里克认为这是令人厌恶的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不眨眼的, 时刻警觉的
vocabulary.context: The furtive glance which Theodoric stole at her from time to time disclosed only an unwinking wakefulness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 狄奥多里克时不时地偷偷地瞥她一眼，只透露出她一眨不眨的清醒

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 照透视法缩短, 缩短
vocabulary.context: A carbine strikingly foreshortened lay across the pommel of the saddle, kept in place by the right hand grasping it at the 'grip;' the left hand, holding the bridle rein, was invisible.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一把明显缩短的卡宾枪横放在马鞍的鞍头上，由右手抓住它的"握把"将其固定在适当的位置；握着缰绳的左手是看不见的

vocabulary.definition: n. 装备, 配备
vocabulary.context: The gray costume harmonized with its aerial background; the metal of accoutrement and caparison was softened and subdued by the shadow; the animal's skin had no points of high light.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 灰色服装与其空中背景相协调；金属的服装和服装在阴影的作用下变得柔和柔和。动物的皮肤没有高光点

vocabulary.definition: n. formidable（可怕的, 令人畏惧的）的变形
vocabulary.context: Magnified by its lift against the sky and by the soldier's testifying sense of the formidableness of a near enemy the group appeared of heroic, almost colossal, size.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于它在天空中的升力，以及士兵对近敌的可怕感的证明，这群人显得英勇，几乎是巨大的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 雕像一般的, 均称的, 轮廓优美的
vocabulary.context: Carter Druse grew pale; he shook in every limb, turned faint, and saw the statuesque group before him as black figures, rising, falling, moving unsteadily in arcs of circles in a fiery sky.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 卡特·德鲁斯 (Carter Druse) 脸色苍白；他四肢颤抖，变得昏厥，看到面前那群雕像般的黑色身影，在火热的天空中升起、落下，摇摇晃晃地画着圆圈

vocabulary.definition: adv. 在那附近, 大约
vocabulary.context: Pulling himself together he ran rapidly obliquely away from the cliff to a point distant from its foot; thereabout he expected to find his man; and thereabout he naturally failed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他振作起来，迅速斜着离开悬崖，跑到远离悬崖脚的地方。他希望很快就能找到他的人。他自然就失败了

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 擦伤, 磨损, 磨；[医] 擦除, 擦破; 磨损, 磨除
vocabulary.context: The familiar sensation of an abraded shin recalled his dazed faculties.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 胫骨磨损的熟悉感觉让他想起了茫然的神智

vocabulary.definition: adj. 狮子的, 狮子般的
vocabulary.context: The father lifted his leonine head, looked at the son a moment in silence, and replied: 'Go, Carter, and whatever may occur, do what you conceive to be your duty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 父亲抬起他狮子般的头，沉默地看了儿子一会儿，然后回答道："去吧，卡特，无论发生什么，做你认为是你的职责的事情。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 弯弯曲曲的, 迂回的, 运动柔软的；[医] 纡曲的
vocabulary.context: He saw creeping across the green meadow a sinuous line of figures of men and horses-some foolish commander was permitting the soldiers of his escort to water their beasts in the open, in plain view from a dozen summits!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他看到一排蜿蜒的人和马的身影在绿色的草地上爬行--某个愚蠢的指挥官正在允许他的护卫士兵在露天浇灌他们的牲畜，从十几座山峰上都可以清楚地看到！

vocabulary.definition: n. 上坡
vocabulary.context: The clump of laurel in which the criminal lay was in the angle of a road which after ascending southward a steep acclivity to that point turned sharply to the west, running along the summit for perhaps one hundred yards.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 罪犯所在的月桂丛位于一条路的拐角处，这条路向南上升后，经过一个陡峭的斜坡，急速转向西边，沿着山顶延伸了大约一百码

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再装；v.i. 再装填弹药
vocabulary.context: After firing his shot, Private Carter Druse reloaded his rifle and resumed his watch.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 开枪后，二等兵卡特·德鲁斯重新装弹并继续值班

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可分类的,不可归类的
vocabulary.context: I am like to lose my mind over the capricious and harassing developments of this unclassifiable zoological freak.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我对这个无法分类的动物怪胎的反复无常和骚扰性的发展感到失去了理智

vocabulary.definition: adj. 丧气的, 失望的, 没有精神的
vocabulary.context: That was a mistake - it had been better to keep the remark to myself; it only gave her an idea - she could save the sick buzzard, and furnish fresh meat to the despondent lions and tigers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那是一个错误--最好把这句话留给我自己；这只是给了她一个想法--她可以拯救生病的秃鹰，并为沮丧的狮子和老虎提供新鲜的肉

vocabulary.definition: adj. 喜模仿的, 伪造的, 仿制的, 摹拟的
vocabulary.context: The old one is tamer than it was and can laugh and talk like a parrot, having learned this, no doubt, from being with the parrot so much, and having the imitative faculty in a high developed degree.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 老家伙比以前更驯服了，可以像鹦鹉一样笑、说话，毫无疑问，这是从与鹦鹉在一起的这么多时间中学到的，并且具有高度发展的模仿能力

vocabulary.definition: n. (=numskull)笨蛋, 傻瓜
vocabulary.context: ...to things that don't need them and don't come when they are called by them, which is a matter of no consequence to her, she is such a numbskull, anyway; so she got a lot of them out and brought them in last night and put them in my bed to keep warm, but I have noticed them now and then all day and I don't see that they are any happier there then...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ......那些不需要的东西，叫来也不来，这对她来说无所谓，反正她就是个傻瓜；所以她昨晚把很多它们拿出来并把它们放在我的床上取暖，但我一整天都时不时地注意到它们，我看不出它们在那里更快乐......

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高压的, 专横的；[法] 高压的, 专横的, 目空一切的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我看来，这已经够霸道了

vocabulary.definition: n. 乳齿象
vocabulary.context: She says they were only made for scenery - like the rhinoceros and the mastodon.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说它们只是为了风景而制作的--就像犀牛和乳齿象一样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 同时代的；n. 同时代的人
vocabulary.context: "There," she said, with triumph, "that is just it; the Serpent mentioned that very jest, and called it the First Chestnut, and said it was coeval with the creation." Alas, I am indeed to blame.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "好了，"她胜利地说，"就是这样；蛇提到了这个笑话，并称其为第一栗子，并说它与创造物同时代。"唉，确实是我的错

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有...腿的
vocabulary.context: Yet it differs from the other four legged animals, in that its front legs are unusually short, consequently this causes the main part of its person to stick up uncomfortably high in the air, and this is not attractive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但它与其他四足动物不同的是，它的前腿异常短，因此导致它的主体部分高高地立在空中，不舒服，不具有吸引力

vocabulary.definition: n. 无花果树叶
vocabulary.context: Swam the Whirlpool and the Rapids in a fig-leaf suit.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿着无花果叶套装游过漩涡和急流

vocabulary.definition: n. 掘, 选择, 挑选, 采摘, 撬开, [U]采得物, 捡得物, 赃物, 扒窃物；[经] 挑拣
vocabulary.context: In fact I was not sorry she came, for there are but meager pickings here, and she brought some of those apples.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，我并不遗憾她来了，因为这里的采摘量很少，而她带来了其中一些苹果

vocabulary.definition: adv. 夺人心魂地；可爱地, 迷人地
vocabulary.context: It was beautiful, surpassingly beautiful, enchantingly beautiful; and now it is lost, and I shall not see it any more.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它很美丽，非常美丽，非常美丽；现在它已经丢失了，我再也见不到它了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无数的, 不可数的
vocabulary.context: I had created something that didn't exist before; I had added a new thing to the world's uncountable properties; I realized this, and was proud of my achievement, and was going to run and find him and tell him about it, thinking to raise myself in his esteem - but I reflected, and did not do it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我创造了一些以前不存在的东西；我为世界上无数的财产添加了一个新的东西；我意识到了这一点，为自己的成就感到自豪，打算跑去找他，告诉他这件事，想提高自己对他的尊重--但我反思了一下，并没有这么做

vocabulary.definition: adv. 令人惊讶地, 不可思议地, 妙极地
vocabulary.context: And certainly marvelously near to being perfect, notwithstanding the shortness of the time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管时间很短，但确实非常接近完美

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自修的, 自我教育的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他自学成才，确实知道很多事情，但事实并非如此

vocabulary.definition: adj. 阳光普照的
vocabulary.context: ...comely creature - lithe, slender, trim, rounded, shapely, nimble, graceful; and once when she was standing marble-white and sun-drenched on a boulder, with her young head tilted back and her hand shading her eyes, watching the flight of a bird in the sky, I recognized that she was beautiful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...美丽的生物 - 轻盈、修长、修剪、圆润、匀称、敏捷、优雅；有一次，当她站在一块大理石白色的、沐浴在阳光下的巨石上，年轻的头向后仰，用手遮住眼睛，看着天空中一只鸟飞翔时，我意识到她是美丽的

vocabulary.definition: v. 节约, 节省, 有效地利用

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我认为还有很多东西需要学习--我希望如此；通过节约和不要操之过急，我认为它们会持续几周又几周

vocabulary.definition: adv. 眼花地, 头晕地, 轻率地

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我是如此渴望抓住每一件漂亮的东西，以至于我轻而易举地抓住它，有时距离太远，有时距离只有六英寸，但似乎有一英尺--唉，中间有刺！

vocabulary.definition: adj. 平稳的, 恬适的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果她能够安静下来并每次保持几分钟的静止，那将是一个宁静的景象

vocabulary.definition: n. 玫瑰花丛, 蔷薇花丛
vocabulary.context: ...I crept warily back, alert, watching, and ready to fly if there was occasion; and when I was come near, I parted the branches of a rose-bush and peeped through - wishing the man was about, I was looking so cunning and pretty - but the sprite was gone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...我小心翼翼地爬回来，保持警惕，观察着，并准备好在有机会时起飞；当我走近时，我拨开玫瑰丛的枝条，往里看--希望那个人就在附近，我看起来那么狡猾又漂亮--但精灵已经不见了

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这种情况下，人们会这样解析它：主格，HE；与格，HIM；占有欲，他的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 也许爱的萌芽正在他们的心中萌芽，这种爱的萌芽如此纯洁，可以在天堂里绽放，因为它无法在地球上成熟；因为女人崇拜他这样温柔的尊严，而骄傲、沉思但又善良的灵魂往往会被她这样的简单所吸引

vocabulary.definition: n. 失去勇气, 失望

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 起初，他的脸上挂着忧郁的表情，几乎是沮丧，就像一个在夜幕降临时独自走在荒凉荒凉的路上的人一样，但当他看到自己受到善意温暖的接待时，他的脸上很快就露出了笑容

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高额头的, 自以为有教养的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 孩子们的小脸从床上偷看，这里有父亲的强健体格，母亲的柔和细心的神情，有眉目清秀的青年，有崭露头角的女孩，还有还在最温暖的地方织毛衣的善良老奶奶

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个家庭位于白山峡谷，那里全年狂风凛冽，冬天无情寒冷，在风降临到萨科河谷之前，给他们的小屋带来了新鲜的恶劣天气

vocabulary.definition: adj. 合唱队的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，他没有急于向门口走去，鞭子很快就被施加了，旅行者们跳进了峡谷，仍然唱歌和大笑，尽管他们的音乐和欢乐从山的中心沉闷地回来了

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 今天，我们是地球上唯一不保证工人带薪病假或带薪产假的发达国家

vocabulary.context: It's held up as proof not just of my own flaws - of which there are many - but also as proof that the vision itself is misguided, and naïve, and that there are too many people in this town who actually benefit from partisanship and gridlock for us to ever do anything about it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它不仅被认为证明了我自己的缺陷--我的缺陷有很多--而且还证明了这种愿景本身是被误导的、天真的，而且这个镇上有太多人实际上从党派之争和僵局中受益，而我们对此却无能为力

vocabulary.definition: adv. 向北方
vocabulary.context: Major evacuation operations moved prisoners out of Auschwitz, Stutthof, and Gross-Rosen westward to Buchenwald, Flossenbürg, Dachau, and Sachsenhausen in winter 1944-1945; from Buchenwald and Flossenbürg to Dachau and Mauthausen in spring 1945; and from Sachsenhausen and Neuengamme northwards to the Baltic Sea in the last weeks of the war.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1944年至1945年冬季，大规模疏散行动将囚犯从奥斯威辛、施图托夫和格罗斯-罗森向西转移到布痕瓦尔德、弗洛森堡、达豪和萨克森豪森； 1945 年春从布痕瓦尔德 (Buchenwald) 和弗洛森堡 (Flossenbürg) 前往达豪 (Dachau) 和毛特豪森 (Mauthausen)；在战争的最后几周，从萨克森豪森和诺因加默向北到达波罗的海

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无条件地

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 德国武装部队于1945年5月7日在西部无条件投降，在东部于1945年5月9日无条件投降

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不合理地, 无理性地
vocabulary.context: (3) some SS leaders, including Himmler, believed irrationally that they could use Jewish concentration camp prisoners as hostages to bargain for a separate peace in the west that would guarantee the survival of the Nazi regime.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: (3) 包括希姆莱在内的一些党卫军领导人非理性地认为，他们可以利用犹太集中营囚犯作为人质，以在西方争取单独的和平，从而保证纳粹政权的生存

vocabulary.definition: v. (使)上岸, (使)登陆

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 按照他们的明确命令，他们射杀了数百名倒下或无法跟上行军步伐，或无法再下火车或轮船的囚犯

Brand managers should not always assume that their loudest and most active Facebook followers are the only ones getting the message.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管当专家讨论 Facebook 参与的好处时，与关注者的被动参与可能并不是最受关注的，但它仍然提供了好处，例如变得更加"受关注"。品牌经理不应总是认为他们最响亮、最活跃的 Facebook 粉丝是唯一收到信息的人

vocabulary.context: He could delay extradition, potentially for months, with court challenges, but probably could not block the process.

vocabulary.definition: adj. warrant的变形；无保证的；未经授权的；无法辩解的；不正当的
vocabulary.context: In a separate case, a federal appeals court ruled Monday against a civil-liberties advocacy group that challenged the constitutionality of the N.S.A.'s warrantless wiretapping program.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在另一起案件中，联邦上诉法院周一对一个公民自由倡导组织做出了裁决，该组织质疑国家安全局无证窃听计划的合宪性

vocabulary.definition: adj. 恶毒的, 极坏的
vocabulary.context: He "has damaged national security, our ability to track down terrorists, or those with nefarious intent, and his disclosure has not made America safer," said Representative Jim Langevin, a Rhode Island Democrat on the House Intelligence Committee.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 众议院情报委员会罗德岛州民主党众议员吉姆·朗之万表示，他"损害了国家安全、我们追查恐怖分子或具有邪恶意图的人的能力，他的披露并没有让美国变得更安全"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 长期间的, 长期存在的
vocabulary.context: The Hong Kong authorities have worked closely with United States law enforcement agencies for years and have usually accepted extradition requests under longstanding agreements, according to Regina Ip, a former secretary of security who serves in the territory's legislature.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 香港立法机关前安全局局长叶刘淑仪表示，香港当局多年来一直与美国执法机构密切合作，通常会根据长期协议接受引渡请求

vocabulary.definition: adj. 低水平的
vocabulary.context: Three years ago, the State Department significantly tightened access to its classified data, in reaction to the release of department cables to WikiLeaks by a low-level intelligence analyst, Pfc.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 三年前，美国国务院大幅收紧了对其机密数据的访问，以应对低级情报分析师 Pfc 向维基解密泄露的部门电报

vocabulary.definition: adj. 一神论的
vocabulary.context: One of the main creeds of Islam, as in all other monotheistic religions, is that there is only one God.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与所有其他一神论宗教一样，伊斯兰教的主要信条之一是只有一位上帝

vocabulary.definition: adj. 多层面的
vocabulary.context: I think Zainab hit the nail on the head: A "good" depiction for me - not just of Islam, but any religion or culture or character - is one that is nuanced and multifaceted.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我认为扎伊纳布说得一中要害：对我来说，"好的"描述--不仅是对伊斯兰教，而且是对任何宗教、文化或人物--是细致入微且多方面的

vocabulary.definition: n. 图像材料, 图示法, 肖像研究；[医] 影像学, 影像塑造术
vocabulary.context: Just to touch on what Zainab was talking about, Faiza Hussein actually wields Excalibur, so it's not so much a rehashing of the "Eastern weapon" iconography but more of a meshing of the East and West.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只是为了触及扎伊纳布所说的内容，法伊扎·侯赛因实际上挥舞着神剑，所以这与其说是对"东方武器"图像的重新整理，不如说是东西方的融合

vocabulary.definition: n. 原型
vocabulary.context: It's not about creating an archetype to represent an entire group, it's not about "getting" or understanding them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这并不是要创建一个原型来代表整个群体，也不是要"获取"或理解它们

vocabulary.definition: n. 运动迷, 影迷
vocabulary.context: DEMBY : Does being Muslim complicate your fandom?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: DEMBY：成为穆斯林会让你的粉丝群体变得复杂吗？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 做得好的, 完全煮熟的
vocabulary.context: ...is a medical doctor, and also briefly became Captain Britain for a time (the comic she appeared in), and her portrayal is well-done.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...是一名医生，还曾短暂担任过英国队长（她出现在漫画中），她的刻画非常出色

vocabulary.definition: n. 占卜官, 预言者；v. 预示, 预言
vocabulary.context: We decided to pick the brains of a few Muslim comics fans to see what this might augur for the future of Muslim representations in comics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们决定听取一些穆斯林漫画迷的意见，看看这对漫画中穆斯林形象的未来有何预示

vocabulary.definition: adj. <俚>蹩脚的没价值的讨厌的( crappy的比较级 )
vocabulary.context: I'm a person, you know, I like comics, crap food, crappier music, and I also happen to be Muslim.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我是一个人，你知道，我喜欢漫画、垃圾食品、垃圾音乐，而且我也恰好是穆斯林

vocabulary.definition: n. 短弯刀, 半月形刀, 短弯刀状物
vocabulary.context: Though I do remember one of the Arabian Knights being super awful, like wearing-a-turban-wielding-a-scimitar-on-a-magic-flying-carpet awful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然我确实记得其中一位阿拉伯骑士非常糟糕，就像在魔法飞毯上戴着头巾挥舞着弯刀一样可怕

vocabulary.definition: adv. 间歇地
vocabulary.context: Both are fringe characters, though, only appearing in the comics intermittently.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不过，两者都是边缘角色，只间歇性地出现在漫画中

vocabulary.definition: n. 广告女郎；歌舞女郎
vocabulary.context: So could the showgirls, gangsters and cowboys who populated early Hollywood fantasies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 早期好莱坞幻想中的歌舞女郎、黑帮和牛仔也可以

vocabulary.definition: n. 潜在的意思, 潜台词
vocabulary.context: The vast majority of happy characters in movies are young, good-looking and well-off, so the subtext is that those qualities all go hand-in-hand.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 电影中绝大多数快乐的角色都是年轻、英俊、富有，所以潜台词是这些品质都是齐头并进的

vocabulary.definition: n. 短帷幔, 窗帘上端的短幔
vocabulary.context: And you make your success as an individual, says rugged individual John Wayne in The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为一个人，你会取得成功，粗犷的约翰·韦恩在《射杀自由的人》中说道

vocabulary.definition: n. 参赛者, 争论者, 斗争者, 竞争者
vocabulary.context: "I coulda been a contender," laments Terry Malloy in On the Waterfront .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我本可以成为一个竞争者，"特里·马洛伊在《海滨》中感叹道

vocabulary.definition: adv. 令人毛骨悚然地
vocabulary.context: African-Americans, Asian-Americans, Latin Americans, gay Americans - the list of people who were left out of Hollywood's dreams in the first half of the last century is appallingly long.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 非裔美国人、亚裔美国人、拉丁美洲人、同性恋美国人--上世纪上半叶被排除在好莱坞梦想之外的人的名单长得惊人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 逃避现实的；n. 逃避现实者
vocabulary.context: It was their own outsider experience these Italian, Irish, German and often Jewish moviemakers were putting on screen, each optimistic, escapist fantasy a virtual American dream checklist:
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些意大利、爱尔兰、德国和犹太电影制片人将他们自己的局外人经历搬上银幕，每一个乐观、逃避现实的幻想都是一个虚拟的美国梦清单：

vocabulary.definition: n. 统舱, 掌舵, 操纵, 管理
vocabulary.context: By the time he gets to Ellis Island, traveling in steerage, he's figured out that these are false hopes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当他乘坐统舱到达埃利斯岛时，他发现这些都是虚假的希望

vocabulary.definition: n. 葡萄藤, 葡萄树, 秘密情报网
vocabulary.context: ...Charlie Chaplin's Little Tramp imagining not great wealth, but an idyllic middle-class existence, in a house with orange trees and grapevines so nearby he could pluck fruit through his window, and a cow in the backyard that came when called and filled a pitcher with fresh milk when Charlie patted its flank.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...查理·卓别林笔下的小流浪汉想象的不是巨额财富，而是田园诗般的中产阶级生活，房子里有橘子树和葡萄藤，离他很近，他可以从窗户摘水果，后院有一头牛，当查理拍拍它的侧腹时，它会在召唤时过来，给一罐新鲜牛奶装满

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 夸大的叙述, 夸张, 过分强调；[经] 多计, 高估
vocabulary.context: with images that filled them with overstated optimism about what they'd find here.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些图像让他们对在这里的发现充满了夸大的乐观情绪

vocabulary.definition: adj. 免疫抑制的
vocabulary.context: When T cell function declines, such as with increasing age or during use of immunosuppressive drugs, viral clearance is delayed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当 T 细胞功能下降时，例如随着年龄的增长或使用免疫抑制药物期间，病毒清除会延迟

vocabulary.definition: n. 丘脑下部；[医] 丘脑下部, 下丘脑
vocabulary.context: Interleukin-1 is important for developing the killer T cell response against the virus, but it also affects the part of the brain in the hypothalamus that regulates body temperature, resulting in fever and headaches.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Interleukin-1 对于产生针对病毒的杀伤性 T 细胞反应非常重要，但它也会影响下丘脑中调节体温的大脑部分，导致发烧和头痛

vocabulary.definition: adj. 上皮的；[医] 上皮的
vocabulary.context: It then travels down the respiratory tract and binds to epithelial cells lining the lung airways via specific molecules on the cell surface.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后它沿着呼吸道传播，并通过细胞表面上的特定分子与肺气道内壁的上皮细胞结合

vocabulary.definition: n. 免疫学；[化] 免疫学
vocabulary.context: I am a researcher who specializes in immunology at the University of Connecticut School of Medicine, and my laboratory focuses on how influenza infection affects the body and how our bodies combat the virus.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我是康涅狄格大学医学院的一名专门研究免疫学的研究员，我的实验室专注于流感感染如何影响身体以及我们的身体如何对抗病毒

vocabulary.definition: n. 支气管炎；[医] 支气管炎
vocabulary.context: This process creates a great deal of lung damage similar to bronchitis, which can worsen existing lung disease and make breathing difficult.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个过程会造成类似于支气管炎的大量肺部损伤，这会使现有的肺部疾病恶化并使呼吸困难

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可逆的, 可撤消的；n. 双面可用织物
vocabulary.context: Normally, this damage triggered by arrival of T cells in the lungs is reversible in a healthy person, but when it advances, it is bad news and can lead to death.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通常情况下，T细胞到达肺部引发的这种损伤对于健康人来说是可逆的，但当这种损伤进一步发展时，那就是坏消息了，可能会导致死亡

vocabulary.definition: adj. 抗病毒的；[医] 抗病毒的
vocabulary.context: Another important cytokine that fights influenza infection is something called "tumor necrosis factor alpha." This cytokine can have direct antiviral effects in the lungs, and that's good.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一种对抗流感感染的重要细胞因子是"肿瘤坏死因子α"。这种细胞因子可以在肺部产生直接的抗病毒作用，这是好事

vocabulary.definition: adj. 嗜中性的；n. 嗜中性白细胞, 嗜中性白血球
vocabulary.context: The initial immune response involves cells of the body's innate immune system, such as macrophages and neutrophils.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最初的免疫反应涉及人体先天免疫系统的细胞，例如巨噬细胞和中性粒细胞

vocabulary.definition: n. 坏死, 枯斑；[医] 坏死
vocabulary.context: Another important cytokine that fights influenza infection is something called "tumor necrosis factor alpha." This cytokine can have direct antiviral effects in the lungs, and that's good.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一种对抗流感感染的重要细胞因子是"肿瘤坏死因子α"。这种细胞因子可以在肺部产生直接的抗病毒作用，这是好事

vocabulary.definition: n. 淋巴；[医] 淋巴, 浆, 苗
vocabulary.context: When T cells specifically recognize influenza virus proteins, they then begin to proliferate in the lymph nodes around the lungs and throat.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当 T 细胞特异性识别流感病毒蛋白时，它们就会开始在肺部和喉咙周围的淋巴结中增殖

vocabulary.definition: n. 蚀本, 投资减缩；[经] 负投资, 停止投资, 减少投资
vocabulary.context: Rebecca Marchiel's book tells the story of a multiracial group of Chicagoans who fought back against redlining and disinvestment in their community.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 丽贝卡·马歇尔的书讲述了一群多种族芝加哥人反抗社区红线和撤资的故事

vocabulary.definition: n. 高速公路
vocabulary.context: The Dan Ryan Expressway, built in Chicago in 1961, was initially planned to go through Bridgeport, a majority white neighborhood and home to the city's white mayor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 丹瑞恩高速公路于 1961 年在芝加哥建成，最初计划穿过布里奇波特，这是一个以白人为主的社区，也是该市白人市长的住所

vocabulary.definition: n. 私房屋主；自己拥有住房者 (homeowner的变形)
vocabulary.context: However, Black people and other people of color have long faced barriers to homeownership.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，黑人和其他有色人种长期以来一直面临着拥有住房的障碍

vocabulary.definition: n. <美>街区房地产跌涨牟利
vocabulary.context: Some realtors and banks would engage in "blockbusting." If a Black family moved into a neighborhood, realtors would encourage neighbors to sell their houses quickly under the threat of their property values dropping.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些房地产经纪人和银行会进行"封锁"。如果一个黑人家庭搬进一个社区，房地产经纪人会鼓励邻居在房产价值下降的威胁下迅速出售他们的房子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 境况较好的
vocabulary.context: This was driven in part by better-off Black residents trying to integrate segregated neighborhoods.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这在一定程度上是由富裕的黑人居民试图整合隔离社区所推动的

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再为...筹钱, 再供...资金
vocabulary.context: The HOLC "purchased loans or mortgages from local lenders and then refinanced them back to the homeowners or the person who was paying the mortgage to make the terms easier to deal with." For example, someone who was paying $52 a month saw their mortgage payment go down to $26 a month.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: HOLC"从当地贷方购买贷款或抵押贷款，然后将其再融资给房主或支付抵押贷款的人，以使条款更容易处理。"例如，每月支付 52 美元的人发现他们的抵押贷款付款减少到每月 26 美元

vocabulary.definition: n. 私房屋主；自己拥有住房者
vocabulary.context: Meanwhile, the FHA provided insurance to banks so that if a homeowner could not pay their mortgage, the federal government ensured the bank would not lose any money.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与此同时，联邦住房管理局向银行提供保险，这样如果房主无法支付抵押贷款，联邦政府可以确保银行不会损失任何金钱

vocabulary.definition: n. 签名者, 使用手势语者
vocabulary.context: They offered contracts for over-priced houses, high payments, tough penalties for missed payments, and no guarantee that signers would own the home in the end.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们提供价格过高的房屋合同、高额付款、对拖欠付款的严厉处罚，并且不保证签字人最终会拥有房屋

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 打烙印, 诬蔑
vocabulary.context: According to Serrato, Sier, and Runes, "CHA high-rises were stigmatized by the city and the media, which portrayed them as vertical drug-ridden ganglands." This would come into play when the city began building highways to connect the white suburban dwellers to the city.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据塞拉托、西尔和鲁内斯的说法，"CHA 的高层建筑受到了城市和媒体的污名，将它们描绘成毒品泛滥的垂直黑帮。"当城市开始修建高速公路将白人郊区居民与城市连接起来时，这一点就会发挥作用

vocabulary.definition: n. 盗用, 挪用, 侵占；[经] 贪污, 盗用(公款), 监守自盗
vocabulary.context: A judge sentenced Husband to the restitution center in 2015 to pay off almost $13,000 she owed from an embezzlement conviction in 2009.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一名法官于 2015 年将丈夫判处归还中心，以偿还她因 2009 年贪污罪而欠下的近 13,000 美元

vocabulary.definition: n. 不支付
vocabulary.context: Fearing she would be thrown in prison for nonpayment, she stopped reporting.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于担心自己会因未付款而被关进监狱，她停止了报道

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再拿到手, 取回；[经] 重新获得
vocabulary.context: One June morning in 2007, while Husband worked at a payday loan store near Biloxi on the Gulf Coast, she looked at a surveillance monitor and watched as the dealership repossessed her white Chevrolet Suburban.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2007 年 6 月的一个早晨，当丈夫在墨西哥湾沿岸比洛克西附近的一家发薪日贷款店工作时，她看着监控监视器，看到经销商收回了她的白色雪佛兰 Suburban

vocabulary.definition: n. 代理权, 经销权；[法] 商品经销特许权, 代理商
vocabulary.context: One June morning in 2007, while Husband worked at a payday loan store near Biloxi on the Gulf Coast, she looked at a surveillance monitor and watched as the dealership repossessed her white Chevrolet Suburban.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2007 年 6 月的一个早晨，当丈夫在墨西哥湾沿岸比洛克西附近的一家发薪日贷款店工作时，她看着监控监视器，看到经销商收回了她的白色雪佛兰 Suburban

vocabulary.definition: n. 付薪水的支票, 薪水
vocabulary.context: The corrections department took her paychecks, she said, giving her back just $10 a week - all in quarters - so she could buy things like soap and deodorant.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说，惩教部门拿走了她的工资，每周只退给她 10 美元--全部按季度退还--这样她就可以买肥皂和除臭剂等东西

vocabulary.definition: n. 打印输出；[计] 印出
vocabulary.context: Inmates can get printouts showing debits and credits in their accounts, which the state calls a monthly balance sheet.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 囚犯可以获得显示其账户借方和贷方的打印输出，该州将其称为每月资产负债表

vocabulary.definition: n. 鲶鱼
vocabulary.context: The inmates work at low-wage, sometimes dangerous jobs, such as slaughtering chickens or gutting catfish at processing plants.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 囚犯从事低工资、有时甚至是危险的工作，例如在加工厂屠宰鸡或去除鲶鱼的内脏

vocabulary.definition: n. 逾期债款, 欠帐；[经] 延迟, 积欠, 尾数
vocabulary.context: Though in arrears on fines and court fees, many didn't need to pay restitution at all - at least 20 percent of them were convicted of drug possession.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管拖欠罚款和法庭费用，许多人根本不需要支付赔偿金--其中至少 20% 的人因持有毒品而被定罪

vocabulary.definition: n. 杂务工, 手巧的人, 水手
vocabulary.context: Private citizens hire them to work as handymen and landscapers at their homes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 私人公民雇用他们在家中担任勤杂工和园艺师

vocabulary.definition: n. 监狱, 收容所, 教养所, 宗教裁判所；adj. 用作感化的, 应处监禁的, 教养所的, 收容所的
vocabulary.context: Mississippi then founded the state penitentiary known as Parchman Farm, which was modeled after a slave plantation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 密西西比州随后建立了名为帕奇曼农场的州立监狱，该农场仿照奴隶种植园而建

vocabulary.definition: n. 权宜之计, 补缺者；adj. 暂时的, 权宜的
vocabulary.context: Let us admit frankly that it would be only a stopgap.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 让我们坦白承认，这只是权宜之计

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未组织起来的, 没有组织的, 没编成的；[医] 无结构的, 无器官的
vocabulary.context: These unhappy times call for the building of plans that rest upon the forgotten, the unorganized but the indispensable units of economic power, for plans like those of 1917 that build from the bottom up and not from the top down, that put their faith once more in the forgotten man at the bottom of the economic pyramid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些不愉快的时代要求制定以被遗忘者为基础的计划，这些计划是无组织的，但却是不可或缺的经济力量单位，像1917年那样的计划是自下而上而不是自上而下制定的，这些计划再次将他们的信心寄托在经济金字塔底部被遗忘的人身上

vocabulary.definition: adj. 胆怯的, 胆小的, 羞怯的
vocabulary.context: Success then was due to a leadership whose vision carried beyond the timorous and futile gesture of sending a tiny army of 150,000 trained soldiers and the regular navy to the aid of our allies.

vocabulary.definition: n. 法典编纂；[经] 编纂, 整理, 编号
vocabulary.context: Roberts also praised Napoleon's "rational and efficient local administration, an end to rural banditry, the encouragement of science and the arts, the abolition of feudalism and the greatest codification of laws since the fall of the Roman Empire."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 罗伯茨还赞扬拿破仑"合理而高效的地方行政管理、结束农村土匪行为、鼓励科学和艺术、废除封建制度以及自罗马帝国灭亡以来最伟大的法律编纂"

vocabulary.definition: adj. (关于)扩张主义的, 赞同扩张主义的；n. 扩张主义者, 领土扩张论者
vocabulary.context: Undeterred as the aggressor and expansionist, in 1808, Napoleon invaded the Iberian Peninsula and installed his brother Joseph as the King of Spain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1808年，作为侵略者和扩张主义者，拿破仑入侵伊比利亚半岛，并拥立他的兄弟约瑟夫为西班牙国王

vocabulary.definition: n. 宽容, 默认, 信仰自由；[医] 耐量; 耐受性, 耐力
vocabulary.context: For example, Andrew Roberts, a prominent British journalist and historian, wrote that "The ideas that underpin our modern world-meritocracy, equality before the law, property rights, religious toleration, modern secular education, sound finances, and so on-were championed, consolidated, codified and geographically extended by Napoleon."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，英国著名记者和历史学家安德鲁·罗伯茨写道："支撑我们现代世界的理念--精英政治、法律面前人人平等、财产权、宗教宽容、现代世俗教育、健全的财政等等--都是拿破仑所倡导、巩固、编纂和地理扩展的。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生就的, 生来的
vocabulary.context: He was a military paragon, a natural-born warrior and a ruthless conqueror bent on controlling ever more nations and territory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他是一位军事典范、一位天生的战士和一位决心控制更多国家和领土的残酷征服者

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无空隙地; 无停顿地

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他是一位出色的政治家，从左翼革命者无缝转变为独裁者

vocabulary.definition: n. 土匪行为, 匪帮；[法] 有组织的抢劫, 盗匪活动, 窃盗罪

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1795年，他击败了保皇党起义，赢得了他的第一场战斗，同年他又与奥地利和意大利的军队作战

vocabulary.definition: adj. (封建)领主的；庄园主的；贵族气派的, 高傲的, 不可一世的
vocabulary.context: He liberalized property laws, ended seigneurial dues, abolished the guild of merchants and craftsmen to facilitate entrepreneurship, legalized divorce, closed the Jewish ghettos and made Jews equal to everyone else.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他放宽了财产法，终止了领主税，废除了商人和工匠行会以促进创业，使离婚合法化，关闭了犹太人聚居区，并使犹太人与其他人平等

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 给...加冕

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1804年，在打这场战争的过程中，拿破仑说服教皇庇护七世加冕为法国皇帝，从而登上了政治的最终阶梯

Taciturn, unpretentious, and only 5 feet 8 inches tall, Grant "often dressed in a plain blue suit and a felt hat" without any gold braid and epaulettes to mark his rank.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...仅在酗酒和骑马时；以平均成绩毕业；并且"是一位看起来最不军事的军官。"格兰特沉默寡言、朴实无华，身高只有 5 英尺 8 英寸，"经常穿着朴素的蓝色西装，戴着毡帽"，没有任何金色辫子和肩章来标记他的等级

vocabulary.definition: adj. 平凡的, 单调的, 令人厌烦的；n. 平凡, 单调, 无聊；v.i. 单调令味地进行
vocabulary.context: The well-connected Philadelphian won more promotions in the Seminole wars in Florida while Grant, despite his reputation as a fierce warrior, was assigned to a humdrum post in California.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这位人脉广泛的费城人在佛罗里达州的塞米诺尔战争中赢得了更多的晋升，而格兰特尽管享有猛烈战士的声誉，却被分配到加利福尼亚州担任一个单调的职位

Taciturn, unpretentious, and only 5 feet 8 inches tall, Grant "often dressed in a plain blue suit and a felt hat" without any gold braid and epaulettes to mark his rank.

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可耻的, 丢脸的, 不道德的, 淫猥的, 猥亵的, 不体面的
vocabulary.context: history, it put to rest the shameful institution of slavery, but it cost almost one million lives.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 历史上，它结束了可耻的奴隶制制度，但它却夺去了近一百万人的生命

vocabulary.definition: adv. 呆板地, 顽固地, 僵硬地
vocabulary.context: When Grant insisted on "unconditional surrender," Pemberton stiffly replied, "I can assure you, sir, you will bury many more of your men before you will enter Vicksburg."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当格兰特坚持"无条件投降"时，彭伯顿生硬地回答道："我可以向您保证，先生，在您进入维克斯堡之前，您会埋葬更多的人。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 装卸工, 码头工, 脚夫；v.i. 装载货物；v.t. 装货于
vocabulary.context: In the next four years, according to Benjamin Quarles, one of Douglass' biographers, a young Douglass "took whatever jobs that came his way, sawing wood, digging cellars, shoveling coal, blowing bellows, and stevedoring," but he also began attending and increasingly speaking up at meetings of abolitionists - whites and blacks who were opposed to slavery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在接下来的四年里，根据道格拉斯的传记作者之一本杰明·夸尔斯的说法，年轻的道格拉斯"做了任何他能做的工作，锯木头、挖地窖、铲煤、吹风箱和装卸"，但他也开始参加废奴主义者--反对奴隶制的白人和黑人的会议，并越来越多地在会议上发言

vocabulary.definition: n. 小轮, 滑轮；v.i. 屈从, 讨好
vocabulary.context: In his newspaper, he bluntly accused Lincoln of "admitting the right to hold men as slaves" and after Lincoln's election predicted that "Whoever lives through the next four years will see [him] and his Administration attacked more bitterly for their pro-slavery truckling, than for doing any anti-slavery work."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他的报纸上，他直言不讳地指责林肯"承认将人类视为奴隶的权利"，并在林肯当选后预测"无论谁活过未来四年，都会看到[他]和他的政府因支持奴隶制的卑鄙行为而受到比任何反奴隶制工作更严厉的攻击。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不合逻辑的, 无缘由的；[法] 不合逻辑的, 无条理的, 说不通的
vocabulary.context: seems to possess an ever-increasing passion for making himself appear silly and ridiculous, if nothing worse." Lincoln's statements were not only "illogical and unfair," he added, but also showed his racism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 似乎越来越热衷于让自己显得愚蠢和可笑，甚至更糟。"他补充说，林肯的言论不仅"不合逻辑和不公平"，而且还表现出了他的种族主义

vocabulary.definition: n. 胆小, 胆怯, 羞怯
vocabulary.context: He was now addressing large audiences, and he gained prominence by boldly speaking up on such sensitive issues as the church leaders' timidity on slavery, demanding its abolition in the federal District of Columbia, and protesting the annexation of Texas as a slave state.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他现在正在向大批听众发表讲话，并通过大胆地谈论教会领袖对奴隶制的胆怯、要求在哥伦比亚联邦特区废除奴隶制以及抗议将德克萨斯州吞并为奴隶制等敏感问题而获得了声望

vocabulary.definition: n. 新当选的总统
vocabulary.context: Even before the Southern states seceded from the Union, Douglass opened a dialogue with the president-elect by urging him in his newspaper, the Douglass Monthly, to "enlist Negroes," freed and slave, into a 75,000-strong "liberating army," and send it to the South to free the slaves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 甚至在南方各州脱离联邦之前，道格拉斯就与当选总统展开了对话，在他的报纸《道格拉斯月刊》上敦促他"招募获得自由的黑人"和奴隶，组成一支75,000人的"解放军"，并将其派往南方解放奴隶

vocabulary.definition: adj. 叛逆的, 不忠的, 背信弃义的；[法] 叛逆的, 背叛的, 不忠的
vocabulary.context: "I think there is great danger," Lincoln wrote the general, "...that liberating slaves of traitorous owners will alarm our Southern Union friends, and turn them against us..."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我认为存在巨大的危险，"林肯在给将军的信中写道，"......解放叛徒奴隶主的奴隶将会惊动我们南方联盟的朋友，并让他们反对我们......"

vocabulary.definition: adv. 坦率地, 率直地
vocabulary.context: In his newspaper, he bluntly accused Lincoln of "admitting the right to hold men as slaves" and after Lincoln's election predicted that "Whoever lives through the next four years will see [him] and his Administration attacked more bitterly for their pro-slavery truckling, than for doing any anti-slavery work."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他的报纸上，他直言不讳地指责林肯"承认将人类视为奴隶的权利"，并在林肯当选后预测"无论谁活过未来四年，都会看到[他]和他的政府因支持奴隶制的卑鄙行为而受到比任何反奴隶制工作更严厉的攻击。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 合并, 附加, 附加物；[法] 兼并, 侵吞, 附加物
vocabulary.context: He was now addressing large audiences, and he gained prominence by boldly speaking up on such sensitive issues as the church leaders' timidity on slavery, demanding its abolition in the federal District of Columbia, and protesting the annexation of Texas as a slave state.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他现在正在向大批听众发表讲话，并通过大胆地谈论教会领袖对奴隶制的胆怯、要求在哥伦比亚联邦特区废除奴隶制以及抗议将德克萨斯州吞并为奴隶制等敏感问题而获得了声望

vocabulary.definition: n. 传记作者
vocabulary.context: In the next four years, according to Benjamin Quarles, one of Douglass' biographers, a young Douglass "took whatever jobs that came his way, sawing wood, digging cellars, shoveling coal, blowing bellows, and stevedoring," but he also began attending and increasingly speaking up at meetings of abolitionists - whites and blacks who were opposed to slavery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在接下来的四年里，根据道格拉斯的传记作者之一本杰明·夸尔斯的说法，年轻的道格拉斯"做了任何他能做的工作，锯木头、挖地窖、铲煤、吹风箱和装卸"，但他也开始参加废奴主义者--反对奴隶制的白人和黑人的会议，并越来越多地在会议上发言

vocabulary.definition: adj. 由大西洋岸至太平洋岸的
vocabulary.context: One of the greatest engineering projects of the 19th Century, the new coast-to-coast link did what Lincoln hoped it would do; it dramatically speeded up continent-wide trade, transportation, and settlement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为 19 世纪最伟大的工程项目之一，新的东西海岸连接线实现了林肯所希望的目标；它极大地加速了整个大陆的贸易、运输和结算

vocabulary.definition: adj. 叛逆的, 谋反的, 不忠的；[法] 犯叛国罪的, 叛国的, 叛逆的, 不忠的
vocabulary.context: After the Rebels fired at Fort Sumter on April 12, 1861, Lincoln almost immediately called out state militias; expanded the Union's Army and Navy; blockaded southern ports; closed post offices to treasonable mail; and ordered the arrest and military detention of suspected traitors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1861 年 4 月 12 日，叛军向萨姆特堡开火后，林肯几乎立即召集了州民兵；扩大联邦陆军和海军；封锁南部港口；关闭邮局以接收叛国邮件；并下令逮捕并军事拘留涉嫌叛徒

vocabulary.definition: n. 诚实, 正直
vocabulary.context: A similar act of probity and courage would be hard to find in the annals of American politics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在美国政治史上很难找到类似的正直和勇气行为

vocabulary.context: And they are strangely anachronistic in one way: Having been brought up in the prairies where industrialization never penetrated, they have jumped with no transition from the old agrarian, self-containing farm where nearly everything used...
