vocabulary.definition: n. 破马车, 老爷汽车, 破旧不堪的东西；adj. 破旧的
vocabulary.context: To the casual traveler on the great highways the movements of the migrants are mysterious if they are seen at all, for suddenly the roads will be filled with open rattletrap cars loaded with children and with dirty bedding, with fire-blackened cooking utensils.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于高速公路上的休闲旅行者来说，即使看到移民的行踪，他们的行动也是神秘的，因为突然间，道路上就会挤满了开着的响尾蛇汽车，车上载着孩子、肮脏的床上用品和被火熏黑的炊具

vocabulary.definition: n. 外国人, 外地人
vocabulary.context: Arriving in a district they find the dislike always meted out by the resident to the foreigner, the outlander.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到达一个地区后，他们发现居民总是对外国人、外地人表示厌恶

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无特权的, 享受不到特权的
vocabulary.context: And they have come into the country where, because of the movement necessary to make a living, they are not allowed any vote whatever, but are rather considered a properly unprivileged class.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们来到了这个国家，由于谋生所需的运动，他们不被允许任何投票，而是被认为是一个真正的无特权阶层

vocabulary.definition: n. 蹲着的人, 擅自占用土地或房屋者；v.i. 涉水而过
vocabulary.context: On side roads and near rivers where there is little travel the squalid, filthy squatters' camp will have been set up, and the orchards will be filled with pickers and cutters and driers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在人迹罕至的小路和河流附近，将搭建肮脏肮脏的棚户区，果园里将挤满采摘者、切割者和烘干者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 每年落叶的, 非永久性的；[医] 脱落的
vocabulary.context: North of San Francisco the produce is of grapes, deciduous fruits and hops.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 旧金山北部出产葡萄、落叶水果和啤酒花

vocabulary.definition: n. 干燥机, 干燥剂；[化] 干燥剂; 催干剂; 干燥设备; 干燥器; 烘缸
vocabulary.context: On side roads and near rivers where there is little travel the squalid, filthy squatters' camp will have been set up, and the orchards will be filled with pickers and cutters and driers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在人迹罕至的小路和河流附近，将搭建肮脏肮脏的棚户区，果园里将挤满采摘者、切割者和烘干者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 漫游的, 徘徊的, 流浪的, 蜿蜒的；n. 闲逛, 流浪, 离题, 胡言乱语
vocabulary.context: Let us see what kind of people they are, where they come from, and the routes of their wanderings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 让我们看看他们是什么样的人，他们来自哪里，以及他们漂泊的路线

vocabulary.definition: adv. 缓慢地, 迟, 迟延地
vocabulary.context: All resources having been used to get to the field, the migrants could not move on; they stayed and starved until government aid tardily was found for them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到达现场的所有资源已被使用，移民无法继续前行；他们留下来挨饿，直到政府迟迟没有为他们找到援助

vocabulary.definition: n. 外表, 伪装, 假象, 少量
vocabulary.context: For if this is true, then California must depart from the semblance of democratic government that remains here.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果这是真的，那么加州就必须背离这里仍然存在的民主政府的外表

vocabulary.definition: adj. 血友病的；n. 血友病患者
vocabulary.context: For a hemophiliac like Alexei, this would have only caused him more suffering.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于像阿列克谢这样的血友病患者来说，这只会给他带来更多的痛苦

vocabulary.definition: n. 催眠术, 催眠状态；[医] 催眠状态, 催眠术
vocabulary.context: Other sources suggest that Rasputin was adept at hypnotism or Siberian folk medicine.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他消息来源表明拉斯普京擅长催眠术或西伯利亚民间医学

vocabulary.definition: n. 催眠术士；[医] 催眠术士
vocabulary.context: Some speculated that the monk was a charlatan, a hypnotist, or even an occultist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有人推测这位和尚是江湖骗子、催眠师，甚至是神秘主义者

vocabulary.definition: n. 神秘学者,术士
vocabulary.context: Some speculated that the monk was a charlatan, a hypnotist, or even an occultist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有人推测这位和尚是江湖骗子、催眠师，甚至是神秘主义者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 沙皇的, 沙皇式的, 专制的, 沙皇时代的, 沙皇派的, 拥护沙皇制度的；n. 沙皇的支持者, 沙皇制度的支持者
vocabulary.context: However, one thing is certain: his scandalous presence in the imperial court helped discredit the tsarist government and contributed to the decline of the Romanovs in the wake of the Russian Revolution.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，有一件事是肯定的：他在宫廷中的丑闻使沙皇政府名誉扫地，并导致罗曼诺夫王朝在俄罗斯革命后衰落

vocabulary.definition: n. 传闻, 谣言
vocabulary.context: What little is known about him is clouded by hearsay, rumors, and legend.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人们对他知之甚少，充满了道听途说、谣言和传说

vocabulary.definition: n. 沙皇皇后, 女沙皇
vocabulary.context: In 1905, he was introduced to Nicholas II and Alexandra Feodorovna, tsar and tsarina of Russia.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1905年，他被介绍给俄罗斯沙皇尼古拉二世和亚历山德拉·费奥多罗芙娜

vocabulary.definition: n. 逊位, 弃权, 辞职
vocabulary.context: This eight-day period of unrest, known as the February Revolution, resulted in Nicholas II's abdication and the end of the Romanov dynasty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这段为期八天的动乱时期，被称为二月革命，导致尼古拉二世退位和罗曼诺夫王朝的结束

vocabulary.definition: n. 庸医；[医] 庸医, 江湖医
vocabulary.context: Some speculated that the monk was a charlatan, a hypnotist, or even an occultist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有人推测这位和尚是江湖骗子、催眠师，甚至是神秘主义者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 蓬乱的, 未被照管好的
vocabulary.context: Despite his unkempt appearance, Rasputin was a charismatic man.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管拉斯普京外表蓬头垢面，但他却是一个有魅力的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 前夫
vocabulary.context: But then Stant confessed and told the FBI why he had carried out the attack; he had been hired by Tonya Harding's ex-husband, Jeff Gillooly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但随后斯坦坦白了，并告诉联邦调查局他实施袭击的原因；他是托尼亚·哈丁（Tonya Harding）的前夫杰夫·吉卢利（Jeff Gillooly）雇用的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 适合低预算的; 根据低预算制定的
vocabulary.context: She appeared as a judge on a handful of skating television shows and acted in a few low-budget films.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她在一些滑冰电视节目中担任评委，并出演了一些低成本电影

vocabulary.definition: n. 受害人；牺牲品；牺牲者 (victim的变形)
vocabulary.context: In tabloids and news features, Kerrigan and Harding found themselves the subjects of a universal story about competition, ambition, victimhood, and justice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在小报和新闻专题中，克里根和哈丁发现自己成为了关于竞争、野心、受害者和正义的普遍故事的主题

vocabulary.definition: n. 搜捕；[法] 搜索逃犯
vocabulary.context: A manhunt ensued, and as everyone searched for an offender to blame for the attack, the eyes of the nation turned to Kerrigan's teammate and top competitor: Tonya Harding.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随后进行了搜捕，当每个人都在寻找对这次袭击负责的罪犯时，全国的目光转向了凯里根的队友和顶级竞争对手：托尼亚·哈丁

vocabulary.definition: n. 职业杀手
vocabulary.context: She told news reporters that she had been terrified when she first got word of the incident, worried that the hitman might come after her, too.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她告诉新闻记者，当她第一次得知这起事件时，她感到很害怕，担心杀手也可能会追杀她

vocabulary.definition: v. 电视广播；n. 电视广播
vocabulary.context: Their event is still one of the most-watched telecasts in American history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的活动仍然是美国历史上收视率最高的电视转播之一

vocabulary.definition: adj. 令人生畏的, 令人毛骨悚然的
vocabulary.context: The cameraman didn't plan on following her - not until he heard what he would later describe as "blood-curdling screams." He raced into the corridor, camera still rolling, and found Kerrigan curled on the floor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 摄影师并不打算跟踪她--直到他听到了他后来描述的"令人毛骨悚然的尖叫声"。他冲进走廊，镜头仍在滚动，发现凯瑞甘蜷缩在地板上

vocabulary.definition: adj. 杂凑的, 不连贯的, 爱打架的, 坚决的
vocabulary.context: It was the perfect opportunity for the media to complete the caricatures they had begun building for each woman: Tonya Harding, the scrappy, disadvantaged athlete who fought for everything she thought she deserved; and Nancy Kerrigan, the elegant, natural performer who had now, in the media's eyes, become a victim of ruthless ambition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是媒体完成他们开始为每位女性打造的漫画的绝佳机会：托尼娅·哈丁（Tonya Harding），一位斗志旺盛、处于不利地位的运动员，为她认为自己应得的一切而奋斗；南希·克里根 (Nancy Kerrigan) 是一位优雅、自然的表演者，但现在在媒体眼中，她成了无情野心的受害者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 前进着的；n. 进取心, 放行信号
vocabulary.context: Gillooly maintained that Harding had not only known about the attack, she had given the go-ahead to carry it out.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 吉卢利坚称哈丁不仅知道这次袭击，而且还批准了实施袭击

vocabulary.definition: n. 小报, 小型画报, 药片, 文摘；adj. 轰动性的, 扼要的, 缩略的, 小报式的
vocabulary.context: In tabloids and news features, Kerrigan and Harding found themselves the subjects of a universal story about competition, ambition, victimhood, and justice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在小报和新闻专题中，克里根和哈丁发现自己成为了关于竞争、野心、受害者和正义的普遍故事的主题

vocabulary.definition: n. 幻灭,觉醒
vocabulary.context: Bruce Springsteen's "Born in the U.S.A." is about the disillusionment of veterans returning home to a country that neglects them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀的《出生在美国》讲述的是退伍军人回到忽视他们的国家后的幻灭

vocabulary.definition: n. 反建制；反政府情绪；adj. 反正统的；反对现有权力结构的
vocabulary.context: It was made up of hippies, student activists, feminists, civil rights activists, anti-war activists, and other groups who were considered anti-establishment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它由嬉皮士、学生活动家、女权主义者、民权活动家、反战活动家和其他被认为反建制的团体组成

vocabulary.definition: n. 反主流文化
vocabulary.context: These songs were anthems for the counterculture movement, which was at the forefront of activism in the 1960s and 1970s.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些歌曲是反主流文化运动的赞歌，该运动处于 20 世纪 60 年代和 1970 年代激进主义的最前沿

vocabulary.definition: v.t. (由现会员)选为, 指定
vocabulary.context: The American colonists eventually co-opted this song and changed the lyrics to celebrate revolutionary figures like George Washington.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国殖民者最终选择了这首歌并更改了歌词以庆祝乔治·华盛顿等革命人物

vocabulary.definition: n. 牧歌, 田园诗；adj. 牧人的, 田园生活的, 牧师的, 田园式的
vocabulary.context: "Pastoral scene of the gallant South / the bulging eyes and the twisted mouth / Scent of magnolias sweet and fresh / Then the sudden smell of burning flesh"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "江南田园风光/凸眼歪嘴/木兰香甜清新/然后突然闻到肉烧焦的味道"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使清醒
vocabulary.context: Young people in particular felt that they had been sold a big lie about their futures, and millions of disenchanted Americans found their voice through song.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 年轻人尤其感到他们被卖掉了关于自己未来的弥天大谎，数以百万计的失望的美国人通过歌曲找到了自己的声音

vocabulary.definition: n. 六十( sixty的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: ...performed a distorted rendition of "The Star-Spangled Banner" at Woodstock; this performance was an ode to the promises of the early sixties corrupted by war and social strife.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...在伍德斯托克音乐节扭曲演绎了《星条旗永不落》；这场表演是对六十年代初期因战争和社会冲突而腐化的承诺的颂歌

vocabulary.definition: adj. 残暴的, 凶恶的
vocabulary.context: In the song, Holiday's rich, mournful voice laments the atrocious murders of African Americans in the South.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在歌曲中，霍乐迪用浑厚而悲伤的声音哀叹南方非裔美国人被残暴的谋杀

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无害地；无毒地
vocabulary.context: They began innocuously: "Would you like to be famous?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们无伤大雅地开始说："你想出名吗？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 异性的, 异性爱的；n. 异性恋的人
vocabulary.context: A heterosexual man and woman enter the lab through separate doors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一名异性恋男子和一名女子通过不同的门进入实验室

vocabulary.definition: n. 信息素( pheromone的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: Science tells us biology matters; our pheromones and hormones do a lot of work behind the scenes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学告诉我们生物学很重要；我们的信息素和荷尔蒙在幕后做了很多工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 角膜；[医] 角膜
vocabulary.context: The sentiment associated with the eye fell away and I was struck by its astounding biological reality: the spherical nature of the eyeball, the visible musculature of the iris and the smooth wet glass of the cornea.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与眼睛相关的情感消失了，我被它令人震惊的生物现实所震惊：眼球的球形性质、虹膜的可见肌肉组织和角膜光滑的湿玻璃

vocabulary.definition: adj. 完全清醒的, 精明的, 机警的；n. 低顶宽边软毡帽
vocabulary.context: The night was warm and I was wide-awake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 夜晚温暖，我完全清醒

vocabulary.definition: adj. 球的, 球面的, 球状的, 天体的；[医] 球形的, 球状的
vocabulary.context: The sentiment associated with the eye fell away and I was struck by its astounding biological reality: the spherical nature of the eyeball, the visible musculature of the iris and the smooth wet glass of the cornea.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与眼睛相关的情感消失了，我被它令人震惊的生物现实所震惊：眼球的球形性质、虹膜的可见肌肉组织和角膜光滑的湿玻璃

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 统治, 威压, 打败, 驳回；[法] 否决, 驳回, 批驳
vocabulary.context: Each time I thought of leaving, my heart overruled my brain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每当我想到离开时，我的心就战胜了我的大脑

vocabulary.definition: n. 亲密, 熟悉, 亲昵行为(言语)；[法] 亲近的行为, 亲密, 隐私
vocabulary.context: Ours was the kind of accelerated intimacy I remembered from summer camp, staying up all night with a new friend, exchanging the details of our short lives.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们的关系是我在夏令营中记忆中的那种加速的亲密关系，和一个新朋友彻夜不眠，交换我们短暂生活的细节

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 预先决定, 预先查明；[经] 预定的, 先定的
vocabulary.context: In some jurisdictions, prosecutors and defendants can work with judges to predetermine what sentence the defendants will get if the defendants accept plea bargains.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一些司法管辖区，检察官和被告可以与法官合作，预先确定如果被告接受辩诉交易，被告将得到什么判决

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 违例出牌, 食言, 毁约；v.t. 否认, 放弃, 拒绝；n. 出牌违例
vocabulary.context: If a prosecutor reneges on plea bargains, defendants may seek relief from the judge.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果检察官违背辩诉交易，被告可以向法官寻求救济

vocabulary.definition: adj. 强制的, 强压的, 强迫的；[法] 强制的, 强迫的, 强行的
vocabulary.context: Others argue that plea bargains are too coercive and undermine important constitutional rights.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他人则认为辩诉交易过于强制性，损害了重要的宪法权利

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 溜掉, 逃避, 推卸；v.t. 逃避
vocabulary.context: Some commentators oppose plea bargains, as they feel that plea bargains allow defendants to shirk responsibility for the crimes they have committed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些评论家反对辩诉交易，因为他们认为辩诉交易允许被告逃避对其所犯罪行的责任

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 向下看, 了望, 监督, 偷看到；[法] 监察, 监督, 俯瞰
vocabulary.context: These agreements allow prosecutors to focus their time and resources on other cases, and reduce the number of trials that judges need to oversee.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些协议使检察官能够将时间和资源集中在其他案件上，并减少法官需要监督的审判数量

vocabulary.definition: n. 特许, 让步, 认可；[经] 核准, 许可, 特殊(权)
vocabulary.context: Plea bargains are agreements between defendants and prosecutors in which defendants agree to plead guilty to some or all of the charges against them in exchange for concessions from the prosecutors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 辩诉交易是被告与检察官之间达成的协议，其中被告同意对针对他们的部分或全部指控认罪，以换取检察官的让步

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自动的, 自愿的, 故意的, 志愿的, 自发的；n. 自愿行动, 志愿者, 自由调
vocabulary.context: The Supreme Court, however, has held that defendants' guilty pleas must be voluntary, and that defendants may only plead guilty if they know the consequences of doing so.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，最高法院认为被告人认罪必须是自愿的，被告人只有在知道认罪后果的情况下才可以认罪

vocabulary.definition: n. 实行者, 告发者, 公诉人；[法] 原告, 起诉人, 检举人
vocabulary.context: Plea bargains are agreements between defendants and prosecutors in which defendants agree to plead guilty to some or all of the charges against them in exchange for concessions from the prosecutors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 辩诉交易是被告与检察官之间达成的协议，其中被告同意对针对他们的部分或全部指控认罪，以换取检察官的让步

vocabulary.definition: n. 罪犯, 犯人, 刑事；adj. 犯了罪的, 刑事的, 有罪的
vocabulary.context: Many successful criminal prosecutions in the United States end not with jury trials, but with plea bargains.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国许多成功的刑事起诉不是以陪审团审判结束，而是以辩诉交易结束

vocabulary.definition: n. 超自我
vocabulary.context: According to Freud, humans are not in control of the everyday decisions they make, but that they are completely controlled by three sections of the unconscious mind: the id, the ego, and the superego.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据弗洛伊德的观点，人类无法控制自己所做的日常决定，而是完全受无意识思维的三个部分控制：本我、自我和超我

vocabulary.definition: n. 自娱, 自我满足
vocabulary.context: The superego strives to act in a socially appropriate manner, whereas the id just wants instant self-gratification.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 超我努力以适合社会的方式行事，而本我只想要即时的自我满足

vocabulary.definition: n. 精神疗法, 心理疗法；[医] 精神疗法, 心理疗法
vocabulary.context: He believed that most of what drives us is buried in our subconscious brains, and that the only way to access this part is through intense psychotherapy with a trained psychiatrist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他相信，驱动我们的大部分力量都埋藏在我们的潜意识大脑中，而进入这部分的唯一方法是通过训练有素的精神科医生的强烈心理治疗

vocabulary.definition: n. 不正当的举止, 不规矩的行为；[法] 行为不端, 品行不良, 不正当的举止
vocabulary.context: According to Freud, "The Super-ego can be thought of as a type of conscience that punishes misbehavior with feelings of guilt," working in contradiction to the id.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据弗洛伊德的说法，"超我可以被认为是一种良心，它用内疚感来惩罚不当行为"，与本我相矛盾

vocabulary.definition: adj. 下意识的, 潜在意识的；[医] 阈下的, 限下的
vocabulary.context: Unconscious phenomena include repressed feelings, automatic skills, subliminal perceptions, thoughts, habits, and automatic reactions, and possibly also complexes, hidden phobias and desires.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 无意识现象包括压抑的情感、自动技能、潜意识知觉、思想、习惯和自动反应，还可能包括情结、隐藏的恐惧症和欲望

vocabulary.definition: n. 潜在意识, 下意识；adj. 下意识的, 潜意识的
vocabulary.context: The second part of the human subconscious is what Freud called the ego: the rational part of our mind.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人类潜意识的第二部分是弗洛伊德所说的自我：我们心灵的理性部分

vocabulary.definition: adj. 审判的
vocabulary.context: Conversely, if a person has an overly dominant superego, he or she may live an overly ordered, rigid life, may ignore their own wants and needs, or may be too judgmental of others.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，如果一个人有过度主导的超我，他或她可能会过着过于有序、僵化的生活，可能会忽视自己的需求，或者可能对他人过于评判

vocabulary.definition: n. 令人满足的事(或物), 满足, 喜悦；[医] 愿望满足, 满意
vocabulary.context: Freud believed that the id acts according to the "pleasure principle" - the psychic force that motivates the tendency to seek immediate gratification of any impulse.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 弗洛伊德认为，本我根据"快乐原则"行事--这种精神力量激发了寻求任何冲动的立即满足的倾向

vocabulary.definition: n. 相属关系；[经] 归属性
vocabulary.context: After physiological and safety needs are fulfilled, the third level of human needs is interpersonal and involves feelings of belongingness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在满足生理和安全需求之后，人类的第三个层次的需求是人际需求，涉及归属感

vocabulary.definition: adj. 变化的, 变形的, 新陈代谢的；[医] [新陈]代谢的
vocabulary.context: Air, water, and food are metabolic requirements for survival in all animals, including humans.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 空气、水和食物是包括人类在内的所有动物生存的代谢需求

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我实现
vocabulary.context: In 1945, Maslow wrote, "what a man can be, he must be." This quotation forms the basis of Maslow's theory about the universal need of self-actualization - the idea that each person has a basic need to reach their full potential.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1945年，马斯洛写道："一个人能够成为什么样的人，他就必须成为什么样的人。"这句话构成了马斯洛关于自我实现的普遍需要理论的基础--每个人都有充分发挥潜力的基本需要

vocabulary.definition: n. 引用语, 语录, 牌价, 报价；[化] 报价
vocabulary.context: In 1945, Maslow wrote, "what a man can be, he must be." This quotation forms the basis of Maslow's theory about the universal need of self-actualization - the idea that each person has a basic need to reach their full potential.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1945年，马斯洛写道："一个人能够成为什么样的人，他就必须成为什么样的人。"这句话构成了马斯洛关于自我实现的普遍需要理论的基础--每个人都有充分发挥潜力的基本需要

vocabulary.definition: n. 心腹朋友, 知己
vocabulary.context: People may also seek to fulfill this need through social interactions with family, boyfriends or girlfriends, mentors, close colleagues, or confidants.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人们还可能通过与家人、男朋友或女朋友、导师、亲密同事或知己的社交互动来寻求满足这种需求

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生理学的, 生理学上的, 生理机能的；[医] 生理学的, 生理的
vocabulary.context: I. Physiological Needs
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一、生理需要

vocabulary.definition: n. 征服, 统治权, 优势, 精通, 掌握；[法] 统治权, 控制权, 征服
vocabulary.context: The higher one is the need for self-respect, the need for strength, competence, mastery, self-confidence, independence, and freedom.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更高的一种是对自尊的需要，对力量、能力、掌握、自信、独立和自由的需要

vocabulary.definition: n. 声望, 威望, 威信；[经] 商誉, 声誉
vocabulary.context: The lower one is the need for the respect of others , the need for status, recognition, fame, prestige, and attention.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 较低的一种是对他人尊重的需要，对地位、认可、名誉、声望和关注的需要

vocabulary.definition: adj. 没有希望的, 没有前途的, 结果未必良好的
vocabulary.context: "It is one of humanity's enduring mysteries," they write, "why some individuals rise from unpromising origins to great heights, when so many others, facing similar obstacles and with seemingly similar capabilities, don't rise at all."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这是人类永恒的谜团之一，"他们写道，"为什么有些人从毫无希望的出身上升到了伟大的高度，而其他许多人面临着类似的障碍，拥有看似相似的能力，却根本没有上升。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 禁欲主义, 苦行生涯；[医] 制欲主义
vocabulary.context: They say that alone, the superiority complex is a recipe for complacency; mere insecurity could be crippling; and impulse control can produce asceticism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们说，仅凭优越感就会导致自满。仅仅缺乏安全感就可能造成严重后果；控制冲动可以产生禁欲主义

vocabulary.definition: n. 抒情诗人, 歌曲作者
vocabulary.context: Supreme Court; over two-thirds of Tony Award-winning lyricists and composers; and a whopping third of American Nobel laureates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最高法院；超过三分之二的托尼奖获奖作词家和作曲家；以及高达三分之一的美国诺贝尔奖获得者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 确切的
vocabulary.context: In the preface of their 2014 book, The Triple Package: How Three Unlikely Traits Explain the Rise and Fall of Cultural Groups in America , they acknowledge the elusiveness of a sure-fire success formula:
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他们 2014 年出版的书《三重包：三个不太可能的特征如何解释美国文化群体的兴衰》的序言中，他们承认肯定成功的公式是难以捉摸的：

vocabulary.definition: n. 少数, 少量, 缺乏
vocabulary.context: The paucity of convincing explanation for why some people are winners is particularly curious because in America, there are so many of them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于为什么有些人是赢家缺乏令人信服的解释尤其令人好奇，因为在美国，这样的人太多了

vocabulary.definition: n. 任命, 委派( posting的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: But try "success" and the response is overwhelming: 1.2 million postings, some serious, some facetious.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但尝试"成功"后，反响是压倒性的：120 万条帖子，有些是严肃的，有些是开玩笑的

vocabulary.definition: n. 幽默家, 幽默作家
vocabulary.context: Glasow, a Wisconsin businessman and humorist: "Success is simple: Do what's right, the right way, at the right time."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 格拉索，威斯康星州商人和幽默家："成功很简单：在正确的时间、以正确的方式做正确的事。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 滑稽的, 爱开玩笑的
vocabulary.context: But try "success" and the response is overwhelming: 1.2 million postings, some serious, some facetious.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但尝试"成功"后，反响是压倒性的：120 万条帖子，有些是严肃的，有些是开玩笑的

vocabulary.definition: n. 总数, 总和, 金额, 概要, 顶点；v.t. 总计, 概括；v.i. 合计；[计] 系统实用程序和维护
vocabulary.context: He summed up his experience like this: "Success is not final, failure is not fatal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他这样总结自己的经验："成功不是最终的，失败也不是致命的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未灌注的,未装填的
vocabulary.context: turning slowly gray the way unprimed wood
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 未涂底漆的木材慢慢变灰

vocabulary.definition: adv. 潮湿地, 有湿气
vocabulary.context: hugged me to her breasts and whispered wetly
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 将我抱在胸前，湿漉漉地低语

vocabulary.definition: n. 灰色, 暗淡；adj. 灰色的, 灰白的, 面色苍白的, 年老的, 老练的, 阴沉的；v. (使)变灰色
vocabulary.context: grays slowly in the grate when the flames
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 火焰在炉排中慢慢变灰

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不知羞耻的
vocabulary.context: The woman behind me said it was shameless,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我身后的女人说这太无耻了，

vocabulary.definition: n. 炉格, 炉篦, 炉栅, 火炉, 壁炉, 格栅；v.t. 装格栅于, 磨擦, 轧碎, 磨碎, 使焦急, 触怒, 使人烦躁, 刺激, 激怒；v.t. vi. 擦响, 发出刺耳的尖厉声
vocabulary.context: grays slowly in the grate when the flames
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 火焰在炉排中慢慢变灰

vocabulary.definition: adj. 尚未被人碰过的, 原封不动的, 完整的；[医] 完整的, 无伤的
vocabulary.context: someone as strong as I still home, still intact
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 像我一样坚强的人还在家里，完好无损

vocabulary.definition: n. 投石器, 抛掷, 吊索, 吊钩；v.t. 用投石器投掷, 用吊钩吊上, 吊起
vocabulary.context: he carried his left arm in a black sling
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他用黑色吊带携带左臂

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高的, 长的, 夸大的；adv. 夸大地
vocabulary.context: taller, more handsome in his white uniform
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿上白色制服更高更帅

vocabulary.definition: n. 制服；adj. 统一的, 一律的, 始终如一的
vocabulary.context: taller, more handsome in his white uniform
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿上白色制服更高更帅

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不够地, 不能胜任地
vocabulary.context: Dressed in old military fatigues and wearing red armbands, the zealots poured out of their homes and classrooms and set out to purge China of "revisionists" and anyone else they deemed to be "backward" or insufficiently enthusiastic about their leader.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 身穿旧军装、戴着红袖章的狂热分子从家中和教室涌出，开始清除中国的"修正主义者"和任何他们认为"落后"或对领导人不够热情的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 修正主义者；adj. 修正主义的
vocabulary.context: Dressed in old military fatigues and wearing red armbands, the zealots poured out of their homes and classrooms and set out to purge China of "revisionists" and anyone else they deemed to be "backward" or insufficiently enthusiastic about their leader.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 身穿旧军装、戴着红袖章的狂热分子从家中和教室涌出，开始清除中国的"修正主义者"和任何他们认为"落后"或对领导人不够热情的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 指南, 手册, 要览
vocabulary.context: Their ideological guidebook was a slender collection of Mao's maxims that captured the violent spirit of his rule.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的意识形态指南是毛泽东格言的薄薄集合，体现了他统治的暴力精神

vocabulary.definition: n. 理论家, 思想家,空想家
vocabulary.context: If the project was outsized and over-ambitious, so was its author: Mao, in addition to being a revolutionary, a warrior, and an ideologue, was an unabashed advocate of violence and one of the most powerful and prolific dictators of the 20th Century.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果说这个项目规模过大、野心太大，那么它的作者也是如此：毛泽东除了是一名革命者、战士和理论家之外，还是一位毫不掩饰的暴力倡导者，也是 20 世纪最强大、最多产的独裁者之一

vocabulary.definition: n. 顽固分子；[法] 顽固分子, 顽固派, 死不屈从者
vocabulary.context: He accused his alleged party enemies of sabotaging his drive to modernize the country, and called on the Chinese youth to carry out a cultural revolution by defeating "diehard bourgeois elements" and enforcing the ideological purity of his regime.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他指责他所谓的党内敌人破坏他实现国家现代化的努力，并呼吁中国青年进行文化大革命，击败"顽固资产阶级分子"并确保其政权的意识形态纯洁性

vocabulary.definition: n. 现代化
vocabulary.context: By 1976, when he died, his modernization campaign resulted in the annihilation of an estimated 30 million to 45 million of his countrymen.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到 1976 年他去世时，他的现代化运动导致估计有 3000 万至 4500 万同胞被消灭

vocabulary.definition: adj. 好战的, 交战的, 交战国的；[法] 交战一方, 交战国, 交战集团
vocabulary.context: Praised by Mao, who mingled with about one million of the "Red Guards" when they came to meet him in Beijing's Tiananmen Square, the youngsters became increasingly belligerent and blind to China's realities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 受到毛泽东的赞扬，毛泽东在北京天安门广场与约百万"红卫兵"混在一起，年轻人变得越来越好战，对中国的现实视而不见

vocabulary.definition: n. 十字军战士, 改革者
vocabulary.context: Armed with this list of unquestioned verities, tens of millions of these crusaders roamed the cities and countryside, persecuting the intelligentsia and denouncing what had remained of China's ancient culture.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 带着这份不容置疑的事实清单，数以千万计的十字军游荡在城市和乡村，迫害知识分子，谴责中国古代文化的残余

vocabulary.definition: n. 实施者, 强制者；[计] 监视程序
vocabulary.context: When the Communist enforcers confiscated the meager harvests to feed the party elites and workers in the cities, there ensued a mass starvation that wiped out entire populations of thousands of villages.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当共产党执法者没收微薄的收成来养活城市里的党内精英和工人时，随之而来的是大规模饥饿，导致数千个村庄的全部人口被消灭

vocabulary.definition: n. 遗物, 遗迹, 纪念物, 遗风, 残留物
vocabulary.context: A large segment of the urban population was forcibly displaced, most notably by their transfer to rural regions during a phase of the "Cultural Revolution" called "the Down to the Countryside Movement." Historical relics and artifacts were destroyed, and cultural and religious sites were ransacked.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 很大一部分城市人口被迫流离失所，尤其是在"文化大革命"的"下乡运动"阶段，他们被转移到农村地区。历史文物和文物遭到破坏，文化和宗教场所遭到洗劫

vocabulary.definition: n. 休息处
vocabulary.context: But is there for the night a resting-place?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是晚上有休息的地方吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 旅行者
vocabulary.context: Shall I meet other wayfarers at night?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 晚上我应该去见其他徒步旅行者吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 劳动, 努力, 工作, 劳工, 分娩；v.i. 劳动, 努力, 苦干；v.t. 详细分析, 使厌烦
vocabulary.context: Of labour you shall find the sum.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你会发现劳动的总和

vocabulary.definition: n. 舒适, 安慰, 安慰者；v.t. 安慰
vocabulary.context: Shall I find comfort, travel-sore and weak?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 旅途中的酸痛和虚弱让我能找到安慰吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 黑暗, 暗, 深色, 隐密, 模糊, 无知
vocabulary.context: May not the darkness hide it from my face?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黑暗难道不能遮住我的脸吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 敲, 敲打, 敲门；v. 敲击, 互撞, 攻击
vocabulary.context: Then must I knock, or call when just in sight?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那么我必须敲门，或者一看到就打电话吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 旅程, 旅行, 行程；v.i. 旅行；v.t. 游历
vocabulary.context: Will the day's journey take the whole long day?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一天的路程会花一整天吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 小时, 钟头, 时间, ...点钟, 课时
vocabulary.context: A roof for when the slow dark hours begin.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 缓慢的黑暗时光开始时的屋顶

vocabulary.definition: n. 朋友, 支持者, 赞助者；[法] 朋友, 友人, 赞助者
vocabulary.context: From morn to night, my friend.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从早到晚，我的朋友

vocabulary.definition: n. [总称]塔珀家用塑料制品
vocabulary.context: When I was growing up, my mother worked as a service representative at the phone company each day, fielding people's complaints, and sold Tupperware at night.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我成长的过程中，我的母亲每天在电话公司担任服务代表，处理人们的投诉，并在晚上出售特百惠

vocabulary.definition: n. 委托；[经] 委托, 寄销, 寄销品
vocabulary.context: Watching them go each day to jobs they didn't love without complaining while making the absolute most of the little we had - dollar movies, consignment shops, neighborhood plays, and warehouse grocery stores - I learned to never look for the lazy win, but instead seek the hard-won victory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 看着他们每天去从事他们不喜欢的工作而不抱怨，同时充分利用我们所拥有的一点点--廉价电影、寄售店、社区戏剧和仓库杂货店--我学会了永远不要寻找懒惰的胜利，而是寻求来之不易的胜利

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可归于...的
vocabulary.context: Anything I am ever able to do or achieve is attributable to her and her living example of hard work in the face of failure, determination in the face of setbacks, and perspective in the face of adversity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我能够做到或取得的任何成就都归功于她和她在失败面前努力工作、在挫折面前坚定决心、在逆境面前保持乐观的活生生的例子

vocabulary.definition: n. 受惠者, 受益人；adj. 受封的
vocabulary.context: As a beneficiary of their toil, grit, and grace, and in acknowledgment to all those single mothers out there working right now, in this moment, for the same things, I wish you all a Happy Mother's Day.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为她们的辛劳、勇气和恩典的受益者，并感谢所有此时此刻为同样的事情而工作的单身母亲，我祝大家母亲节快乐

vocabulary.definition: n. 息票, 赠券；[经] 息票, 利息单, 联票
vocabulary.context: Out of college I worked with people who had never known the inside of a Marshall's, clipped a coupon - let alone knew what a double coupon was - or walked the front lawn of a yard sale looking for clothes or dishes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大学毕业后，我和一些从来不知道马歇尔百货公司内部的人一起工作，剪过一张优惠券--更不用说知道什么是双倍优惠券了--或者在庭院旧货出售的前草坪上寻找衣服或菜肴

vocabulary.definition: adv. 显著地
vocabulary.context: And the Marshall's layaway window featured prominently in our lives.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 马歇尔酒店的分期付款窗口在我们的生活中占有重要地位

vocabulary.definition: adj. 难得的, 来之不易的
vocabulary.context: Watching them go each day to jobs they didn't love without complaining while making the absolute most of the little we had - dollar movies, consignment shops, neighborhood plays, and warehouse grocery stores - I learned to never look for the lazy win, but instead seek the hard-won victory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 看着他们每天去从事他们不喜欢的工作而不抱怨，同时充分利用我们所拥有的一点点--廉价电影、寄售店、社区戏剧和仓库杂货店--我学会了永远不要寻找懒惰的胜利，而是寻求来之不易的胜利

vocabulary.definition: n. 魅力, 迷人的美, 魔法；v.t. 迷惑
vocabulary.context: She and so many other women in my neighborhood worked one, usually two jobs, with no glamour, little pay and minimal respect, and fought hard each day for their kids to have a life that was far easier, less stressful, and more stable than their own.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她和我附近的许多其他女性都做一份（通常是两份）工作，没有魅力，工资微薄，也没有多少尊重，每天都在努力奋斗，让她们的孩子过上比她们自己轻松、压力更小、更稳定的生活

vocabulary.definition: n. 色彩, 浅色；v.t. 染色于
vocabulary.context: But she read and she worked, and she took advantage of Washington D.C.'s free museums and libraries to teach me just how much was possible, even if the window looked tinted at the moment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但她读书、工作，她利用华盛顿特区的免费博物馆和图书馆来告诉我有多少可能性，即使窗户此刻看起来有色

vocabulary.definition: adj. 没有人争的, 无竞争的, 明显的；[法] 无争议的, 无竞争的, 无异议的
vocabulary.context: The rest of the time, uncontested or rigged elections kept El Jefe and the Dominican Party in power.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其余时间，无争议或被操纵的选举让杰夫和多米尼加党继续掌权

vocabulary.definition: n. 研发代码；代号
vocabulary.context: According to one account, this is what happened to the plan of Las Mariposas - the codename of the Mirabal sisters - to assassinate Trujillo in 1960 at a cattle fair.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据一种说法，这就是 Las Mariposas（米拉巴尔姐妹的代号）于 1960 年在一次牛市上刺杀特鲁希略的计划所发生的事情

vocabulary.definition: n. 忠实追随者, 党羽, 跟踪者；[法] 亲信, 心腹, 顺从者
vocabulary.context: Eager to show how "liked" he was by his people, Trujillo organized so-called "civic reviews" where hired henchmen shouted praise for his government.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特鲁希略急于展示他的人民多么"喜欢"他，他组织了所谓的"公民评论"，雇佣的追随者大声赞扬他的政府

vocabulary.definition: n. 蓝天曰本丰田
vocabulary.context: Churches had to post the slogan "Dios en cielo, Trujillo en tierra" ("God in Heaven, Trujillo on Earth").
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 教堂必须张贴口号"Dios en cielo, Trujillo en tierra"（"上帝在天堂，特鲁希略在地球"）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 过度的, 过高的, 过分的
vocabulary.context: Meanwhile, his own and his family's share in the good times was exorbitant - by the 1950s, he was one of the wealthiest men in the world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与此同时，他自己和他的家人在美好时光中所分享的份额是巨大的--到 20 世纪 50 年代，他已成为世界上最富有的人之一

vocabulary.definition: adv. 欢乐的, 轻率的, 无忧无虑的, 愉快的
vocabulary.context: The only reason why Trujillo briefly allowed the creation of an opposition party was to make it easier to identify candidates for his hit list, who were then blithely killed by the SIM, (the Servicio de Inteligencia Militar), the military secret service.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特鲁希略短暂允许成立反对党的唯一原因是为了更容易地确定他的暗杀名单中的候选人，这些候选人随后被军事特勤局 SIM（the Servicio de Inteligencia Militar）愉快地杀害

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我陶醉, 自恋；[医] 自爱欲, 恋已癖
vocabulary.context: For example, the Dominican Congress, bowing to El Jefe's narcissism, changed the name of the capital from Santo Domingo to Ciudad Trujillo.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，多米尼加国会屈服于杰夫的自恋，将首都名称从圣多明各改为特鲁希略城

vocabulary.definition: n. 专制君主, 暴君；[法] 暴君, 专制君主
vocabulary.context: At one time or another, each was ruled by an iron-fisted despot who treated human rights as a joke.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每一个人都曾一度被一位铁腕暴君统治，他把人权当作一个笑话

vocabulary.definition: n. 兽性, 原始状态, 野蛮人
vocabulary.context: Trujillo's regime was also known for its savagery, and he enjoyed the support of many outsiders, including the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特鲁希略政权也以残暴着称，他得到了包括美国在内的许多局外人的支持

vocabulary.definition: n. 古典学者, 古典主义者
vocabulary.context: "There are a few hazards to this work," laughs Benefiel, a 35-year old classicist from Washington and Lee University who has spent part of the past six summers in Pompeii.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这项工作存在一些危险，"来自华盛顿与李大学的 35 岁古典学家贝尼菲尔笑着说，过去六个夏天的部分时间都在庞贝城度过

vocabulary.definition: adj. 干的, 粉状的；v. 使干燥(desiccate的过去式); 变干
vocabulary.context: And - much higher on the ick meter - her flashlight revealed a desiccated corpse that looked as if it was struggling to rise from the floor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 而且 - 在恶心计的更高处 - 她的手电筒显示出一具干燥的尸体，看起来好像正在努力从地板上爬起来

vocabulary.definition: n. 乱画, 粗糙雕刻
vocabulary.context: Inside elite dwellings like that of Maius Castricius - a four-story home with panoramic windows overlooking the Bay of Naples that was excavated in the 1960s - she's examined 85 graffito.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在 Maius Castricius 等精英住宅的内部--一栋 20 世纪 60 年代出土的四层住宅，配有全景窗户，俯瞰那不勒斯湾--她检查了 85 幅涂鸦

vocabulary.definition: n. 沉闷；乏味
vocabulary.context: On the book's introductory page, an epigram taken from a wall in Pompeii speaks to the multitude of graffiti in the ancient world: "I'm amazed, O wall, that you have not fallen in ruins, you who support the tediousness of so many writers."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这本书的介绍页上，庞贝古城墙上的一句警句讲述了古代世界的大量涂鸦："我很惊讶，墙壁啊，你没有沦为废墟，你支持了这么多作家的乏味。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 闲谈, 聊天
vocabulary.context: She soon found what she was looking for: a string of names and a cluster of numbers, part of the vibrant graffiti chitchat carried on by the citizens of Pompeii before Mount Vesuvius erupted in A.D.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她很快就找到了她要找的东西：一串名字和一串数字，这是公元维苏威火山爆发之前庞贝城居民进行的充满活力的涂鸦聊天的一部分

vocabulary.definition: n. 警句, 讽刺短诗
vocabulary.context: On the book's introductory page, an epigram taken from a wall in Pompeii speaks to the multitude of graffiti in the ancient world: "I'm amazed, O wall, that you have not fallen in ruins, you who support the tediousness of so many writers."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这本书的介绍页上，庞贝古城墙上的一句警句讲述了古代世界的大量涂鸦："我很惊讶，墙壁啊，你没有沦为废墟，你支持了这么多作家的乏味。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 切割, 雕刻；[医] 切
vocabulary.context: Some were greetings from friends, carefully incised around the edges of frescoes in the home's finest room.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有些是朋友们的问候，是在家里最好的房间里的壁画边缘仔细雕刻的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非精英的
vocabulary.context: Contemporary scholars have been drawn to the study of graffiti, interested to hear the voices of the non-elite and marginal groups that earlier scholars spurned and then surprised to learn that the practice of graffiti was widespread among all groups across the ancient world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当代学者被涂鸦研究所吸引，有兴趣听到早期学者所唾弃的非精英和边缘群体的声音，然后惊讶地发现涂鸦的做法在古代世界的所有群体中都很普遍

vocabulary.definition: n. （弯曲路面或轨道的）外道( outside的复数形式 ); （靠近路中央的）外侧; （建筑物等的）周围
vocabulary.context: From the very beginning, archaeologists noticed copious amounts of graffiti on the outsides of buildings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从一开始，考古学家就注意到建筑物外部有大量的涂鸦

vocabulary.definition: n. 离开正路, 偏离, 畸变, 光行差, 心理失常, 色差；[化] 光行差; 像差
vocabulary.context: Nonetheless, she moved closer to the walls and searched for aberrations in the stucco.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管如此，她还是靠近墙壁，寻找灰泥中的异常现象

vocabulary.definition: adj. 所有者的, 所有权的, 私有的；n. 所有者, 业主, 所有权
vocabulary.context: Another reason that so few companies have signed the pledge so far could be because, historically, factory lists have been viewed as proprietary information, says Sharon Waxman, president of the Fair Labor Association, a Washington, D.C.-based nonprofit that brings together brand suppliers and labor rights groups with the goal of improving workers' rights.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 迄今为止，很少有公司签署该承诺的另一个原因可能是因为，从历史上看，工厂名单一直被视为专有信息，公平劳工协会主席莎伦·韦克斯曼说，该协会是一家总部位于华盛顿特区的非营利组织，将品牌供应商和劳工权利团体聚集在一起，旨在改善工人的权利

vocabulary.definition: n. 补足物, 补语, 补数；v.t. 补充, 补足；[计] 补码; 反相器; 补数
vocabulary.context: "What we're saying is that there are clear limitations to those systems and what transparency does complements everything else you have."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们想说的是，这些系统存在明显的局限性，而透明度可以补充你所拥有的其他一切。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 关门, 停工, 停机；[经] 停工
vocabulary.context: When tens of thousands of workers walked off the job to protest low wages at the end of last year, it resulted in a week-long shutdown of more than 50 factories outside Dhaka.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 去年底，数万名工人罢工抗议低工资，导致达卡以外的 50 多家工厂停工一周

vocabulary.definition: n. 零售商人, 传播的人；[经] 零售商
vocabulary.context: Two major agreements between global retailers and brands and trade unions - the Accord on Fire and Building Safety in Bangladesh and the Alliance for Bangladesh Worker Safety were also signed after the tragedy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 悲剧发生后，全球零售商、品牌和工会还签署了两项主要协议--《孟加拉国消防和建筑安全协议》和《孟加拉国工人安全联盟》

vocabulary.definition: adj. 用长弹毛做的, 以次充好的；n. 长弹毛, 软再生毛, 赝品
vocabulary.context: The causes included shoddy construction, a building with too many floors and too much heavy equipment for the structure to withstand.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 原因包括建筑质量低劣、建筑物楼层过多、重型设备过多，导致结构无法承受

vocabulary.definition: n. 广场, 市场, 购物区
vocabulary.context: On April 24, 2013, the eight-story Rana Plaza building outside Dhaka, Bangladesh, collapsed, killing more than 1,100 people and injuring thousands of others.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2013年4月24日，孟加拉国达卡郊外的八层拉纳广场大楼倒塌，造成1100多人死亡、数千人受伤

vocabulary.definition: n. 零售；adj. 零售的；v.t. 零售, 详述, 传播；v.i. 零售
vocabulary.context: At the time, the building housed five garment factories that manufactured goods for major retail companies in Europe and North America.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当时，该建筑内有五家服装厂，为欧洲和北美的主要零售公司生产商品

vocabulary.definition: n. 揭发, 泄露, 揭发(或暴露)的事实；[经] 揭示, 披露
vocabulary.context: The Apparel and Footwear Supply Chain Transparency Pledge sets a minimum industry-wide standard for supply chain disclosure, says Aruna Kashyap, senior counsel in the Women's Rights Division of Human Rights Watch and co-author of the report.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人权观察组织妇女权利部高级顾问、报告合著者阿鲁纳·卡什亚普 (Aruna Kashyap) 表示，服装和鞋类供应链透明度承诺为供应链披露制定了全行业最低标准

vocabulary.definition: adj. 国会的, 议会的, 议会制度的
vocabulary.context: In 1947, Japan adopted a new constitution and a parliamentary system of government, which is still in effect today.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1947年，日本通过了新宪法和议会制政体，至今仍然有效

vocabulary.definition: n. 帝国主义者, 皇帝统治的拥护者, 皇帝派的人；adj. 帝国主义的
vocabulary.context: To punish Japan for its imperialist ways and its brutal actions during the war, the victorious Allied powers revoked all of the land Japan had conquered since the late 1800s and returned the territories to China.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了惩罚日本的帝国主义行径和战争期间的残暴行径，战胜国同盟国收回了日本自1800年代末以来所征服的所有土地，并将这些领土归还给中国

vocabulary.definition: adj. 原尺寸的, 全面的, 完全的
vocabulary.context: They used the incident as an excuse to alert the army and begin a full-scale attack on China.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们以此事件为借口，向军队发出警报，开始对中国进行全面进攻

vocabulary.definition: n. 民族主义, 民族特性
vocabulary.context: Due to strong feelings of nationalism, more leaders began to recognize the benefits of using force to become a world power.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于强烈的民族主义情绪，更多的领导人开始认识到使用武力成为世界强国的好处

vocabulary.definition: n. 傀儡, 木偶；[法] 木偶, 傀儡, 受他人操纵的人
vocabulary.context: In 1931, Japan invaded Manchuria again and set up a "puppet government" designed to serve Japanese needs and desires.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1931年，日本再次入侵满洲，并成立了一个旨在满足日本需要和欲望的"傀儡政府"

vocabulary.definition: n. 后冲, 强烈反对；[电] 反撞, 逆栅流
vocabulary.context: Under his rule, democratic reformers achieved some political success, but they pushed too hard for reform and received an extreme backlash from conservative nationalists.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他的统治下，民主改革派取得了一些政治成功，但他们推行改革的力度太大，遭到了保守民族主义者的强烈反对

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 撤回, 废除；v.i. 藏牌；n. 藏牌；[计] 取消权限程序
vocabulary.context: To punish Japan for its imperialist ways and its brutal actions during the war, the victorious Allied powers revoked all of the land Japan had conquered since the late 1800s and returned the territories to China.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了惩罚日本的帝国主义行径和战争期间的残暴行径，战胜国同盟国收回了日本自1800年代末以来所征服的所有土地，并将这些领土归还给中国

vocabulary.definition: n. 恢复, 归还, 复位；[医] 恢复, 康复, 复位, 回复, 修复
vocabulary.context: In the decades after this "Meiji Restoration" Japan worked hard to keep up with European empires of the day and industrialized its economy and military very rapidly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在"明治维新"后的几十年里，日本努力跟上当时的欧洲帝国的步伐，并迅速实现了经济和军事的工业化

vocabulary.definition: n. 纳旁(一种铝皂), 凝固汽油；v.t. 用凝固汽油炸
vocabulary.context: Women's groups broadened their support by appealing to morality; they especially did not like some of the brutal tactics used in the war, such as chemical bombing and napalm.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 妇女团体通过道德诉求扩大支持范围；他们尤其不喜欢战争中使用的一些残酷战术，例如化学轰炸和凝固汽油弹

vocabulary.definition: adj. [军] 反战的
vocabulary.context: They wrote anti-war articles and held peaceful rallies with speeches against the war.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们撰写反战文章，举行和平集会，发表反战演讲

vocabulary.definition: n. 抽彩售货, 废物；v.t. 抽彩售货；v.i. 抽彩
vocabulary.context: Each man over 18 years old had a "draft card" with a number on it, and when the military needed more troops they would pick the numbers of men who had to go to war-like a lottery or a raffle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每个18岁以上的男人都有一张"征兵卡"，上面有一个号码，当军队需要更多的军队时，他们会像抽奖或抽奖一样挑选必须参战的男人的号码

vocabulary.definition: n. 再次竞选
vocabulary.context: Johnson, who originally sent troops to Vietnam, was so discouraged that he did not run for re-election in 1968.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 原本出兵越南的约翰逊因此灰心丧气，没有在1968年竞选连任

vocabulary.definition: adj. 全国的
vocabulary.context: Nation-wide student groups often led the protests.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 全国性的学生团体经常领导抗议活动

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 增强, 放大, 扩大, 详述；v.i. 引伸
vocabulary.context: This concern was amplified as the world's two largest communist countries, China and the USSR, provided military and financial assistance to North Vietnam.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着世界上最大的两个共产主义国家中国和苏联向北越提供军事和财政援助，这种担忧被放大

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 闯入, 侵入；v.t. 强加于
vocabulary.context: to intrude on their country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 入侵他们的国家

vocabulary.definition: n. 共产主义
vocabulary.context: At the time, many Americans were afraid of communism - to them, it represented the opposite of freedom.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当时，许多美国人害怕共产主义--对他们来说，它代表着自由的对立面

vocabulary.context: is monsoon brocade, is winter stars stitched into puddles
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是季风锦，是冬天的星星缝成水坑

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就像淹死的房子窗台上的泪水，窗户是向瀑布敞开的吗

vocabulary.context: is good-bye in a flooded, antique room, is good-bye in a room of crystal bowls
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一个被水淹没的古董房间里再见，在一个水晶碗的房间里再见

vocabulary.context: is water seeps into the cobblestone streets of my sleep, is still raining
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 水渗入我睡梦中的鹅卵石街道，还在下雨

vocabulary.definition: n. 一针, 疼痛, 针法, 碎布条, 针脚；v. 缝, 缝合, 装订
vocabulary.context: is monsoon brocade, is winter stars stitched into puddles
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是季风锦，是冬天的星星缝成水坑

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有水晶杯，是水从嘴里滴下来的叮叮响

vocabulary.context: is a bed is a small boat is a ship, is a current come to carry me in its arms
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是床是小船是船是水流来把我抱在怀里

vocabulary.context: My comrade I wrapt in his blanket, envelop'd well his form,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的战友，我裹着他的毯子，包裹着他的身体，

vocabulary.context: When you my son and my comrade dropt at my side that day,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当你我的儿子和我的战友那天来到我身边时，

vocabulary.context: But not a tear fell, not even a long-drawn sigh, long, long I gazed,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但我没有流下一滴眼泪，甚至没有一声长长的叹息，我凝视了很久，

vocabulary.definition: n. 朝东方向；adj. 向东方的；adv. 向东
vocabulary.context: As onward silently stars aloft, eastward new ones upward stole,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当星星静静地高高升起，东方的新星偷偷向上，

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使斜靠；v.i. 靠, 斜倚, 依赖
vocabulary.context: Then on the earth partially reclining sat by your side leaning my chin in my hands,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后在地上半斜坐在你身边，双手托着下巴，

vocabulary.definition: adj. 神秘的, 奥秘的；n. 神秘主义者
vocabulary.context: Passing sweet hours, immortal and mystic hours with you dearest comrade - not a tear, not a word,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与你最亲爱的同志一起度过甜蜜的时光，不朽而神秘的时光--没有一滴眼泪，没有一句话，

vocabulary.definition: adv. 微暗, 朦胧
vocabulary.context: Long there and then in vigil I stood, dimly around me the battle-field spreading,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我站在那里很久，然后守夜，战场在我周围朦胧地蔓延，

vocabulary.definition: n. 同志, 伙伴
vocabulary.context: When you my son and my comrade dropt at my side that day,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当你我的儿子和我的战友那天来到我身边时，

vocabulary.definition: n. 小心, 照料, 忧虑；v.i. 关心, 介意；v.t. 在意, 喜欢
vocabulary.context: I faithfully loved you and cared for you living, I think we shall surely meet again,)
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我忠实地爱着你，照顾着你的生活，我想我们一定会再见面的，）

vocabulary.definition: n. 中途停留
vocabulary.context: British delegates wanted to secure the southern and eastern coasts of Africa as stopover ports to Asia and Britain's Indian territory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 英国代表希望确保非洲南部和东部海岸成为亚洲和英国印度领土的中转港

vocabulary.definition: adj. 速射的
vocabulary.context: Native resistance was often suppressed, as their weaponry was no match for the colonizers' rapid-fire Maxim machine gun.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 土著人的抵抗经常受到压制，因为他们的武器无法与殖民者的速射马克沁机枪匹敌

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无手的, 手笨拙的
vocabulary.context: Horrific images emerged of dismembered bodies and handless Congolese men, women and children.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 出现了被肢解的尸体和没有手的刚果男人、女人和儿童的可怕图像

vocabulary.definition: n. 厌恶, 痛恶, 令人厌恶的事物
vocabulary.context: Stories of these and other abominations were gradually appearing in European newspapers and providing ammunition for human rights and religious groups that were agitating against the African colonization.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些和其他令人憎恶的故事逐渐出现在欧洲报纸上，并为鼓动反对非洲殖民统治的人权和宗教团体提供了弹药

vocabulary.definition: n. 穿透, 渗透, 侵入, 识穿, 洞察(力)；[化] 渗入
vocabulary.context: But the penetration into Africa wasn't just about Bibles and maps.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但对非洲的渗透不仅仅是圣经和地图

vocabulary.definition: n. 奎宁；[化] 奎宁; 金鸡纳碱; 金鸡纳霜
vocabulary.context: ...fire machine gun, which would prove effective against hostile natives; and medical advancements, particularly the development of quinine, an anti-malaria drug.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...火机枪，这对敌对的当地人有效；以及医学进步，特别是抗疟疾药物奎宁的开发

vocabulary.definition: n. 殖民地开拓者, 殖民者, 移入选民
vocabulary.context: Other lasting effects of this colonization include cultural assimilation: natives in these newly colonized nations were expected to learn the language of the colonizer and adopt many of their practices.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种殖民化的其他持久影响包括文化同化：这些新殖民国家的当地人被期望学习殖民者的语言并采用他们的许多做法

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 增殖, 激增, 扩散；v.t. 使激增
vocabulary.context: By the 17th century, the slave trade proliferated, and Europeans settled in large numbers along the coast of Africa where they would set up ports for the transportation of slaves to the Americas.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到了17世纪，奴隶贸易激增，欧洲人在非洲海岸大量定居，并在那里建立港口，将奴隶运往美洲

vocabulary.definition: adj. 照相的, 摄影用的, 详细记录的, 逼真的, 酷似的
vocabulary.context: Leopold and his peers kept denying the atrocities, but the invention of the roll-film camera by Kodak enabled many reformers to capture photographic evidence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 利奥波德和他的同僚一直否认这些暴行，但柯达胶卷相机的发明使许多改革者能够捕捉到照片证据

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未开化的, 不文明的
vocabulary.context: Africans were dehumanized, regarded as uncivilized, savage and brutal - racist tropes that were used by colonizers to justify the oppression of the indigenous people and the atrocities that were carried out against them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 非洲人被非人化，被视为不文明、野蛮和残酷--殖民者利用这些种族主义比喻来为对土著人民的压迫和针对他们的暴行辩护

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无差别的, 任意的, 杂乱的；[医] 无差别的, 普遍的
vocabulary.context: The plague was indiscriminate, and its cause was unknown.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 瘟疫不分青红皂白，其原因不明

vocabulary.definition: adj. 预防的, 防止的；n. 预防法, 预防措施, 预防物, 预防药
vocabulary.context: Simple health measures and preventative care, such as washing hands and cleaning cuts, slow the spread of illness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 简单的卫生措施和预防性护理，例如洗手和清洁伤口，可以减缓疾病的传播

vocabulary.definition: n. 农权制, 农奴地位, 农奴境遇, 奴役
vocabulary.context: Before the plague, through the practice of serfdom, servants were tied to the land that their lord owned and made almost no money.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在瘟疫爆发之前，通过农奴制的做法，仆人被束缚在领主拥有的土地上，几乎赚不到钱

vocabulary.definition: n. 肿胀, 肿大, 隆起部, 身上的肿胀处, 膨胀, 增大；adj. 肿大的, 突起的
vocabulary.context: There were corpses with swellings on their bodies and black spots on their skin.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那里有尸体，身上浮肿，皮肤上有黑斑

vocabulary.definition: n. 腹股沟, 交叉拱；[医] 腹股沟
vocabulary.context: They had swollen lumps in their armpits and near their groins.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的腋窝和腹股沟附近有肿块

vocabulary.definition: adv. 禁止, 起阻止作用, 抑制
vocabulary.context: Earlier in the 14th century, trade between distant countries was prohibitively time-consuming and expensive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 14 世纪早期，遥远国家之间的贸易极其耗时且昂贵

vocabulary.definition: n. 毒气, 沼气；[医] 瘴毒, 瘴气
vocabulary.context: At the time, people believed that disease spread through poisonous vapors, or miasma, coming from the bodies of the dead.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当时，人们相信疾病是通过死者尸体产生的有毒蒸气或瘴气传播的

vocabulary.definition: n. 地方病；adj. 某地(或某些人中)流行的, 地方的
vocabulary.context: Scientists believe that because of the climate in Europe, the Black Death never became endemic on the continent.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家认为，由于欧洲的气候，黑死病从未在该大陆流行

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我惩罚, 忏悔, 悔过, 赎罪, 苦修；v.t. 使悔罪, 使赎罪
vocabulary.context: They decided to repent for their sins by wandering from village to village, publicly whipping themselves and others as penance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们决定为自己的罪孽忏悔，从一个村庄流浪到另一个村庄，公开鞭打自己和他人以示忏悔

vocabulary.definition: n. 现代性, 现代状态, 现代东西
vocabulary.context: Looking back, some scholars argue that the Black Death pushed Europe into modernity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 回顾过去，一些学者认为黑死病将欧洲推向了现代化

vocabulary.definition: v. 重新解释( reinterpret的现在分词 )
vocabulary.context: By interpreting and reinterpreting the words of the Constitution and Bill of Rights, our courts are able to use the guiding principles of these ancient documents to make decisions in a modern world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过解释和重新解释《宪法》和《权利法案》的文字，我们的法院能够利用这些古老文件的指导原则在现代世界中做出裁决

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 测定口径, 校准, 使标准化, 调整；[化] 校准
vocabulary.context: The Supreme Court has calibrated these boundaries through a constant reinterpretation of the Constitution and its amendments.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最高法院通过不断重新解释宪法及其修正案来校准这些界限

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 踏过, 逾越, 超出...的限度；[法] 违犯, 逾越
vocabulary.context: With fresh memories of the oversteps of the British government, a group known as the Anti-Federalists refused to sign onto the Constitution without assurances protecting their "natural rights," rights that they asserted were given to them by God, and which could not be violated by any government.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对英国政府的越权行为记忆犹新，一个被称为反联邦党人的团体在没有保证保护他们的"自然权利"的情况下拒绝签署宪法，他们声称这些权利是上帝赋予他们的，任何政府都不能侵犯

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非法的, 违法的
vocabulary.context: For instance, after a man falsely yelled "fire!" in a crowded movie theater, it ruled that any speech that incites "imminent lawless action" was not protected by the First Amendment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，一名男子错误地大喊"着火了！"在拥挤的电影院里，它规定任何煽动"迫在眉睫的不法行为"的言论不受第一修正案的保护

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 刺激, 激励, 引诱；[法] 鼓动, 煽动
vocabulary.context: For instance, after a man falsely yelled "fire!" in a crowded movie theater, it ruled that any speech that incites "imminent lawless action" was not protected by the First Amendment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，一名男子错误地大喊"着火了！"在拥挤的电影院里，它规定任何煽动"迫在眉睫的不法行为"的言论不受第一修正案的保护

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生动的, 轮廓分明的, 绘画似的, 图解的；[计] 图形的
vocabulary.context: A public school student sued after being suspended for giving a speech at a school assembly including lewd and graphic content.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一名公立学校学生因在学校集会上发表包含猥亵和色情内容的演讲而被停学后提起诉讼

vocabulary.definition: adv. 本质地, 天生, 体质上
vocabulary.context: John, Chris, and Mary Beth's actions were found to be symbolic speech, constitutionally protected under the First Amendment, the first of ten which make up the Bill of Rights.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 约翰、克里斯和玛丽·贝丝的行为被认为是象征性言论，受到宪法第一修正案的保护，该修正案是构成《权利法案》的十项修正案中的第一条

vocabulary.definition: adv. 实质上, 重大, 物质上
vocabulary.context: Des Moines interpreted the First Amendment to protect political speech - even for students, as long as their speech did not "materially and substantially interfere with the requirements of appropriate discipline in the operation of the school."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 得梅因解释第一修正案是为了保护政治言论--即使是对学生来说，只要他们的言论不会"实质性地干扰学校运作中适当纪律的要求"

vocabulary.definition: n. 侧线, 旁轨, 边宽
vocabulary.context: A judge's initial decision lifted the suspension, siding with the students.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 法官初步决定解除停学，并站在学生一边

vocabulary.definition: adj. 粗俗的, 庸俗的, 普通的, 通俗的, 方言的；[法] 庸俗的, 卑下的, 粗俗的
vocabulary.context: "The First Amendment does not prevent the school officials from determining that," Justice Warren Burger wrote, "to permit such a vulgar and lewd speech such as [the] respondent's would undermine the school's basic educational mission." Moreover, "It was perfectly appropriate for the school to make the point to pupils that vulgar speech...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "第一修正案并不阻止学校官员认定，"沃伦·伯格法官写道，"允许像被告那样的粗俗和下流言论将损害学校的基本教育使命。"此外，"学校向学生强调粗俗言论是完全恰当的......

vocabulary.definition: n. 死产, 死胎；[医] 死产
vocabulary.context: Jackie had previously suffered a miscarriage and a stillbirth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 杰基此前曾流产过死产

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生下来已死的, 死产的；[医] 死产的
vocabulary.context: In addition to the miscarriage she suffered and the birth of a stillborn child, she endured the death of another infant son, Patrick, while in the White House.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了流产和死产之外，她在白宫期间还经历了另一个婴儿帕特里克的死亡

vocabulary.definition: n. 汽车行列
vocabulary.context: This occurred as the two of them rode in an open car in a presidential motorcade.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这发生在两人乘坐总统车队的敞篷车时

vocabulary.definition: n. 碉堡, 掩体, 药丸盒, 筒形女帽
vocabulary.context: On the one hand, she was stylish and praised for her iconic outfits - which included bright colors and pillbox hats.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一方面，她很时尚，并因其标志性服装而受到称赞--其中包括鲜艳的色彩和药盒帽

vocabulary.definition: n. 淋巴瘤, 淋巴组织瘤, 淋巴腺瘤；[医] 淋巴[组织]瘤
vocabulary.context: While she was being examined, doctors realized that she had lymphoma.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当她接受检查时，医生意识到她患有淋巴瘤

vocabulary.definition: n. 初进社交界的少女/女子, 初次登台的女演员
vocabulary.context: When she debuted in Manhattan, she was considered one of the most promising debutantes of the season.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当她在曼哈顿首次亮相时，她被认为是本季最有前途的新人之一

vocabulary.definition: n. 失败, 误送, 流产；[医] 流产
vocabulary.context: Jackie had previously suffered a miscarriage and a stillbirth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 杰基此前曾流产过死产

vocabulary.definition: n. 都市的郊区, 郊区居民, 郊区居民所特有的风俗习惯
vocabulary.context: They both had an Irish-Catholic heritage, a love of letters, and a desire for a more exciting life than what suburbia promised.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们都有爱尔兰天主教传统，热爱文学，渴望过上比郊区所承诺的更令人兴奋的生活

vocabulary.definition: n. 哀悼, 吊唁
vocabulary.context: Jackie received hundreds of thousands of condolence letters from Americans.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 杰基收到了数十万封来自美国人的慰问信

vocabulary.definition: n. (赛马赌博的)三连胜式
vocabulary.context: With a basis in the Constitution and Bill of Rights, an idealistic but deeply imperfect beginning, and involvement in many controversial issues today, the justice system trifecta of law enforcement, courts, and corrections has a hugely consequential role in our society.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 以《宪法》和《权利法案》为基础，一个理想主义但极其不完美的开端，并涉及当今许多有争议的问题，由执法、法院和惩教组成的司法系统三重奏在我们的社会中发挥着巨大的作用

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再试, 再审；[计] 重发; 重试
vocabulary.context: This means that the case can be retried.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这意味着该案可以重审

vocabulary.definition: n. 令状, 文书, 文件, 书面命令；[经] 法院命令
vocabulary.context: For example, British "Writs of Assistance" allowed illegal searches of colonial homes and also legalized the seizure of private property for use by the British government.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，英国的"协助令"允许非法搜查殖民地房屋，也将扣押英国政府使用的私人财产合法化

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 对簿法庭；v.t. 在法庭相争, 诉讼
vocabulary.context: The amendment declares "excessive bail shall not be required, nor excessive fines imposed, nor cruel and unusual punishments inflicted." The final phrase of the amendment has been controversial and litigated throughout our history, most notably in cases where the punishment involves the death penalty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 修正案宣称"不得要求过多的保释，不得处以过高的罚款，不得施加残酷和非常的处罚"。修正案的最后一句话在我们的历史上一直存在争议和诉讼，尤其是在涉及死刑的情况下

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 编成法典；[法] 使成文法化, 编成法典, 编纂
vocabulary.context: At the national level, thousands of statutes and regulations codify our laws.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在国家层面，数以千计的法规和条例编纂了我们的法律

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无组织的；紊乱的
vocabulary.context: The colonists felt that they were the victims of an unbalanced and disorganized justice system.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 殖民者认为他们是不平衡和无组织的司法系统的受害者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 理想主义的, 唯心论的, 唯心主义的, 唯心主义者的
vocabulary.context: With a basis in the Constitution and Bill of Rights, an idealistic but deeply imperfect beginning, and involvement in many controversial issues today, the justice system trifecta of law enforcement, courts, and corrections has a hugely consequential role in our society.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 以《宪法》和《权利法案》为基础，一个理想主义但极其不完美的开端，并涉及当今许多有争议的问题，由执法、法院和惩教组成的司法系统三重奏在我们的社会中发挥着巨大的作用

vocabulary.definition: n. 广大, 无边, 巨物
vocabulary.context: The immensity of our justice system has many parts, involves a diverse group of people, and is constantly evolving.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们庞大的司法系统由许多部分组成，涉及不同的人群，并且在不断发展

vocabulary.definition: n. 团块, 混合物, 聚集；[建] 堆集(作用)
vocabulary.context: "We got to look more deeply at the root of where this behavior came from than we would have had it gone a trial route - the anger issues in the family, exploring the drama in their relationship, the whole conglomeration of factors that led to that moment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们必须更深入地了解这种行为的根源，而不是尝试一下--家庭中的愤怒问题，探索他们关系中的戏剧性，以及导致那一刻的所有因素

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 赋于理智
vocabulary.context: They don't intellectualize what happened or repress emotions - I saw them cry and I heard them laugh - but they were always able to speak thoughtfully about Ann's death and its aftermath.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们不会理智地思考所发生的事情或压抑情绪 - 我看到他们哭，我听到他们笑 - 但他们总是能够深思熟虑地谈论安的死亡及其后果

vocabulary.definition: adj. 公诉人的, 原告的； 起诉的
vocabulary.context: And it allows victims, who often feel shut out of the prosecutorial process, a way to be heard and participate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它为经常感到被排除在起诉程序之外的受害者提供了一种表达意见和参与的方式

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自我激发的, 自己主动的
vocabulary.context: "As long as I'm self-motivated enough," Conor says, "I can really improve myself." The Grosmaires come, too, about once a month.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "只要我有足够的上进心，"康纳说，"我真的可以提高自己。"格罗斯梅尔夫妇也来一次，大约每月一次

vocabulary.definition: v.t. vi. 想象, 出现幻想, 产生幻想, 使幻想化, 幻想
vocabulary.context: Later, Andy told me that he had fantasized or hoped that maybe it had been an accident, maybe Conor's finger had slipped - that he would hear something unexpected to help him make sense of his daughter's death.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 后来，安迪告诉我，他幻想或希望也许这是一次意外，也许康纳的手指滑倒了--他会听到一些意想不到的声音，帮助他理解女儿的死亡

vocabulary.definition: n. 宾馆, 高级寄宿舍, 招待所
vocabulary.context: ...like, 'what are you so angry about?' And I told them, and they'd say, 'that's actually pretty crazy.'" The family that operated the guesthouse where Baliga was staying told her that people often wrote to the Dalai Lama for advice and suggested she try it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...就像，"你为什么这么生气？"我告诉他们，他们会说，'这实际上相当疯狂。'"巴利加住的旅馆的经营者告诉她，人们经常写信给达赖喇嘛寻求建议，并建议她尝试一下

vocabulary.definition: n. 脑干
vocabulary.context: Ann had some brainstem function, the doctors said, and although her parents, who are practicing Catholics, held out hope, it was clear to Andy that unless God did "wondrous things," Ann would not survive her injuries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 医生说，安有一些脑干功能，尽管她的父母是天主教徒，对此抱有希望，但安迪很清楚，除非上帝做"奇妙的事情"，否则安将无法在受伤后幸存

vocabulary.definition: adj. 最低级的, 最严重的, 一级的, 最高级的, 第一度的, 最轻度的
vocabulary.context: Campbell had charged Conor with first-degree murder, which, as most people in Florida understand it, carries a mandatory life sentence or, potentially, the death penalty.

vocabulary.definition: n. 兵工厂, 军械库；[机] 兵工厂
vocabulary.context: Along with a key ally named Gullah Jack, Vesey organized a slave rebellion in 1822 that planned to capture the Charleston arsenal and seize the city long enough for its black population to escape to the free black republic of Haiti.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1822 年，维西与一位名叫格拉·杰克 (Gullah Jack) 的重要盟友一起组织了一场奴隶叛乱，计划夺取查尔斯顿军火库，并占领这座城市足够长的时间，以便其黑人人口逃往自由的黑人共和国海地

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可否认的

vocabulary.definition: n. <苏格兰>三分之二
vocabulary.context: Henry Clay, a leading congressman, known as "The Great Compromiser," played a crucial role in brokering a two-part solution known as the Missouri Compromise.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 亨利·克莱（Henry Clay）是一位著名的国会议员，被称为"伟大的妥协者"，他在促成被称为"密苏里妥协"的两部分解决方案中发挥了至关重要的作用

vocabulary.definition: num. 三十五

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 叛乱在计划开始日期前几天被背叛，导致 35 名组织者被处决，维西布道的黑人教堂被毁

vocabulary.definition: n. 反对奴隶制；adj. 反对奴隶制度的
vocabulary.context: Slaveholders were clearly on the defensive with antislavery sentiment building in the north and undeniable opposition among African Americans in the south.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 奴隶主显然处于守势，北部反奴隶制情绪高涨，而南部非洲裔美国人则无可否认地反对

vocabulary.definition: adj. 主教的, 主教制度的, 英国国教的
vocabulary.context: Vesey quoted the Bible as well as congressional debates over the Missouri issue to denounce slavery from the pulpit of the African Methodist Episcopal Church where he was a lay minister.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 维西在担任非宗教牧师的非洲卫理公会教堂的讲坛上引用了《圣经》以及国会关于密苏里州问题的辩论，谴责奴隶制

vocabulary.definition: n. 讲道坛
vocabulary.context: Vesey quoted the Bible as well as congressional debates over the Missouri issue to denounce slavery from the pulpit of the African Methodist Episcopal Church where he was a lay minister.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 维西在担任非宗教牧师的非洲卫理公会教堂的讲坛上引用了《圣经》以及国会关于密苏里州问题的辩论，谴责奴隶制

vocabulary.definition: n. 功绩, 勋绩；v.t. 开发, 利用, 开拓, 剥削
vocabulary.context: African Americans knew that they could not rely upon whites to end slavery, but they also recognized that the increasing divide between north and south and their battle over western expansion could open opportunities for blacks to exploit.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 非裔美国人知道他们不能依靠白人来结束奴隶制，但他们也认识到南北之间日益扩大的分歧以及他们对西部扩张的斗争可能为黑人提供利用的机会

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自制的, 自力更生的, 白手起家的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他是一个白手起家的人，代表了美国梦

vocabulary.definition: n. 流域, 分水岭；[医] 分水界, 分水岭
vocabulary.context: The Presidential Election of 1860 was a watershed moment in American history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1860年的总统选举是美国历史上的一个分水岭时刻

vocabulary.definition: n. 判决, 裁定, 统治；adj. 统治的, 支配的, 普遍的
vocabulary.context: This idea was called "popular sovereignty." When Lincoln countered that this violated Supreme Court rulings, Douglas famously replied that whatever the Supreme Court decided was not as important as the actions of the citizens.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个想法被称为"人民主权"。当林肯反驳说这违反了最高法院的裁决时，道格拉斯做出了著名的回答说，无论最高法院的裁决如何，都不如公民的行为重要

vocabulary.definition: n. 石灰光, 石灰光灯, 以石灰光灯照射的舞台；v.t. 使受到注目, 使显露头角
vocabulary.context: And of course, his debates with Douglas had made him a national figure and brought the anti-slavery Republican movement into the limelight.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当然，他与道格拉斯的辩论使他成为全国性人物，并使反奴隶制共和党运动成为众人瞩目的焦点

vocabulary.definition: n. 符木, 记账, 得分, 比分, 计数器, 标签, 符合, 对应物；v.t. 记录, 点数, 计算, 加标签于, 使符合；v.i. 记帐, 符合, 吻合, 记分；[计] 计数
vocabulary.context: With the results tallied, the question was, would the South accept the outcome?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 统计结果后，问题是，南方会接受这个结果吗？

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使永存, 使不朽, 保持
vocabulary.context: In the debates, Lincoln argued that Douglas' position of "popular sovereignty" would perpetuate slavery in the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在辩论中，林肯认为道格拉斯的"人民主权"立场将使奴隶制在美国永久化

vocabulary.definition: adv. 狭窄地, 严密地, 仔细地, 几乎不, 勉强地, 仅仅
vocabulary.context: With four candidates in the field, Lincoln received only 40% of the popular vote and 180 electoral votes - enough to narrowly win the crowded election.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在四名候选人参选的情况下，林肯只获得了 40% 的普选票和 180 张选举人票--足以以微弱优势赢得这场拥挤的选举

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 插入, 调停, 干涉；[经] 进场干预
vocabulary.context: Or should the federal government intervene to keep slave states and free states equal in Congress or promote the decline of slavery?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 或者联邦政府应该进行干预，以保持蓄奴州和自由州在国会中的平等或促进奴隶制的衰落？

vocabulary.definition: v. 再供给, 再补给

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 攻击林肯的补给船将使南方成为侵略方

vocabulary.definition: n. 脱离论者, 分离论者
vocabulary.context: States that still remained part of the Union (such as Virginia and North Carolina) might be driven into the secessionist camp if they thought that the Union soldiers were the aggressors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 仍然是联邦一部分的州（例如弗吉尼亚州和北卡罗来纳州）如果认为联邦士兵是侵略者，可能会被赶入分裂主义阵营

vocabulary.definition: n. 法令, 条例, 圣餐礼；[经] 法令, 条款, 条例
vocabulary.context: On December 20, 1860, by a vote of 169-0, the South Carolina legislature approved an "ordinance of secession," stating that "the union now subsisting between South Carolina and other States, under the name of 'The United States of America,' is hereby dissolved."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1860年12月20日，南卡罗来纳州议会以169票对0票通过了一项"分裂国家法令"，声明"南卡罗来纳州与其他州之间现有的、以'美利坚合众国'名义存在的联盟特此解散。"

vocabulary.definition: adv. 毫无疑问, 不成问题, 无可非议, 确实
vocabulary.context: The commander at Fort Sumter was Major Robert Anderson, a former slave owner who was nevertheless unquestionably loyal to the Union.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 萨姆特堡的指挥官是罗伯特·安德森少校，他是一位前奴隶主，但无疑忠于联邦

vocabulary.definition: n. 侵略者, 挑畔者；[法] 侵略者, 攻击者
vocabulary.context: States that still remained part of the Union (such as Virginia and North Carolina) might be driven into the secessionist camp if they thought that the Union soldiers were the aggressors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 仍然是联邦一部分的州（例如弗吉尼亚州和北卡罗来纳州）如果认为联邦士兵是侵略者，可能会被赶入分裂主义阵营

vocabulary.definition: adv. 因此, 据此
vocabulary.context: On December 20, 1860, by a vote of 169-0, the South Carolina legislature approved an "ordinance of secession," stating that "the union now subsisting between South Carolina and other States, under the name of 'The United States of America,' is hereby dissolved."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1860年12月20日，南卡罗来纳州议会以169票对0票通过了一项"分裂国家法令"，声明"南卡罗来纳州与其他州之间现有的、以'美利坚合众国'名义存在的联盟特此解散。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 修改, 改善, 改良；v.i. 改过自新

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该妥协将修改宪法并将旧的密苏里妥协线一直延伸到太平洋海岸

vocabulary.definition: adj. 相当的, 可观的, 重要的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是林肯不能让他的部队挨饿，也不能让他们冒着表现出相当虚弱的风险投降

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到下午早些时候，战场上已有 40,000 名士兵在城镇北部和西部排成半圆形
