vocabulary.definition: n. 再选, 改选；[法] 再选, 重选, 再次当选
vocabulary.context: Such a victory in Pennsylvania would relieve Virginia of the burden of war, strengthen the hand of "Peace Democrats" in the North who wanted an immediate end to the war, and undermine President Abraham Lincoln's chances for reelection.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 宾夕法尼亚州的这样的胜利将减轻弗吉尼亚州的战争负担，增强北方希望立即结束战争的"和平民主党"的势力，并削弱亚伯拉罕·林肯总统连任的机会

vocabulary.definition: n. 陆军准将, 准将
vocabulary.context: While there, Hill's division was surprised to see Union troops under the command of Brigadier General John Buford arriving south of town.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在那里，希尔的师惊讶地看到约翰·布福德准将指挥的联邦军队抵达城镇南部

vocabulary.definition: adj. 没有防卫的, 无掩护的, 未设防的, 没有保护人的, 不受关税保护的；[计] 无保护的, 非保护的
vocabulary.context: The Big Round Top and Little Round Top were two nearby hills that had been left unprotected.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大圆顶和小圆顶是附近两座未受保护的山丘

vocabulary.definition: n. 辞职, 辞呈, 听从；[经] 辞职
vocabulary.context: A month later, he offered his resignation to Confederate President Jefferson Davis, which was refused.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个月后，他向南部邦联总统杰斐逊·戴维斯提出辞职，但遭到拒绝

vocabulary.definition: adj. 胜利的, 得胜的, 战胜的
vocabulary.context: Just after Antietam, feeling victorious despite heavy Union losses, President Abraham Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation, which would abolish slavery in the South and free all African-Americans from bondage should the North regain control of the southern states.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就在安提特姆战役之后，尽管联邦损失惨重，亚伯拉罕·林肯总统仍感到胜利，他发布了《解放奴隶宣言》，如果北方重新控制南方各州，该宣言将废除南方的奴隶制，并将所有非裔美国人从奴役中解放出来

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 在...下面挖, 渐渐破坏, 暗地里破坏；[法] 暗中破坏, 以阴谋中伤伤害
vocabulary.context: Such a victory in Pennsylvania would relieve Virginia of the burden of war, strengthen the hand of "Peace Democrats" in the North who wanted an immediate end to the war, and undermine President Abraham Lincoln's chances for reelection.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 宾夕法尼亚州的这样的胜利将减轻弗吉尼亚州的战争负担，增强北方希望立即结束战争的"和平民主党"的势力，并削弱亚伯拉罕·林肯总统连任的机会

vocabulary.definition: adj. 速射的, 讲话快而尖声的
vocabulary.context: Armed with new weapons of destruction such as the rapid-firing Gatling gun, Custer and his soldiers felt that it was only a matter of time before the Indians would surrender and submit to life on a smaller reservation than promised in the Fort Laramie Treaty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 配备了新型毁灭性武器，例如速射加特林机枪，卡斯特和他的士兵们认为，印第安人投降并在比《拉勒米堡条约》中承诺的更小的保留地上屈服只是时间问题

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 高涨, 涌起；n. 增长, 高潮
vocabulary.context: By 1868, the upsurge of violence on the Great Plains forced the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到了 1868 年，大平原上暴力事件的激增迫使美国

vocabulary.definition: n. 平衡; 冷淡; 僵持
vocabulary.context: After a stand-off between tribal warriors and the United States Army, their leader Chief Joseph directed his followers toward Canada to avoid capture.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在部落战士与美国陆军对峙后，他们的领袖约瑟夫酋长指示他的追随者前往加拿大以避免被俘

vocabulary.definition: n. 实际知识, 技术秘诀, 诀窍；[计] 技术诀窍
vocabulary.context: Many lacked the know-how to implement complex irrigation systems.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多人缺乏实施复杂灌溉系统的专业知识

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未缓和的, 未减轻的, 全然的；[法] 全然的, 绝对的, 未减轻的
vocabulary.context: The Dawes Act was an unmitigated disaster for tribal units.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 《道斯法案》对部落单位来说是一场彻头彻尾的灾难

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自傲的
vocabulary.context: But the prideful Custer sought to engage the Sioux on his own.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但骄傲的卡斯特试图独自与苏人交战

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 每十人杀一人, 大批杀害
vocabulary.context: The Lakota Sioux were decimated by yet another American tactic of war: an attack on their food supply.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 拉科塔苏族人因美国的另一种战争策略而遭受重创：攻击他们的食物供应

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有力的, 强烈的, 有说服力的
vocabulary.context: In the second half of the late 19th century, America's westward expansion was meeting forceful resistance from native populations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 19世纪下半叶，美国的西进扩张遭到了原住民的强烈抵制

vocabulary.definition: n. 物料, 军品
vocabulary.context: It took the members of the 9th Marine Expeditionary Brigade almost an entire day to bring their men and materiel ashore that day in March 1965.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1965年3月的那天，海军陆战队第9远征旅的成员几乎花了一整天的时间才将人员和物资运上岸

vocabulary.definition: n. (=barbecue)烤肉野餐, 烤肉架；v. 烤肉,用调料烤(肉)
vocabulary.context: He holds occasional barbeques where former Marines and Viet Cong drink, swap stories and laugh.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他偶尔会举办烧烤活动，前海军陆战队员和越共成员可以在那里喝酒、交换故事和欢笑

vocabulary.definition: v. 重新入伍
vocabulary.context: He didn't re-enlist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他没有重新入伍

vocabulary.definition: n. 侦察, 交换子；[计] 追溯转换
vocabulary.context: His unit was among the first to land in Danang, and he spent three tours in country; first as an engineer, then leading recon patrols in the mountains around Danang, and finally as an infantry captain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他所在的部队是第一批登陆岘港的部队，他曾三度进行乡村巡演；首先担任工程师，然后在岘港周围的山区领导侦察巡逻，最后担任步兵上尉

vocabulary.definition: n. 脱叶剂, 落叶剂；[化] 脱叶剂
vocabulary.context: When Vetter's wife died a few years ago, he moved to Danang to help victims of the defoliant Agent Orange.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 维特的妻子几年前去世后，他搬到岘港帮助落叶剂橙剂的受害者

vocabulary.definition: n. 讯问者, 质问者；[电] 询问机
vocabulary.context: "The other prisoners were going down to battalion headquarters, which was down the beach, and they had South Vietnamese interrogators," he says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "其他囚犯正前往位于海滩下方的营部，那里有南越审讯者，"他说

vocabulary.definition: n. 夫(或妻)的姊妹, 嫂子, 弟媳；[法] 夫或妻的姐妹, 姑, 姨
vocabulary.context: And inside the shelter, I saw my sister-in-law, with her baby still in her arms.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在避难所里，我看到了我的嫂子，她的孩子还在她的怀里

vocabulary.definition: n. 手榴弹
vocabulary.context: He ran to the jungle, where he watched the helicopters land in his village - and a short time later heard the sound of grenades.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他跑到丛林里，看着直升机降落在他的村庄里，不久之后就听到了手榴弹的声音

vocabulary.definition: n. 反革命
vocabulary.context: Within 15 months of Castro's triumph, CIA officers in Cuba were seeking out his opponents and attempting to organize a new counterrevolution.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 卡斯特罗获胜后的 15 个月内，驻古巴的中央情报局官员开始寻找他的对手，并试图组织一场新的反革命

vocabulary.definition: v. 辱骂, 斥责
vocabulary.context: Dictator Fulgencio Batista, reviled as a corrupt tyrant, fled the island in the early morning hours as Castro's rebel forces entered Havana, and people poured into the streets on New Year's Day to celebrate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当卡斯特罗的叛军进入哈瓦那时，被辱骂为腐败暴君的独裁者富尔亨西奥·巴蒂斯塔在凌晨逃离了该岛，人们在元旦那天涌上街头庆祝

vocabulary.definition: n. 被任命者；[法] 被指定的人, 被任命者
vocabulary.context: Smith was a political appointee, not a professional diplomat, and he didn't always pay close attention to his political officers or to the CIA agents assigned to Cuba at the time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 史密斯是政治任命者，而不是专业外交官，他并不总是密切关注他的政治官员或当时派往古巴的中央情报局特工

vocabulary.definition: adj. 打了就跑的；[法] 肇事后即逃遁的, 打了人后即逃跑的
vocabulary.context: Nearly two years of hit-and-run warfare culminated in victory as 1958 ended," announced one U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 近两年的打了就跑的战争在 1958 年结束时取得了胜利，"一名美国官员宣布

vocabulary.definition: adj. 沙哑的, 粗声的, 喧闹的
vocabulary.context: The uncertainty surrounding Castro stemmed in part from the raucous scenes that accompanied his triumph.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 卡斯特罗周围的不确定性部分源于他胜利时的喧闹场面

vocabulary.definition: n. 新闻影片
vocabulary.context: newsreel, featuring scenes of Castro's men arriving in Havana to a wild reception.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 新闻片，展示卡斯特罗的部下抵达哈瓦那受到热烈欢迎的场景

vocabulary.definition: n. 卡西诺赌场, 卡西诺牌戏
vocabulary.context: On January 1, 1959, his supporters stormed the casinos in Havana hotels, tearing them apart as symbols of the Mafia-controlled gambling world that Batista had allowed to flourish on the island.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1959 年 1 月 1 日，他的支持者冲进了哈瓦那酒店的赌场，将它们撕碎，这些赌场是巴蒂斯塔允许黑手党控制的赌博世界在岛上蓬勃发展的象征

vocabulary.definition: adv. 朝气蓬勃, 精力充沛, 壮健, 茁壮, 健壮, 强有力
vocabulary.context: Chapman vigorously denies such allegations, saying he was suspicious of Castro and dutifully reported that the Cuban had Communist connections.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 查普曼极力否认此类指控，称他对卡斯特罗持怀疑态度，并尽职尽责地报告了古巴人与共产党有联系

vocabulary.definition: n. 取消；消除
vocabulary.context: This year, a summer league comprising U.S.-sponsored teams collapsed, canceling the entire schedule.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 今年，由美国赞助球队组成的夏季联赛崩溃，整个赛程被取消

vocabulary.definition: n. 棒球手( ballplayer的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: teams have added small-scale education programs, perhaps eight hours a week, that emphasize English and financial skills that could prove useful to ballplayers, Farrell says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 球队增加了小规模的教育项目，可能每周八小时，强调英语和财务技能，这对球员来说可能有用，法雷尔说

vocabulary.definition: n. 围攻, 盟员；v.t. 围攻
vocabulary.context: The success of Dominican major leaguers has been a boon for the Caribbean nation's economy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 多米尼加大联盟的成功为这个加勒比国家的经济带来了福音

vocabulary.definition: adj. 已经发展的, 最新型的
vocabulary.context: "Most are more or less state-of-the-art facilities" that now emphasize proper nutrition and provide good living quarters, he says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他说，"大多数或多或少都是最先进的设施"，现在强调适当的营养并提供良好的居住环境

vocabulary.definition: n. 断言, 主张, 申辩；[法] 声明, 事实陈述, 断言
vocabulary.context: Allegations of exploitation, doping and other abuses have dogged the U.S.-run academies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 关于剥削、兴奋剂和其他滥用行为的指控一直困扰着美国管理的学院

vocabulary.definition: n. 开发, 开采, 剥削；[经] 开发, 剥削, 宜传
vocabulary.context: Allegations of exploitation, doping and other abuses have dogged the U.S.-run academies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 关于剥削、兴奋剂和其他滥用行为的指控一直困扰着美国管理的学院

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高姿态的; 高调的; 备受瞩目的; 知名度高的
vocabulary.context: Current Dominican stars, including Albert Pujols and David Ortiz, have launched high-profile charity ventures in their home country through their foundations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 包括阿尔伯特·普霍尔斯和大卫·奥尔蒂斯在内的多米尼加现任明星通过他们的基金会在自己的祖国发起了备受瞩目的慈善事业

vocabulary.definition: adv. 极好地, 非常令人满意地
vocabulary.context: Pedro Martinez, a pitcher best known for his time on the Boston Red Sox and elected last year to baseball's Hall of Fame, famously rebuilt his Dominican neighborhood of Manoguayabo with homes for dozens as well as churches, schools, a window factory and a baseball field.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 佩德罗·马丁内斯 (Pedro Martinez) 是一名投手，因在波士顿红袜队效力而闻名，去年入选棒球名人堂，他重建了多米尼加社区马诺瓜亚博 (Manoguayabo)，该社区拥有数十户住宅，还有教堂、学校、窗户工厂和棒球场

vocabulary.definition: adj. 说明的, 指示的
vocabulary.context: Mathematics, in the sense of demonstrative deductive arguments, begins with Pythagoras: he is credited as the author of the first known mathematical formulation, the theorem which states that the square of the longest side of a right triangle equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从证明演绎论证的意义上来说，数学始于毕达哥拉斯：他被认为是第一个已知数学公式的作者，该定理指出直角三角形最长边的平方等于其他两条边的平方和

vocabulary.definition: adj. 认识论的
vocabulary.context: Plato had many philosophical interests including ethics and politics but he is best known for his metaphysical and epistemological ideas.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 柏拉图有许多哲学兴趣，包括伦理学和政治学，但他最出名的是他的形而上学和认识论思想

vocabulary.definition: n. 转世化身, 再生
vocabulary.context: Pythagoras considered a charlatan for claiming the doctrine of reincarnation, a half-naked Socrates haranguing people in the street with provocative and unanswerable questions, Aristotle tutoring great generals: these are examples of how Greek thinkers dared to question...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 毕达哥拉斯因主张轮回学说而被认为是江湖骗子，半裸的苏格拉底在街上向人们高谈阔论，提出挑衅性和无法回答的问题，亚里士多德辅导伟大的将军：这些都是希腊思想家敢于质疑的例子......

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使组织化, 使系统化, 把...分类；[医] 系统化, 分类
vocabulary.context: The Skeptic philosophical school systematized old doubts: the senses caused troubles to most philosophers except some rare exceptions like Plato who simply denied the cognitive value of perception in favor of his world of ideas.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 怀疑论哲学流派系统化了古老的怀疑：感官给大多数哲学家带来了麻烦，除了一些罕见的例外，比如柏拉图，他简单地否认知觉的认知价值，转而支持他的思想世界

vocabulary.definition: n. 不可知论
vocabulary.context: By combining a humble spirit (he never claimed to be any wiser than anyone else) and a strict agnosticism (he said he knew nothing) with a method that challenged conventional assumptions and intolerance for unclear thinking, Socrates gradually earned enemies from various sectors of Athenian society.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过将谦虚的精神（他从未声称自己比任何人更聪明）和严格的不可知论（他说他一无所知）与挑战传统假设和对不清晰思维的不容忍的方法相结合，苏格拉底逐渐赢得了雅典社会各个阶层的敌人

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使中立, 使成中立地带, 使中和, 抵销；v.i. 中立化, 变无效, 中和
vocabulary.context: After the first and second Punic Wars (264-241 and 218-201 B.C.), Rome neutralized Carthage and controlled Syracuse (the two leading city-states of the western Mediterranean), and continued its expansion by conquering the Macedonian monarchies during the second century B.C.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在第一次和第二次布匿战争（公元前264-241年和218-201年）之后，罗马压制了迦太基并控制了锡拉丘兹（西地中海的两个主要城邦），并在公元前二世纪通过征服马其顿君主国继续扩张

vocabulary.definition: adj. 社会政治的
vocabulary.context: Some years earlier, Athens implemented a socio-political innovation by which all free male citizens had equal rights regardless of their origin and fortune.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 几年前，雅典实施了一项社会政治创新，所有自由男性公民无论其出身和财富如何都享有平等权利

vocabulary.definition: adj. 宇宙哲学的, 宇宙论的
vocabulary.context: Their main concern was to come up with a cosmological theory purely based on natural phenomena.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们主要关心的是提出一种纯粹基于自然现象的宇宙学理论

vocabulary.definition: adj. 推论的, 演绎的；[法] 推定的, 推断的, 演绎的
vocabulary.context: Mathematics, in the sense of demonstrative deductive arguments, begins with Pythagoras: he is credited as the author of the first known mathematical formulation, the theorem which states that the square of the longest side of a right triangle equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从证明演绎论证的意义上来说，数学始于毕达哥拉斯：他被认为是第一个已知数学公式的作者，该定理指出直角三角形最长边的平方等于其他两条边的平方和

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可追踪的, 可描绘的；[经] 可追溯的, 可追踪的
vocabulary.context: Some of the components of Aristotelian logic existed long before Aristotle, such as Socrates' ideas on exact definition, argumentative techniques found in Zeno of Elea, Parmenides and Plato, and many other elements traceable to legal reasoning and mathematical proof.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 亚里士多德逻辑的一些组成部分早在亚里士多德之前就已经存在，例如苏格拉底关于精确定义的思想，埃利亚、巴门尼德和柏拉图的芝诺中发现的论证技巧，以及许多其他可追溯到法律推理和数学证明的元素

vocabulary.definition: n. 根深蒂固, 牢不可破
vocabulary.context: Psychologists even possess an entire vocabulary for the affectionate bonds between people and places: There's "topophilia," "rootedness" and "attachment to place," which are all used to describe the feelings of comfort and security that bind us to a place.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 心理学家甚至拥有一整套描述人与地方之间情感纽带的词汇："恋地癖"、"扎根感"和"对地方的依恋"，这些都用来描述将我们与某个地方联系在一起的舒适和安全感

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 含蓄着, 内涵着, 意味着
vocabulary.context: Environmental psychologists have long understood that the word "home" clearly connotes more than just a house.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 环境心理学家很早就明白，"家"这个词显然不仅仅意味着一座房子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 年终的；[法] 年终, 年底; 年终的, 年底的
vocabulary.context: It's where they raise their kids and communicate with spirits, and there's an annual ritual - called the Shalako - in which homes are blessed and consecrated as part of the year-end winter solstice celebration.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那里是他们抚养孩子、与神灵交流的地方，还有一年一度的仪式 - 称为 Shalako - 作为年终冬至庆祝活动的一部分，家庭受到祝福和圣化

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使平均, 使平衡, 使弥补；n. 平衡量, 平衡力, 势均力敌
vocabulary.context: No matter where they come from, people tend to think about home as a central place that represents order, a counterbalance to the chaos that exists elsewhere.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 无论来自哪里，人们都倾向于将家视为代表秩序的中心场所，是对其他地方存在的混乱的平衡

vocabulary.definition: adj. 匆匆忙忙的, 草率的, 粗略的
vocabulary.context: Strip away the cursory Christmas rituals - the TV specials, the lights, the gifts, the music - and what remains is home.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 去掉粗略的圣诞仪式--电视特别节目、灯光、礼物、音乐--剩下的就是家

vocabulary.definition: adj. 渴望的, 想望的
vocabulary.context: While Christmas playlists often include cheesy favorites like "Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree" and "I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus," there are also a handful of wistful tracks that go a little bit deeper.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然圣诞节播放列表通常包括"Rockin' around the Christmas Tree"和"I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus"等俗气的最爱，但也有一些更深层次的渴望歌曲

vocabulary.definition: adj. 干酪质的, 俊俏的, 优秀的；[医] 干酪样的
vocabulary.context: While Christmas playlists often include cheesy favorites like "Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree" and "I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus," there are also a handful of wistful tracks that go a little bit deeper.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然圣诞节播放列表通常包括"Rockin' around the Christmas Tree"和"I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus"等俗气的最爱，但也有一些更深层次的渴望歌曲

vocabulary.definition: adv. 适当地, 适宜地
vocabulary.context: It is a place where, as the poet Robert Frost aptly wrote, "When you have to go there, they have to take you in."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如诗人罗伯特·弗罗斯特（Robert Frost）恰当地写道，这是一个"当你必须去那里时，他们必须带你进去"的地方

vocabulary.definition: adj. 主要的, 最初的, 原始的
vocabulary.context: It is the beating heart of the holiday, and its importance reflects our primal need to have a meaningful relationship with a setting - a place that transcends the boundary between the self and the physical world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它是假期的核心，它的重要性反映了我们与环境建立有意义的关系的原始需求 - 一个超越自我和物质世界之间界限的地方

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未翻转的
vocabulary.context: an unturned layer of earth I
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 未翻土层 I

vocabulary.definition: n. 油地毡, 漆布；[化] 油毡; 亚麻油毡
vocabulary.context: across her red linoleum floor
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿过她的红色油毡地板

vocabulary.definition: adj. 流利的, 流畅的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我不会说流利的马绍尔语

vocabulary.definition: adj. 花似的, 花的, 植物的
vocabulary.context: the white floral print that must be dancing
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一定会跳舞的白色印花

vocabulary.definition: n. 柔和, 温柔
vocabulary.context: the softness of her palm
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她手掌的柔软

vocabulary.definition: n. 网, 网状物, 罗网, 净利, 净价；adj. 净的, 最终的；v.t. 用网捕, 撒网, 净赚, 得到；v.i. 编网；[计] 网络, 网络分析程序
vocabulary.context: that her stories could be netted
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的故事可以被网罗

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 要(做), 想(做), 想要(某物)
vocabulary.context: and I wanna tell my grandmother that I wish
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我想告诉我的祖母我希望

vocabulary.definition: n. 工装裤, 牛仔裤
vocabulary.context: sweating in jeans, my hands folded tight
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿着牛仔裤出汗，双手紧握

vocabulary.definition: n. 地板, 楼层, 底部, 底价；v.t. 铺地板, 打倒；n. 地面, 地板, 基底；[计] 基底
vocabulary.context: across her red linoleum floor
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿过她的红色油毡地板

vocabulary.definition: n. (给...)扣“赤色分子”帽子进行政治迫害
vocabulary.context: The red-baiting ended up serving as some of the most effective attacks against King and his Southern Christian Leadership Conference.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 红色诱饵最终成为针对金和他的南方基督教领袖会议的最有效的攻击之一

vocabulary.definition: n. 改写；重写
vocabulary.context: In this speech, I recognized what others had missed: King had subtly ended his speech by rewriting Langston Hughes' "I Dream a World."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这次演讲中，我认识到了其他人错过的东西：金巧妙地重写了兰斯顿休斯的《我梦想一个世界》来结束他的演讲

vocabulary.definition: adj. 从事颠覆的, 破坏性的；n. 破坏分子, 颠覆分子
vocabulary.context: In the 1930s, Hughes earned a subversive reputation by writing several radical poems.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 20世纪30年代，休斯因创作了几首激进的诗歌而赢得了颠覆性的声誉

vocabulary.definition: adj. 兄弟的, 亲兄弟似的, 亲切的
vocabulary.context: King and the bus boycott titled "Brotherly Love." At the time, Hughes was much more famous than King, who was honored to have become a subject for the poet.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 金和名为"兄弟之爱"的巴士抵制活动。当时，休斯比金更有名，金很荣幸能成为诗人的主题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 题目的, 主题的, 主旋律的
vocabulary.context: 11, 1956, King delivered a speech titled "The Birth of a New Age." Many King scholars consider this address - which talked about King's vision for a new world - the thematic precursor to his "I Have a Dream" speech.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1956年11月，金发表题为"新时代的诞生"的演讲。许多金学者认为这次演讲--谈到了金对新世界的愿景--是他的"我有一个梦想"演讲的主题先驱

vocabulary.definition: n. 先驱者, 前导, 前兆；[化] 前体; 前身; 产物母体
vocabulary.context: 11, 1956, King delivered a speech titled "The Birth of a New Age." Many King scholars consider this address - which talked about King's vision for a new world - the thematic precursor to his "I Have a Dream" speech.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1956年11月，金发表题为"新时代的诞生"的演讲。许多金学者认为这次演讲--谈到了金对新世界的愿景--是他的"我有一个梦想"演讲的主题先驱

vocabulary.definition: n. 同情者, 怜悯者, 同意者, 支持者；[医] 同感眼, 交感眼
vocabulary.context: However, Hughes was also suspected of being a communist sympathizer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，休斯也被怀疑是共产主义同情者

vocabulary.definition: adv. 敏锐地, 巧妙地, 精细地
vocabulary.context: In this speech, I recognized what others had missed: King had subtly ended his speech by rewriting Langston Hughes' "I Dream a World."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这次演讲中，我认识到了其他人错过的东西：金巧妙地重写了兰斯顿休斯的《我梦想一个世界》来结束他的演讲

vocabulary.definition: n. 说教, 启示, 布道
vocabulary.context: But my research has found traces of Hughes' poetry in King's speeches and sermons.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但我的研究在金的演讲和布道中发现了休斯诗歌的痕迹

vocabulary.definition: n. 企业家, 实业家；[经] 实业家, 工业主义者
vocabulary.context: Southern industrialists resisted regulations, insisting that they were uplifting poor whites.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 南方实业家抵制法规，坚称这些法规是在提高贫困白人的生活水平

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使衰弱
vocabulary.context: The image of pale, shrunken-faced, debilitated poor white boys and girls in Southern textile mills was sensationalized in the North as "white child slavery." Once the issue became a national obsession, activists formed the National Child Labor Committee in 1904 to "change the public conscience" on this issue.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 南方纺织厂里苍白、脸色萎缩、虚弱的贫穷白人男孩和女孩的形象在北方被耸人听闻地称为"白人儿童奴隶制"。当这个问题成为全国性的焦点时，活动人士于 1904 年成立了全国童工委员会，以"改变公众对此问题的良心"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使引起轰动, 耸人听闻地报道, 耸人听闻地描绘；[法] 使引起轰动, 耸人听闻的报导
vocabulary.context: The image of pale, shrunken-faced, debilitated poor white boys and girls in Southern textile mills was sensationalized in the North as "white child slavery." Once the issue became a national obsession, activists formed the National Child Labor Committee in 1904 to "change the public conscience" on this issue.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 南方纺织厂里苍白、脸色萎缩、虚弱的贫穷白人男孩和女孩的形象在北方被耸人听闻地称为"白人儿童奴隶制"。当这个问题成为全国性的焦点时，活动人士于 1904 年成立了全国童工委员会，以"改变公众对此问题的良心"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有利益的, 唯利是图的
vocabulary.context: The government first gathered data on child labor, which was defined at the time as the gainful employment of children under the age of 15, in 1870.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1870年，政府首次收集了童工数据，当时童工的定义是15岁以下儿童的有酬就业

vocabulary.definition: n. 有罪；作恶多端
vocabulary.context: In 1913, the minister Owen Lovejoy brought new religious allies to the committee, which by then focused on the sinfulness of child labor in America.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1913年，牧师欧文·洛夫乔伊为委员会带来了新的宗教盟友，当时委员会的重点是美国童工的罪恶

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未付款的, 不支薪水的；[经] 未付的, 未缴纳的
vocabulary.context: Not only were these enslaved people unpaid "child laborers"; the law cast them as property subject to the threat of sale.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些被奴役的人不仅是无薪的"童工"，而且是无薪的"童工"。法律将它们视为财产，受到出售威胁

vocabulary.definition: n. 枪眼, 小窗, 换气孔
vocabulary.context: Despite Southern opposition, reformers argued that state-level regulations were rife with loopholes and difficult to enforce.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管南方反对，改革者认为州级法规漏洞百出且难以执行

vocabulary.definition: adj. 基本的, 起码的, 初步的, 发展不完全的, 残留的, 退化的；[医] 原基的, 未成熟的; 残遗的, 已退化的
vocabulary.context: After the Civil War officially ended child slavery, most Americans still did not think there was anything wrong with children earning their keep, as long as working kids could get at least a rudimentary education.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 南北战争正式结束儿童奴隶制后，大多数美国人仍然不认为孩子们自食其力有什么问题，只要工作的孩子至少能接受基础教育

vocabulary.definition: adj. 惊人的
vocabulary.context: The ancient Greeks have provided us with fine art, breath-taking temples, timeless theatre, and some of the greatest philosophers - but it is democracy that is, perhaps, their greatest and most enduring legacy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 古希腊人为我们提供了精美的艺术、令人叹为观止的寺庙、永恒的戏剧和一些最伟大的哲学家--但民主也许才是他们最伟大、最持久的遗产

vocabulary.definition: n. 外交使节, 特使；[法] 使者, 代表, 使节
vocabulary.context: Specific issues discussed in the assembly included deciding military and financial offices, organizing and maintaining food supplies, initiating legislation and political trials, deciding to send envoys, deciding whether or not to sign treaties, voting to raise or spend funds, and debating military matters.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 会议讨论的具体问题包括决定军事和财政机构、组织和维持粮食供应、发起立法和政治审判、决定派遣使节、决定是否签署条约、投票筹集或支出资金以及辩论军事事项

vocabulary.definition: adj. 阴谋的, 阴谋者的；[法] 阴谋的, 共谋的
vocabulary.context: Of this group, perhaps as few as 100 citizens - the wealthiest, most influential, and the best speakers - dominated the political arena both in front of the assembly and behind the scenes in private conspiratorial political meetings and groups.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这个群体中，也许只有 100 名公民--最富有、最有影响力和最优秀的演讲者--在集会前和私人阴谋政治会议和团体的幕后主导着政治舞台

vocabulary.definition: n. 归化, 移入, 移植；[法] 入籍, 归化
vocabulary.context: It was here in the courts that laws made by the assembly could be challenged and decisions were made regarding exile, naturalization, and debt.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在法庭上，议会制定的法律可以受到质疑，有关流亡、入籍和债务的决定也可以在法庭上做出

vocabulary.definition: n. 煽动者
vocabulary.context: Critics of democracy, such as Thucydides and Aristophanes, pointed out that not only were proceedings dominated by an elite, but that the people could be too often swayed by a good orator or popular leaders (the demagogues), get carried away with their emotions, or lack the necessary knowledge to make informed decisions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 修昔底德和阿里斯托芬等民主批评者指出，不仅诉讼程序由精英主导，而且人民也可能经常被优秀演说家或受欢迎的领导人（煽动者）所左右，情绪失控，或者缺乏做出明智决定所需的知识

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不适当地, 过度地；[法] 过分的, 过度的, 不正当的
vocabulary.context: With people chosen at random to hold important positions and with terms of office strictly limited, it was difficult for any individual or small group to dominate or unduly influence the decision-making process either directly themselves or, because one never knew exactly who would be selected, indirectly by bribing those in power at any one time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于担任重要职务的人是随机选出的，任期也受到严格限制，任何个人或小团体都很难直接主导或不当影响决策过程，或者因为人们永远不知道谁会被选中而间接地在任何时候贿赂当权者

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 参与, 分担, 分享；v.i. 分享, 参与, 带有
vocabulary.context: ...participate actively in the running of the polis is clear in this quote from Thucydides: "We alone consider a citizen who does not partake in politics not only one who minds his own business but useless." Direct involvement in the politics of the polis also meant that the Athenians developed a unique collective identity and probably too, a...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...积极参与城邦的管理在修昔底德的这句话中说得很清楚："我们认为，一个不参与政治的公民不仅只管自己的事，而且毫无用处。"直接参与城邦政治也意味着雅典人形成了独特的集体身份，而且可能......

vocabulary.definition: n. 表征, 记号, 代币；adj. 象征的, 表意的；[计] 记号
vocabulary.context: Indeed, there was a specially designed machine of colored tokens used to ensure those selected were chosen randomly, a process judges had to go through twice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，有一个专门设计的彩色令牌机器，用于确保所选择的令牌是随机选择的，评委必须经历两次这个过程

vocabulary.definition: n. 方阵, 密集队列, 密集的人群(或兽群), 指骨, 趾骨；[医] 指[趾]骨
vocabulary.context: The long-range assault tactics of the Persian archers was to come up against the heavy infantry of Greek hoplites with their large round shields, spears and swords, and organized in a solid line or phalanx where each man's shield protected both himself and his neighbor in a wall of bronze.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 波斯弓箭手的远程攻击战术是对抗手持大圆盾、长矛和剑的希腊重装步兵，他们排列成实线或方阵，每个人的盾牌都保护自己和邻人的青铜墙

vocabulary.definition: n. (古希腊)重甲步兵
vocabulary.context: The Greeks fielded the largest hoplite army ever seen which came from some 30 city-states and numbered around 110,000.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 希腊人派出了有史以来规模最大的重装步兵军队，他们来自约 30 个城邦，人数约为 110,000 人

vocabulary.definition: n. 总督
vocabulary.context: The Ionian rebellion, the offering of earth and water in submission to the Persian satrap in 508 B.C., and the attack by Athens and Eretria on the city of Sardis in 499 B.C.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 爱奥尼亚叛乱，公元前 508 年向波斯总督献上土和水，以及公元前 499 年雅典和埃雷特里亚对萨迪斯城的攻击

vocabulary.definition: adj. 世界主义的, 世界性的, 全世界的, 遍生的, 不偏狭的, 卓有见识的；n. 世界主义者, 世界性
vocabulary.context: Darius did not lead the invasion of mainland Greece in person but put his general Datis in charge of his cosmopolitan army.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大流士并没有亲自领导入侵希腊大陆，而是让他的将军达蒂斯负责他的世界性军队

vocabulary.definition: n. 致命打击
vocabulary.context: While the Greeks were euphoric in victory, the Persian Empire was not dealt a deathblow by its defeat.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管希腊人因胜利而欣喜若狂，但波斯帝国并没有因失败而遭受致命打击

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不胡闹的, 严肃的, 讲究实际的
vocabulary.context: The Greeks sent a no-nonsense reply by executing the envoys, and Athens and Sparta promised to form an alliance for the defense of Greece.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 希腊人通过处决使节做出了严肃的答复，雅典和斯巴达承诺结成联盟来保卫希腊

vocabulary.definition: adj. 欣快症的, 欣快的；[医] 精神愉快的, 欣快的
vocabulary.context: While the Greeks were euphoric in victory, the Persian Empire was not dealt a deathblow by its defeat.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管希腊人因胜利而欣喜若狂，但波斯帝国并没有因失败而遭受致命打击

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再拿（retake的过去分词形式）
vocabulary.context: Further, the Chersonnese controlling the Black Sea and Byzantium controlling the Bosphorus were both retaken.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，控制黑海的切尔松尼斯和控制博斯普鲁斯海峡的拜占庭都被夺回

vocabulary.definition: adj. 神秘的, 含义模糊的, 隐藏的；[医] 隐蔽的
vocabulary.context: The cryptic oracle of Apollo at Delphi had been proved right: "only a wooden wall will keep you safe" and the wooden triremes of the Greeks had done their job.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 德尔斐阿波罗的神秘神谕已被证明是正确的："只有木墙才能保证你的安全"，而希腊人的木制三层桨帆船已经完成了它们的任务

vocabulary.definition: adv. 令人惊讶地
vocabulary.context: For Greece, however, the victory not only guaranteed her freedom from foreign rule but also permitted, soon after, an astonishingly rich period of artistic and cultural endeavor, which would lay the cultural foundations of all future Western civilizations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，对于希腊来说，这场胜利不仅保证了她免受外国统治的自由，而且不久之后也让希腊进入了一段极其丰富的艺术和文化努力时期，这将为所有未来的西方文明奠定文化基础

vocabulary.definition: adv. 仪式上, 礼仪上
vocabulary.context: Because Egyptian tradition held that a woman needed a male consort to rule, her 12-year-old brother, Ptolemy XIII, was ceremonially married to her.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于埃及传统认为女性需要男性配偶来统治，她12岁的弟弟托勒密十三世在仪式上与她结婚

vocabulary.definition: n. 侄孙, 甥外孙, 甥孙, 侄外孙
vocabulary.context: Caesar's right-hand man, Mark Antony, joined with Octavian (Caesar's grandnephew and adopted heir) and friend Lepidus to pursue and defeat the conspirators who had murdered Caesar.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 凯撒的得力助手马克·安东尼与屋大维（凯撒的侄孙、收养的继承人）和朋友莱必达一起追捕并击败了谋杀凯撒的阴谋者

vocabulary.definition: n. 高手, 专家；adj. 精通的, 熟练的
vocabulary.context: Cleopatra, however, was fluent in Egyptian, eloquent in her native Greek, and proficient in other languages as well.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，克利奥帕特拉能说流利的埃及语，能说流利的母语希腊语，并且还精通其他语言

vocabulary.definition: n. 重婚, 重婚罪；[法] 重婚, 重婚罪
vocabulary.context: As Caesar was already married to Calpurnia at this time, and the Roman laws against bigamy were strictly adhered to, many of the members of the senate, as well as the public, were upset by Caesar's actions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于凯撒此时已经与卡尔普尔尼亚结婚，并且罗马法律严格遵守禁止重婚的法律，许多元老院成员以及公众都对凯撒的行为感到不满

vocabulary.definition: n. 横笛, 吹横笛；v. 吹横笛
vocabulary.context: "She came sailing up the river Cydnus in a barge with gilded stern and outspread sails of purple, while oars of silver beat time to the music of flutes and fifes and harps.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "她乘坐一艘驳船沿锡德努斯河逆流而上，船尾镀金，紫色帆张开，银色的桨随着长笛、横笛和竖琴的音乐敲打着节拍

vocabulary.definition: n. 异父姐妹, 异母姐妹；[法] 同父异母或同母异父姐妹
vocabulary.context: Cleopatra and her half-sister, Arsinoe, fled to Thebaid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 克利奥帕特拉和她同父异母的妹妹阿尔西诺逃到了塞拜德

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无比的, 不能比较的
vocabulary.context: "Her own beauty, so we are told, was not of that incomparable type that immediately captivates the beholder.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "据我们所知，她自己的美丽并不是那种无与伦比的美丽，可以立即吸引旁观者

vocabulary.definition: n. 观看者
vocabulary.context: "Her own beauty, so we are told, was not of that incomparable type that immediately captivates the beholder.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "据我们所知，她自己的美丽并不是那种无与伦比的美丽，可以立即吸引旁观者

vocabulary.definition: adv. 可公开, 显然, 表面, 外表, 诡称, 假装
vocabulary.context: Recognizing in Caesar her chance to regain power, she had herself rolled in a rug, ostensibly a gift for the Roman general, and carried through the enemy lines to the palace and presented to Caesar.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 意识到凯撒有机会重新夺回权力，她把自己裹在地毯里，表面上是送给罗马将军的礼物，然后穿过敌军防线，来到宫殿，献给凯撒

vocabulary.definition: adj. 部分的, 部门的, 节的, 截面的；n. 组合式家具
vocabulary.context: Another seed of sectional conflict had been deeply planted in America's psyche.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一颗分裂冲突的种子已深深植根于美国的心灵中

vocabulary.definition: n. 动产, 奴隶；[经] 动产
vocabulary.context: Life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness simply did not seem consistent with the practice of chattel slavery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 生命、自由和对幸福的追求似乎与动产奴隶制的做法根本不一致

vocabulary.definition: n. 反感, 厌恶, 不一致, 矛盾, 抵触；[法] 不一致, 矛盾, 不一致之处
vocabulary.context: Before any meaningful reform could happen, people needed to recognize that the economic benefit was vastly overshadowed by the overwhelming repugnance, immorality, and inhumanity of slavery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在任何有意义的改革发生之前，人们需要认识到经济利益被奴隶制的压倒性反感、不道德和不人道所掩盖

vocabulary.definition: adj. 激动的, 煽动的, 炎症性的；[医] 炎的, 炎性的
vocabulary.context: They sent petitions to Congress and the states, campaigned for office, and flooded the South with inflammatory literature.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们向国会和各州发出请愿书，竞选公职，并向南方传播煽动性文学作品

vocabulary.definition: n. 不道德, 道德败坏, 不道德的行为, 伤风败俗的行为
vocabulary.context: Before any meaningful reform could happen, people needed to recognize that the economic benefit was vastly overshadowed by the overwhelming repugnance, immorality, and inhumanity of slavery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在任何有意义的改革发生之前，人们需要认识到经济利益被奴隶制的压倒性反感、不道德和不人道所掩盖

vocabulary.definition: n. 祖先, 祖宗
vocabulary.context: These new Abolitionists were different from their forebears.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些新废奴主义者与他们的祖先不同

vocabulary.definition: n. 进口, 输入品；[医] 输入, 传入
vocabulary.context: Revolutionary sentiments led to the banning of the importation of slaves in 1807.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 革命情绪导致 1807 年禁止进口奴隶

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 围以壕沟, 防护, 保卫, 使处于牢固地位；v.i. 掘壕, 侵犯
vocabulary.context: As the cotton industry grew and slavery became more and more entrenched across the American South, opposition to it also grew.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着棉花工业的发展和奴隶制在美国南部变得越来越根深蒂固，反对它的声音也随之增加

vocabulary.definition: n. 感情, 感伤, 情操, 情绪, 感想, 意见；[医] 情感, 情操
vocabulary.context: Revolutionary sentiments led to the banning of the importation of slaves in 1807.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 革命情绪导致 1807 年禁止进口奴隶

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不符合传统的, 非传统的
vocabulary.context: Some of his reasoning is because they identify less with parents, do not want to be clones of the older child or children who went before, and give themselves more latitude to grow in nontraditional ways.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的一些推理是因为他们不太认同父母，不想成为大孩子或以前的孩子的克隆人，并给自己更多的自由度以非传统的方式成长

vocabulary.definition: n. 重新定义
vocabulary.context: Rebellion at this age is primarily a process through which the young person rejects the old child identity that he or she now wants to shed to clear the way for more grown up redefinition ahead.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个年龄段的叛逆主要是一个过程，通过这个过程，年轻人拒绝他或她现在想要摆脱的旧孩子身份，为未来更成熟的重新定义扫清道路

vocabulary.definition: n. 兼容性；[计] 兼容性
vocabulary.context: At this older age, risk taking can be more dangerous, while they miss the loss of closeness and compatibility with their son or daughter that they have enjoyed for so many years.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这个年纪，冒险可能会更加危险，同时他们也怀念失去多年来与儿子或女儿的亲密和兼容性

vocabulary.definition: n. （对命令、规章等的）不服从
vocabulary.context: Two common types of rebellion are against fitting in socially (rebellion of non-conformity) and against adult authority (rebellion of non-compliance).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 两种常见的反叛类型是反对融入社会（不合规的反叛）和反对成人权威（不合规的反叛）

vocabulary.definition: n. （对自身天性及基本素质的）自我界定
vocabulary.context: Rebellion causes the young person to suspend self-definition and do the opposite of what other people want.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 叛逆会导致年轻人暂停自我定义并做与他人想要的相反的事情

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自毁的
vocabulary.context: It can cause them to engage in self-defeating and self-destructive behavior - refusing to do school work or even physically hurting themselves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这可能会导致他们做出自我挫败和自我毁灭的行为--拒绝做功课，甚至伤害自己

vocabulary.definition: n. 恶化, 加重, 恼怒；[法] 加重, 严重
vocabulary.context: So adolescent rebellion is not simply a matter of parental aggravation; it is also a matter of concern.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，青少年叛逆不仅仅是父母惹恼的问题；这也是一个令人担忧的问题

vocabulary.definition: v. 废黜, 废位赶出( dethrone的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: Having dethroned parental authority for leading her life and supplanted it with her own authority, she finds herself rebelling against it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在废除了父母领导她生活的权威并用自己的权威取代了它之后，她发现自己在反抗它

vocabulary.definition: adj. 同量的, 同等的, 相称的
vocabulary.context: So their job is to allow more independence while expecting commensurate responsibility, staying empathetic during disagreements, and providing calm and clear guidance about any significant risk taking that may be going on.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，他们的工作是允许更多的独立性，同时期待相应的责任，在分歧期间保持同理心，并对可能发生的任何重大风险提供冷静和明确的指导

vocabulary.definition: adj. 教学的, 教育的
vocabulary.context: So far, we have observed that the youth, for the most part, like to come to instructional sessions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到目前为止，我们观察到，大多数青少年喜欢参加辅导班

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自动调节的
vocabulary.context: Although all of this sounds great, there is little research into the influence learning to play chess may have on self-regulatory functions and on the life choices youth make.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管所有这些听起来都很棒，但很少有人研究学习下棋可能对青少年的自我调节功能和生活选择产生的影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 喧闹；v. 大声地要求
vocabulary.context: The room fills with noisy young voices, sharing how their week has gone and clamouring for cookies and juice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 房间里充满了年轻人吵闹的声音，他们分享着他们这一周的经历，并吵着要饼干和果汁

vocabulary.definition: adj. 相互作用的, 相互影响的；[计] 交互, 人机对话的
vocabulary.context: An interactive white board shows a game in progress.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 交互式白板显示正在进行的游戏

vocabulary.definition: n. 退学学生, 中途退学；[电] 离去
vocabulary.context: In fact, punitive sentencing can result in poor social outcomes, low rates of employment and higher school dropout rates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，惩罚性判决可能会导致不良的社会后果、低就业率和更高的辍学率

vocabulary.definition: n. 鉴定, 查验, 证明, 试用, 察看, 缓刑；[医] 审辨, 试验, 鉴定, 试用, 见习
vocabulary.context: The scene is like any youth gathering, with one difference: Group home workers and probation officers are in attendance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个场景就像任何青年聚会一样，有一点不同：团体家庭工作人员和缓刑官员都在场

vocabulary.definition: n. 初步做法, 初步措施, 预试, 预选赛；adj. 初步的, 开始的, 预备的
vocabulary.context: Although the study is in preliminary stages of collecting data, initial observations suggest the Chess for Life program is a good alternate sentence choice for helping these young people get their lives back on track.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管该研究处于收集数据的初步阶段，但初步观察表明，"生命棋"计划是帮助这些年轻人让生活重回正轨的一个很好的替代句子选择

vocabulary.definition: n. 主动行动, 首创精神, 主动权；adj. 自发的, 起始的
vocabulary.context: Alternate sentencing initiatives focus on fair sentencing practices that are appropriate and support the reintegration of youth back into the community.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 替代量刑举措侧重于适当的公平量刑做法，并支持青少年重新融入社区

vocabulary.definition: adv. 病理学地; 由疾病引起地; 莫名其妙地; 反常地
vocabulary.context: As long as this is not used pathologically, but to create beneficial change for others, it is a strength.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只要这不是病态地使用，而是为他人创造有益的改变，它就是一种力量

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可实行的
vocabulary.context: "Well, if you were paralyzed from the neck down, don't you think that aggressiveness would be an asset?" He told me that I was infeasible to ever work.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "好吧，如果你从脖子以下瘫痪，你不认为攻击性会是一种资产吗？"他告诉我，我不可能再工作了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 下身麻痹的；n. 下身麻痹患者
vocabulary.context: I knew a guy who was paraplegic - he was a second story man.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我认识一个截瘫的人--他是二楼的人

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 剥, 削, 消减
vocabulary.context: The head of HEW was trying to get 504 pared down.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: HEW 的负责人试图减少 504 的数量

vocabulary.definition: n. 通风设备, 送风机, 换气扇；[医] 通风机
vocabulary.context: At that time, portable ventilators had not been invented.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当时，便携式呼吸机还没有发明

vocabulary.definition: n. 鞋带, 小额资本
vocabulary.context: We had a shoestring budget and a hole in the wall office, but it was enough.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们的预算有限，而且办公室墙上有一个洞，但这已经足够了

vocabulary.definition: n. 不规则, 反常事物, 畸形；[医] 异常, 反常
vocabulary.context: Her father was a doctor, and her whole upbringing she had been treated by him and his colleagues as a medical anomaly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的父亲是一名医生，在她的整个成长过程中，她一直被他和他的同事视为医学异常

vocabulary.definition: n. 散步者, 流浪者
vocabulary.context: For instance, people pushing strollers use curb cuts, as do people on bikes and elderly people who can't lift their legs so high.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，推婴儿车的人使用路缘切口，骑自行车的人和无法将腿抬得太高的老年人也是如此

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 小便, 下大雨, 抱怨；v.t. 撒尿弄脏, 尿在...上；n. 小便
vocabulary.context: My mother was so pissed off.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我妈妈很生气

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 以大写字母写, 使资本化, 估计...的价值；[经] 资本化, 收益的现值化
vocabulary.context: I heard women talk about how they had manipulated men by capitalizing on these stereotypes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我听到女性谈论她们如何利用这些刻板印象来操纵男性

vocabulary.definition: adv. 卓越地, 杰出地, 超群地；卓越地, 杰出地
vocabulary.context: This is preeminently the time to speak the truth, the whole truth, frankly and boldly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在正是坦率、大胆地说出真相、全部真相的最佳时机

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不经济的, 浪费的
vocabulary.context: It can be helped by the unifying of relief activities which today are often scattered, uneconomical, and unequal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 统一救灾活动可以起到帮助作用，而这些活动如今往往是分散的、不经济的和不平等的

vocabulary.definition: n. 缩减, 缩短；[经] 削减, 紧缩
vocabulary.context: Values have shrunken to fantastic levels; taxes have risen; our ability to pay has fallen; government of all kinds is faced by serious curtailment of income; the means of exchange are frozen in the currents of trade; the withered leaves of industrial enterprise lie on every side; farmers find no markets for their produce; the savings of many years...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 价值观已经缩水到了不可思议的水平；税收增加了；我们的支付能力下降了；各类政府都面临收入严重缩减的问题；交换手段在贸易流通中被冻结；工业企业的枯叶遍地都是；农民的产品找不到市场；多年的积蓄...

vocabulary.definition: adj. 逐渐消失的, 容易消散的, 短暂的；[医] 易消灭的, 渐消失的, 挥发性的
vocabulary.context: The joy and moral stimulation of work no longer must be forgotten in the mad chase of evanescent profits.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在对转瞬即逝的利润的疯狂追逐中，工作的快乐和道德刺激不再被遗忘

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不能解决的
vocabulary.context: This is no unsolvable problem if we face it wisely and courageously.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果我们明智而勇敢地面对它，这并不是无法解决的问题

vocabulary.definition: n. 转换开关装置；改变者（change的名词形式）
vocabulary.context: Practices of the unscrupulous money changers stand indicted in the court of public opinion, rejected by the hearts and minds of men.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不择手段的货币兑换商的行为受到舆论法庭的起诉，受到人心的谴责

vocabulary.definition: n. 外出, 支出, 出口；v.t. 胜过, 优于
vocabulary.context: Through this program of action we address ourselves to putting our own national house in order and making income balance outgo.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过这一行动纲领，我们致力于让我们自己的国家秩序井然有序，实现收入平衡

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不踌躇地, 坚定地, 迅速地; 爽爽快快
vocabulary.context: With this pledge taken, I assume unhesitatingly the leadership of this great army of our people dedicated to a disciplined attack upon our common problems.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 做出这一承诺后，我将毫不犹豫地领导我们这支伟大的人民军队，致力于有纪律地解决我们的共同问题

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使倾覆, (重量、价值)超过, 使失去均衡, 跌倒；v.i. 失去平衡；n. 超重, 超量, 失去平衡
vocabulary.context: Hand in hand with this we must frankly recognize the overbalance of population in our industrial centers and, by engaging on a national scale in a redistribution, endeavor to provide a better use of the land for those best fitted for the land.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与此同时，我们必须坦率地认识到我们工业中心的人口失衡，并通过在全国范围内进行重新分配，努力为最适合土地的人提供更好的土地利用

vocabulary.definition: adj. 能实行的, 可行的, 适宜的；[法] 可行的, 可执行的, 可完成的
vocabulary.context: Action in this image and to this end is feasible under the form of government which we have inherited from our ancestors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我们从祖先那里继承下来的政府形式下，按照这种形象和目的采取行动是可行的

vocabulary.definition: n. 肌肉, 精力, 体力, 力量的源泉, 腱；v.t. 赋予力量, 加强
vocabulary.context: Could twist the sinews of thy heart?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 能扭曲你的心筋吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 铁砧；[医] 砧骨; 铁砧
vocabulary.context: What the anvil?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 什么铁砧？

vocabulary.definition: n. 火炉, 熔炉, 考验；v.t. 在炉中烧
vocabulary.context: In what furnace was thy brain?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你的大脑在哪个熔炉里？

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 渴望, 立志于
vocabulary.context: On what wings dare he aspire?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他敢于向往什么翅膀？

vocabulary.definition: n. 锤, 铁锤, 钉锤；v.t. 锤打, 敲打, 钉；v.i. 连续锤打；[计] 锤头
vocabulary.context: What the hammer?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 什么锤子？

vocabulary.definition: n. 一扭, 扭曲, 曲折, 歪曲, 螺旋状, 新手法；v.t. 拧, 扭, 捻, 编织, 使扭转, 缠绕, 盘绕, 歪曲, 使转动, 使苦恼, 使混乱, 使旋转；v.i. 转向, 弯曲, 缠绕, 扭动, 呈螺旋形
vocabulary.context: Could twist the sinews of thy heart?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 能扭曲你的心筋吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 翅膀, 翼, 机翼, 派别；v.t. 给...装上翼, 飞过, 使飞, 空运, 增加...速度；v.i. 飞行
vocabulary.context: On what wings dare he aspire?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他敢于向往什么翅膀？

vocabulary.definition: n. 链, 枷锁, 束缚；v.t. 用铁练锁住, 束缚, 囚禁
vocabulary.context: what the chain?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 什么是外链？

vocabulary.definition: n. 教养院；adj. 改革的, 革新的
vocabulary.context: The idea of treating juveniles differently in the justice system has a long history. In the early 1800s, special facilities to deal with juveniles were created in cities like New York and Chicago. These "reformatories" housed kids deemed "juvenile delinquents."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 司法系统中区别对待青少年的想法由来已久。 1800年代初，纽约和芝加哥等城市建立了专门处理青少年的设施。这些"管教所"关押着被视为"少年犯"的孩子

vocabulary.definition: n. 易冲动
vocabulary.context: The high court's rulings also took into account new scientific research, including findings that the areas of the brain that deal with decision making, impulsiveness, and consequences are often not fully developed until people are in their 20s.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 高等法院的裁决还考虑到了新的科学研究，包括研究结果表明，大脑中处理决策、冲动和后果的区域通常要到20多岁才完全发育

vocabulary.definition: n. 可卡因, 古柯碱；[化] 可卡因; 古柯碱
vocabulary.context: But in the 1980s, juvenile crime rates - especially for violent crimes - began to increase drastically, in part because of a nationwide crack cocaine epidemic.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但在 20 世纪 80 年代，青少年犯罪率--尤其是暴力犯罪--开始急剧上升，部分原因是全国范围内的强效可卡因流行

vocabulary.definition: n. 归还, 偿还, 赔偿；[医] 整复, 恢复, 转回(胎头)
vocabulary.context: These courts were staffed by judges and prosecutors who were trained to use methods such as probation, restitution to victims, and occasionally incarceration to rehabilitate young people in trouble.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些法院配备了法官和检察官，他们接受过培训，可以使用缓刑、受害者赔偿以及偶尔监禁等方法来帮助陷入困境的年轻人康复

vocabulary.definition: adj. 两党连立的
vocabulary.context: Last year, Senator Tom Cotton, Republican of Arkansas, blocked bipartisan legislation that would have prohibited states receiving federal money from jailing juveniles in adult prisons.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 去年，阿肯色州共和党参议员汤姆·科顿（Tom Cotton）阻止了两党立法，该立法禁止各州通过在成人监狱关押青少年而获得联邦资金

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 恢复原状, 修复, 使康复；[法] 恢复, 使恢复心理健康, 修复
vocabulary.context: These courts were staffed by judges and prosecutors who were trained to use methods such as probation, restitution to victims, and occasionally incarceration to rehabilitate young people in trouble.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些法院配备了法官和检察官，他们接受过培训，可以使用缓刑、受害者赔偿以及偶尔监禁等方法来帮助陷入困境的年轻人康复

vocabulary.definition: n. 行为不良的人, 违法者；adj. 怠忽的, 有过失的
vocabulary.context: The idea of treating juveniles differently in the justice system has a long history. In the early 1800s, special facilities to deal with juveniles were created in cities like New York and Chicago. These "reformatories" housed kids deemed "juvenile delinquents."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 司法系统中区别对待青少年的想法由来已久。 1800年代初，纽约和芝加哥等城市建立了专门处理青少年的设施。这些"管教所"关押着被视为"少年犯"的孩子

vocabulary.definition: n. 限制, 被禁闭, 分娩；[医] 分娩, 生产
vocabulary.context: He was transferred between multiple adult jails, where he endured extensive time in solitary confinement - for his own protection, he was told.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他被转移到多个成人监狱，在那里他忍受了很长一段时间的单独监禁--他被告知是为了保护自己

vocabulary.definition: adj. 冷静的, 不带感情的
vocabulary.context: A dispassionate review of existing research suggests that what adolescents lack is not so much the ability to control their behavior, but the wisdom that adults gain through experience.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对现有研究的冷静回顾表明，青少年缺乏的并不是控制自己行为的能力，而是成年人通过经验获得的智慧

vocabulary.definition: adv. 用口语, 用通俗语
vocabulary.context: This learning, colloquially known as wisdom, continues to grow well into adulthood.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种学习，俗称智慧，会在成年后继续成长

vocabulary.definition: n. 化脓, 成熟；[医] 成熟, 化脓
vocabulary.context: This ability to exert cognitive control peaks well before structural brain maturation, which peaks at about age 25.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种发挥认知控制的能力早在大脑结构成熟之前就达到了顶峰，大约在 25 岁时达到顶峰

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 合并；异文合并
vocabulary.context: In my view, the most striking failure of the teen brain hypothesis is its conflating of important differences between different kinds of risky behavior, only a fraction of which support the notion of the impulsive, unbridled adolescent.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我看来，青少年大脑假说最显着的失败在于它混淆了不同类型的危险行为之间的重要差异，其中只有一小部分支持冲动、肆无忌惮的青少年的概念

vocabulary.definition: n. 不平衡, 不均衡；[医] 不平衡, 失调
vocabulary.context: We used to attribute this behavior to "raging hormones." More recently, it's been popular in some scientific circles to explain adolescent behavior as the result of an imbalance in the development of the brain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们曾经将这种行为归因于"狂暴的荷尔蒙"。最近，一些科学界流行将青少年行为解释为大脑发育不平衡的结果

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 放松缰绳, 放纵
vocabulary.context: In my view, the most striking failure of the teen brain hypothesis is its conflating of important differences between different kinds of risky behavior, only a fraction of which support the notion of the impulsive, unbridled adolescent.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我看来，青少年大脑假说最显着的失败在于它混淆了不同类型的危险行为之间的重要差异，其中只有一小部分支持冲动、肆无忌惮的青少年的概念

vocabulary.definition: n. 普遍, 流行, 广泛；[医] 流行, 盛行
vocabulary.context: Although the rise in injurious and other risky behavior among teens is cause for concern, this represents much more of a rise in the incidence of this behavior than of its prevalence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管青少年中伤害性和其他危险行为的增加令人担忧，但这更多地代表了这种行为发生率的上升，而不是其流行率的上升

vocabulary.definition: n. 反语, 讽刺, 具有讽刺意味的事
vocabulary.context: The irony is that most late adolescents and young adults are more able to control their behavior than many older adults, resulting in what some have called the wisdom paradox.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 具有讽刺意味的是，大多数晚期青少年和年轻人比许多老年人更能控制自己的行为，从而导致了一些人所说的智慧悖论

vocabulary.definition: adj. 变形的, 畸形的
vocabulary.context: It resulted in an unknown number of deaths and malformed humans and animals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它导致了未知数量的死亡和畸形的人类和动物

vocabulary.definition: n. 后果；余波；[数] 后效
vocabulary.context: However, the extent of the aftereffects such as cancer, birth defects and a number of illnesses due to the exposure to radioactive material is still disputed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，由于暴露于放射性物质而导致的癌症、出生缺陷和许多疾病等后遗症的程度仍然存在争议

vocabulary.definition: n. 福尔马林；[化] 福尔马林
vocabulary.context: BL: I still have dreams about it, the smell of formalin and the sight of the animals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: BL：我仍然会梦见它，福尔马林的气味和动物的景象

vocabulary.definition: n. 铯；[化] 铯Cs
vocabulary.context: Some animal meat still has too high cesium content.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些动物肉的铯含量仍然过高

vocabulary.definition: n. 净化, 排除污染；[化] 净化; 纯化; 清除放射性污染
vocabulary.context: A lot of the ones I've met are dead now, especially of those who helped with the decontamination.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我遇到的很多人现在都死了，尤其是那些帮助净化的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 放射性；[化] 放射性
vocabulary.context: And the radiation gauge which sometimes showed several hundred times more radioactivity than normal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 辐射计有时显示的放射性比正常情况高出数百倍

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不宜居住的, 不舒适的
vocabulary.context: The city of Pripyat, which is the closest large city to the power plant, is still an unlivable region.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 距离发电厂最近的大城市普里皮亚特市仍然是一个不适宜居住的地区

vocabulary.definition: n. 白血病, 白血球过多症；[化] 白血病
vocabulary.context: Cancer in children has increased a lot according to the World Health Organization, and diseases such as leukemia, heart diseases, and so on continue to grow in extent.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据世界卫生组织统计，儿童癌症发病率大幅上升，白血病、心脏病等疾病发病率持续增长

vocabulary.definition: n. 惯例性, 老一套的行为, 习俗, 因袭性
vocabulary.context: I am not talking to you now through the medium of custom, conventionalities, nor even of mortal flesh; - it is my spirit that addresses your spirit; just as if both had passed through the grave, and we stood at God's feet, equal, - as we are!"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我现在不是通过习俗、惯例，甚至也不是通过凡人的肉体来与你们交谈； - 是我的灵魂在与你的灵魂对话；就好像我们俩都穿过了坟墓，我们平等地站在上帝的脚下--就像我们一样！"

vocabulary.definition: n. 自动机, 机器人；[计] 自动机
vocabulary.context: Do you think I am an automaton?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你认为我是一个机器人吗？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 过于激动
vocabulary.context: "Jane, be still a few moments: you are over-excited: I will be still too."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "简，安静一会儿：你太兴奋了：我也会安静的。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 狂女, 疯女
vocabulary.context: Rochester was married all along - to a madwoman named Bertha who has been shut away in the house.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 罗切斯特一直有婚姻--嫁给了一个名叫伯莎的疯女人，她被关在房子里

vocabulary.definition: adj. 没有精神的, 没有灵魂的, 泄气的
vocabulary.context: Do you think, because I am poor, obscure, plain, and little, I am soulless and heartless?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你以为我穷、不起眼、相貌平庸、个子小，就没有灵魂、没有心吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 敏锐, 聪明
vocabulary.context: Rochester had sometimes read my unspoken thoughts with an acumen to me incomprehensible: in the present instance he took no notice of my abrupt vocal response; but he smiled at me with a certain smile he had of his own, and which he used but on rare occasions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 罗切斯特有时会以我难以理解的敏锐读懂我未说出口的想法：在目前的情况下，他没有注意到我突然的声音反应；他没有注意到我的声音。但他对我微笑，带着他特有的微笑，但他很少使用这种微笑

vocabulary.definition: n. 春药；v.t. 用春药迷惑, 刺激, 使兴奋
vocabulary.context: Tell me now, fairy as you are - can't you give me a charm, or a philter, or something of that sort, to make me a handsome man?"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在告诉我，仙女，你能不能给我一个符咒，或者一剂春药，或者诸如此类的东西，让我成为一个英俊的男人？"

vocabulary.definition: n. 凝视者
vocabulary.context: Most true is it that "beauty is in the eye of the gazer." My master's colourless, olive face, square, massive brow, broad and jetty eyebrows, deep eyes, strong features, firm, grim mouth, - all energy, decision, will, - were not beautiful, according to rule;...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最真实的是"凝视者眼中出西施"。我主人无色的橄榄色脸，方方的粗眉毛，宽而黑的眉毛，深邃的眼睛，坚强的五官，坚定而冷酷的嘴，--所有的能量，决心，意志，--根据规则，并不美丽；......

vocabulary.definition: n. 怀疑者, 怀疑论者, 不可知论者
vocabulary.context: "Little skeptic, you shall be convinced.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "小怀疑论者，你会被说服的

vocabulary.definition: n. 严格, 严厉
vocabulary.context: "It would be past the power of magic, sir;" and, in thought, I added, "A loving eye is all the charm needed: to such you are handsome enough; or rather your sternness has a power beyond beauty."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这将超越魔法的力量，先生；"我心里想，又补充道："魅力就在于一双充满爱意的眼睛：对于这样的人来说，你足够英俊；或者更确切地说，你的严厉具有超越美丽的力量。"

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 粗心和过度自信通常比故意接受的风险更危险

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此后我的观察让我更加坚定地相信人类飞行是可能且可行的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的想法只是为了解决平衡问题而进行实验和实践

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们将[滑翔机]飞行时间和长度增加到 43 秒，比去年的记录增加了一又三分之二，大约是其他记录的三倍

vocabulary.context: Will probably have made another trial before you receive this unless weather is unfavorable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除非天气不利，否则在您收到此信息之前可能会再进行一次尝试

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他曾经存了一百块钱，开始分期买房，但发生了他不知道的事情，房东把他赶了出去，并扣留了这一百块钱

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我可以预见婚礼的一切--周六晚上我们会去她家，那里会有音乐，有很多人，我们会度过一段愉快的社交时光

vocabulary.definition: prep. 直到, 在...以前, 迄；conj. 直到...为止；v.t. 耕种；n. 放钱的抽屉, 备用现金, 冰碛

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 任何一个民族都无法繁荣昌盛，除非它知道耕种田地和写诗一样有尊严

vocabulary.definition: n. 注意, 留心；v. 注意, 留心
vocabulary.context: The captain of the distressed vessel, at last heeding the injunction, cast down his bucket, and it came up full of fresh, sparkling water.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这艘遇险船只的船长最终听从了命令，扔下了水桶，水桶浮上来，里面装满了新鲜的、闪闪发光的水

vocabulary.definition: n. 忠实, 诚实, 忠诚, 保真度；[电] 逼真度

vocabulary.definition: n. 公证人；[经] 公证人, 公证员

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 柏林市禁止犹太律师和公证人处理法律事务，慕尼黑市长禁止犹太医生治疗非犹太患者，巴伐利亚内政部拒绝犹太学生进入医学院

vocabulary.definition: n. 反犹太主义
vocabulary.context: Antisemitism and the persecution of Jews represented a central tenet of Nazi ideology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 反犹太主义和对犹太人的迫害代表了纳粹意识形态的核心原则

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 缩减, 剥夺, 简略；[法] 剥夺特权
vocabulary.context: In the same month, further legislation sharply curtailed "Jewish activity" in the medical and legal professions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 同月，进一步的立法大幅限制了医疗和法律行业的"犹太人活动"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 从属的, 辅助的, 副的；[经] 辅助的, 附属的, 助手
vocabulary.context: These "Nuremberg Laws" excluded German Jews from Reich citizenship and prohibited them from marrying or having sexual relations with persons of "German or German-related blood." Ancillary ordinances to these laws deprived them of most political rights.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些"纽伦堡法律"将德国犹太人排除在帝国公民身份之外，并禁止他们与"德国或德国相关血统"的人结婚或发生性关系。这些法律的附属条例剥夺了他们的大部分政治权利

vocabulary.definition: n. 征用, 收用, 征收；[经] 征用, 没收, 征购
vocabulary.context: Jews were required to register their domestic and foreign property and assets, a prelude to the gradual expropriation of their material wealth by the state.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 犹太人被要求登记其国内外财产和资产，这是国家逐步征用其物质财富的前奏

vocabulary.definition: n. 系统陈述, 公式化, 配制；[化] 表述; 数学表述; 数式化; 数学表述
vocabulary.context: The new Civil Service Law was the German authorities' first formulation of the so-called Aryan Paragraph, a kind of regulation used to exclude Jews (and often by extension other "non-Aryans") from organizations, professions, and other aspects of public life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 新的《公务员法》是德国当局首次制定所谓的"雅利安人条款"，这是一种用于将犹太人（通常还延伸到其他"非雅利安人"）排除在组织、职业和公共生活其他方面的法规

vocabulary.definition: n. 接管, 接收；[经] 接收
vocabulary.context: "Aryanization" meant the dismissal of Jewish workers and managers of a company and/or the takeover of Jewish-owned businesses by non-Jewish Germans who bought them at bargain prices fixed by government or Nazi party officials.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "雅利安化"意味着解雇犹太工人和公司经理和/或非犹太德国人以政府或纳粹党官员确定的便宜价格收购犹太人拥有的企业

vocabulary.definition: n. 子程序；[计] 子程序; 子例行程序

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的一个项目帮助计算机翻译自己的代码并使用预编程的子程序

vocabulary.definition: n. 裱糊, 涂；粘合
vocabulary.context: ...be "'all Navy', but when you reach inside, you find a 'Pirate' dying to be released." Whether destroying alarm clocks as a child or pasting a burnt moth into a computer log, Hopper had a genuine ability to engage people and technology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...是"'全海军'，但当你进入里面时，你会发现一个'海盗'渴望被释放。"无论是小时候毁坏闹钟，还是将烧焦的飞蛾粘贴到计算机日志中，霍珀都拥有与人和技术互动的真正能力

vocabulary.definition: n. 如猫的性格, 恶毒

vocabulary.definition: n. 倾向, 癖性

vocabulary.contextTranslation: "人类女性有一种特殊的倾向，即使用间接攻击，这种攻击通常针对其他女性，尤其是有吸引力且性欲旺盛的女性，"加拿大渥太华大学的特雷西·瓦兰库特（Tracy Vaillancourt）写道

vocabulary.definition: n. 规则性, 匀整, 正规, 经常；[医] 规律性
vocabulary.context: But only after the sexual revolution of the 1970s did the phrase "competition among females" even begin to crop up with any regularity in books, anthropologist Sarah Hrdy points out in the introduction to an October 28 special issue of Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但直到 20 世纪 70 年代的性革命之后，"女性之间的竞争"这个词才开始在书籍中出现，人类学家莎拉·赫迪 (Sarah Hrdy) 在 10 月 28 日英国皇家学会 B 哲学汇刊特刊的介绍中指出

Some of them also have proven hard to grow in the lab. Now Jansson doesn't have to. She uses a set of techniques...

vocabulary.definition: n. 装载量, 列车载重
vocabulary.context: As the Holocaust proceeded, ghettos were emptied by the trainload.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着大屠杀的进行，隔都被满载的火车清空

vocabulary.definition: n. 语源, 语源学
vocabulary.context: In his etymology column for the Oxford University Press, Anatoly Liberman took a look at each of these possibilities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在牛津大学出版社的词源学专栏中，阿纳托利·利伯曼（Anatoly Liberman）对这些可能性进行了研究

vocabulary.definition: adj. 临时配备的
vocabulary.context: "Ghetto" as an adjective, roughly synonymous with "jury-rigged," for anything cobbled together out of subpar materials.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "贫民窟"是一个形容词，大致与"临时搭建的"同义，指用劣质材料拼凑而成的任何东西

vocabulary.definition: n. 棘轮, 棘爪；[机] 棘轮, 棘齿轮
vocabulary.context: "Being ghetto," or behaving in a low-class manner (see also: "ratchet").
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "贫民窟"，或以低级的方式行事（另见："棘轮"）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 低级的, 品质低劣的
vocabulary.context: "Being ghetto," or behaving in a low-class manner (see also: "ratchet").
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "贫民窟"，或以低级的方式行事（另见："棘轮"）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 在一般标准以下的,在平均水平以下的;在票面价值以下的
vocabulary.context: "Ghetto" as an adjective, roughly synonymous with "jury-rigged," for anything cobbled together out of subpar materials.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "贫民窟"是一个形容词，大致与"临时搭建的"同义，指用劣质材料拼凑而成的任何东西

vocabulary.definition: n. 阶级歧视者；adj. 实行阶级歧视的；有阶级偏见的
vocabulary.context: Using ghetto as an insult is, as our own Karen Grigsby Bates has pointed out, inherently classist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如我们自己的凯伦·格里格斯比·贝茨（Karen Grigsby Bates）所指出的那样，使用贫民区作为一种侮辱本质上是阶级主义
